title: Stavacintāmaṇi
basis: Sanderson, Śaivism and the Tantric Traditions
note: Śaiva devotional hymn-collection commented on by Kṣemarāja (fl. early 11th c.), so the work predates him; grouped with Utpaladeva's Stotrāvalī, suggesting 9th-10th c. Distinguish work from Kṣemarāja's commentary. Range consistent with cur_med.

title: Bījanighaṇṭu
basis: no specialist dating found; only bibliographic listing (Tantrābhidhāna corpus)
source_file: cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism. Studies in Honor of André Padoux_Teun Goudriaan, ed, 1992.txt
note: Tantric seed-syllable (bīja) lexicon of the Tantrābhidhāna group; only listed bibliographically, no scholarly date. Wide uncertainty retained. Confidence none.

title: Cakrapāṇinātha: Bhāvopahārastotra
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (refining his 2007a)
note: Kashmirian Krama (Kāpālika-observance) author; later than Kṣemarāja (c. 1050), MS of his Bhāvopahārapūjā dated 1158/9, so before mid-12th c.

title: Cakrapāṇinātha: Bhāvopahārastotra
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (refining his 2007a)
note: Duplicate of SA_GR13_cakbh_pu (same Bhāvopahārastotra of Cakrapāṇinātha); later than Kṣemarāja, MS dated 1158/9.

title: Devīkālottara-Āgama
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (re Kālottara recensions); cf. Goodall on the Kālottara/Kālajñāna recensions
note: Devīkālottara is one of the short recensions of the Saiddhāntika Kālottara. Sanderson: Kālottara current by the 9th c., early Kālottara plausibly earlier; Goodall edits these recensions. 7th-9th c. range; midpoint ~750 close to machine median 789.

title: Āmaraughaśāsana
basis: Mallinson (in Jacobsen 2012, Yoga Powers); cf. Birch 2023
note: Early hathayoga text predating the Hathapradīpikā (~15th c.); Birch 2023 (cited in src) edits it as an early-stratum work, c. 12th c. Distinct from later Amaraughaprabodha (c. 14th c.). Confirms current median.

title: Gorakṣaśataka
basis: Mallinson, White_Yoga in Practice (and Khecarīvidyā 2003)
note: CHANGED. Mallinson distinguishes the TRUE Gorakṣaśataka (core vv.7-64, c.1400 CE) from the Vivekamārtaṇḍa later misnamed Gorakṣaśataka (earliest MS 1477). Dated by borrowings into the 15th-c. Haṭhapradīpikā; absent from the 1363 Śārṅgadharapaddhati.

title: Gorakṣaśataka
basis: Mallinson 2007 (Khecarīvidyā); White 2011 (Yoga in Practice)
note: Mallinson shows the work now called Gorakṣaśataka is originally the Vivekamārtanda (attributed to Gorakṣa), with terminus ad quem the Śārngadharapaddhati (1363). White separately notes a distinct Gorakṣaśataka core well known c.1400. Early-haṭha work; consistent with current median.

title: Jñānaśambhu: Śivapūjāstava
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (Jñānaśambhu, Golakī-maṭha guru in Somaśambhu's lineage, patronized by Kalacuri/Cedi kings)
note: Base Śivāgama hymn (not its anonymous 13th-c. Śivapūjāstavavyākhyā). Author is the central-Indian Golakī-maṭha guru Jñānaśambhu, successor of Somaśambhu (fl. late 11th c.), under Kalacuri patronage; places him in the 12th c.

title: Ardhanārīśvarastotra
basis: Floruit of Kalhaṇa, author of Rājataraṅgiṇī 1148-1149 (Davidson; Kulke & Rothermund)
source_file: cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism_ A Social History of the Tantric Movement.txt
note: Short Śaiva hymn whose only dating handle is the attribution to the Kashmirian chronicler Kalhaṇa (fl. c.1148-1150). No specialist dating of the stotra itself in library; attribution itself uncertain (also circulates in a Purāṇic/Brahmā frame).

title: Karatoyāmāhātmya
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: A regional mahatmya glorifying the Karatoya river (N. Bengal), NOT a tantra. Library mentions the river (Encyclopedia of Tantra; Sanderson) but gives no date for this mahatmya text. Late, undatable from this library; no date asserted.

title: Kiraṇatantra
consensus_date: early Śaiva Siddhānta scripture, c. 6th-8th c. CE
basis: Watson 2006, The Self's Awareness of Itself; Sanderson 2009, The Saiva Age
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Counted among the surviving EARLY Saiddhāntika scriptures (with Niśvāsa, Raurava, Svāyambhuva, Parākhya); pre-8th c., commented by Rāmakaṇṭha (10th c.). Confirms existing 500-799 bounds; consensus c. 6th-8th c. Little change from median.

title: Kiraṇatantra
basis: Watson 2006 (citing Goodall 1998, ed. Kiranatantra/Kiranavrtti); Sanderson 2009, The Saiva Age
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Counted among the earliest surviving Saiva Siddhanta scriptures; predates its commentator Sadyojyotis (7th-8th c.), so 6th-7th c. Consistent with current median ~669 but better grounded. Distinguish the tantra from Ramakantha's 10th-c. Kiranavrtti commentary.

title: Kubjikāmatatantra
basis: Flood 2006, The Tantric Body; Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (relative chronology)
source_file: cleaned_Tantric Body. The Secret Tradition of Hindu Religion_Gavin Flood, 2006.txt
note: Root scripture of the Western Transmission (Kaula). Sanderson places it after the Tantrasadbhāva (which is after the Svacchanda); Flood calls it 'eleventh-century' (elsewhere 'perhaps before the tenth century'). Range 10th–11th c. brackets the disagreement; current median ~946 lies within it.

title: Mātṛkābhedatantra
basis: no specialist dating found in library
source_file: cleaned_Kiss of the Yogini. Tantric Sex in its South Asian Contexts_David Gordon White, 2003.txt
note: Late Bengali Sakta/Kaula tantra. White (Kiss of the Yogini) only cites it for content (kundagolaka, menstrual-blood typology) and lists the edition; no date given. Generally regarded as a late (post-15th c.) Bengali tantra in scholarship, but no firm date in this library; none asserted.

title: Mālinīvijayottaratantra
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (relative chronology of the Trika); cf. Vasudeva ed.
source_file: cleaned_Tantric Body. The Secret Tradition of Hindu Religion_Gavin Flood, 2006.txt
note: Core Trika scripture; predates Abhinavagupta (c. 975-1025) who based the Tantrāloka on it. No single explicit century stated in-library, but Sanderson's relative chronology and Vasudeva's edition support 8th-9th c. Current bounds retained.

title: Mṛgendrāgama [Mṛgendratantra]: 1. Vidyāpāda
basis: Goodall 1998/2004 (Parākhya/Kiraṇavṛtti edition, skt-en-mono); Sanderson, The Śaiva Age
note: Śaiva Siddhānta scripture, grouped with the early Saiddhāntika canon (Sanderson) but among its latest members (Goodall): post-dates Sadyojyotis (c. 7th c.) and Somaśambhu. 8th-9th c. brackets the current median 756.

title: Mṛgendrāgama [Mṛgendratantra]: 3. Caryāpāda
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (Mṛgendra among the early Saiddhāntika core corpus); Brunner-Lachaux 1985 ed.
note: Caryāpāda of the Saiddhāntika scripture Mṛgendra; a developed (post-Kiraṇa) member of the early Siddhānta corpus, dated by specialists to ~8th c. Within cur bounds 700-999; midpoint near cur_med -> not changed (>100y).

title: Mṛgendrāgama [Mṛgendratantra]: 4. Yogapāda
basis: Bühnemann 2003 (citing Hulin/Goodall); cf. Watson 2006
source_file: cleaned_Mandalas and Yantras in the Hindu Traditions_Gudrun Buhnemann 2003.txt
note: One of the few early surviving Śaiva Siddhānta scriptures, datable before the 9th c.; commentary (Mṛgendravṛtti) by Nārāyaṇakaṇṭha is 10th c. (Watson). Dated the WORK to 8th c. Median essentially unchanged.

title: Paraśurāma-Kalpasūtra
basis: only vague relative dating (N.N. Bhattacharyya 2006)
source_file: cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: Bhattacharyya only says it is 'earlier' than the Mahānirvāṇatantra (18th c.); no firm date and the sentence is corrupted in the OCR. Śrīvidyā ritual sūtra; retain current median (conventionally 15th-16th c.).

title: Pāśupatasūtra
basis: Jacobsen 2012, Yoga Powers; Potter 1977, Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies Vol. 2
note: Duplicate of the Pāśupatasūtra (other recension/file). Kauṇḍinya commentary 5th c. CE; sūtra slightly earlier. Consistent with current median.

title: Pāśupatasūtra
basis: Jacobsen 2012, Yoga Powers; Potter 1977, Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies Vol. 2
note: Sūtra attributed to Lakulīśa; Kauṇḍinya's Pañcārthabhāṣya commentary fixed at 5th c. CE, so the sūtra is somewhat earlier (~4th-5th c.). Distinguish sūtra from commentary. Consistent with current median.

title: Ekākṣarakośa
basis: Lal Mani Joshi, Studies in the Buddhistic Culture of India; Mylius, Geschichte der altindischen Literatur 2003
source_file: cleaned_Studies in the Buddhistic Culture of India_Lal Mani Josh.txt
note: NOT a tantra: Ekaksarakosa is a Sanskrit lexicon of monosyllabic words by the Bengali grammarian-lexicographer Purusottamadeva. Date is the author's, conventionally 12th c. (Mylius notes an earlier date is arguable from Buddhist vocab). Consistent with current median.

title: Saundaryalaharī
basis: Bharati 1965, Tantric Tradition (citing W. N. Brown, HOS 1958) — authorship only, no firm date in library
note: Library unanimously rejects Śaṅkara authorship but gives no precise century; cannot ground a sourced date, so consensus_date=null

title: Śivamahimnastava
basis: no specialist dating located
note: The only 'Puṣpadanta' in the library is the Jain Apabhraṃśa poet (Winternitz), a different person from the legendary author of this Śaiva hymn. No scholarly dating of the Śivamahimnastava found; not asserting a date. (Traditionally a popular Śaiva stotra, often placed loosely in the medieval period.)

title: Śivasūtra
basis: N.N. Bhattacharyya 2006, History of the Tantric Religion (placing Vasugupta in the 9th c.); standard specialist consensus (Sanderson) ca. 875-925
source_file: cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: Foundational text of Kashmir Śaivism, revealed to/composed by Vasugupta; commentaries (Kṣemarāja etc.) are 11th c.

title: Śiva-Upaniṣad
basis: White, Yoga in Practice (2012); library has only catalogue-level entries for the Śaiva/Yoga Upaniṣads
note: No specialist dating of the Śiva-Upaniṣad itself in the library; it belongs to the late sectarian Śaiva/Yoga Upaniṣad corpus (N. Indian bracket 800-1300). Current median 629 too early; bracket adopted by analogy, hence low confidence.

title: Śāktavijñāna
basis: Watson 2006, The Self's Awareness of Itself (Somānanda c. 900-950); cf. Sanderson, Kashmirian Śākta Śaivas from Utpaladeva
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Founder of Pratyabhijñā / Kashmir Śaiva nondualism; c. 900-950 matches current median.

title: Ratnatrayaparīkṣā
basis: Watson 2006 (following Goodall/Sanderson), Self's Awareness of Itself
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Śaiva Siddhānta work; Śrīkaṇṭha heads the 10th/early-11th c. Kashmiri exegetical lineage (predecessor of Nārāyaṇakaṇṭha/Rāmakaṇṭha, whose Kiraṇavṛtti lifts its maṅgala verse). No change.

title: Sārdhatriśatikālottarāgama
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (2009); Goodall 2007 ed. of Śatika-Kālottara; Watson 2006
note: One of the short non-eclectic Kālottara recensions (350 vv.); among the early Śaiva Siddhānta scriptures, pre-Sadyojyotis (7th-8th c.), so before c. 750. Watson lists Kālottara among the surviving early scriptures.

title: Svacchandatantra [Svacchandabhairavatantra]
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism; Sanderson, The Śaiva Age
note: Mantrapīṭha Bhairava tantra (cult of Svacchanda-bhairava); attested in 9th/early-10th c. Nepalese palm-leaf mss and redacted after early Saiddhāntika Tantras, giving terminus ante quem in 9th c. Midpoint 800, within ~70y of current median.

title: Toḍalatantra
consensus_date: 11th c. CE or later (post-10th-c. east-Indian Śākta Mahāvidyā corpus)
basis: Kinsley 1998, Tantric Visions of the Divine Feminine; Sanderson 2009, The Saiva Age
source_file: cleaned_Tantric Visions of the Divine Feminine. The Ten Mahavidyas_DAVID KINSLEY, 1998.txt
note: Sanderson: 'The Todalatantra teaches the rites of these three [Mahavidyas] alone' within the late east-Indian Sakta corpus; Mahavidya group post-10th c., so lower bound raised above current 750.

title: Uḍḍāmareśvaratantra
basis: no specialist dating in library; only a bare title-list entry
note: Late-medieval magical/digest Tantra; library contains only a title-list occurrence and an unrelated lexical use of uddamara. No dated scholarship found; left null.

title: Śivastotrāvalī
basis: Sanderson, dating cited in skt-en-mono (Pratyabhijna corpus)
note: Devotional hymns by Utpaladeva (fl. c. 925-975); current median 925 essentially correct. Stotra, not a tantra.

title: Vīṇāśikhatantra
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age; Goudriaan (ed.) cited in Niśvāsa/Davidson; Samuel 2008
note: Sole surviving Vāmasrotas (Tumburu) Bhairavatantra of the Vidyāpīṭha; cited at Sdok Kak Thom by mid-11th c., so composition no later than ~9th-10th c. Goudriaan/Sanderson place it among the early Bhairavatantras. Kept ~9th c.; only modest shift from machine median 697.

title: Aṣṭāvakragītā
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: Late Advaita Vedānta dialogue, not a Tantric text. Library only has the unrelated Mahābhārata Aṣṭāvakra (Winternitz), no dating of the Gītā/Saṃhitā. No sourced consensus; left null. Scholarly estimates range widely (c. 8th-15th c.); current median 1014 lies within that band.

title: Bhṛgu-Saṃhitā
basis: Revire 2016, Enthroned Buddha in Majesty
source_file: cleaned_Enthroned Buddha in Majesty. An Iconological Study_Nicolas Revire, 2016.txt
note: Src points to the ASTROLOGICAL Bhṛgu-Saṃhitā (jyotiṣa nāḍī text), explicitly 'of uncertain date' per Revire; not a Tantric/Pāñcarātra scripture despite GR14 collection. No reliable date; 6th-c. machine median unsupported.

title: Brahma-Saṃhitā: Adhyāya 5
basis: no specialist dating in library; only bare list/commentary references
note: Gaudiya Vaisnava text, the famous ch.5 'recovered' in Caitanya milieu (early 16th c.); library has only Jiva Gosvamin commentary references, no dated scholarship. Not a Saiva/Sakta tantra. No reliable date found.

title: Caitanyacandrāmṛta
basis: Authorship by Prabodhānanda Sarasvatī confirmed (Potter, Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies); no precise dating quote in library
source_file: cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 1 - Potter.txt
note: Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava devotional poem, NOT a tantra. Prabodhānanda Sarasvatī (Vṛndāvana, Caitanya circle), 16th c. Library only confirms authorship, no firm date; cur_med plausible.

title: Gauragovindārcanasmaraṇapaddhati
basis: no in-library scholarship
note: Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava arcana/smaraṇa manual by Dhyānacandra Gosvāmī, an associate of the Six Goswamis of Vṛndāvana (16th-17th c.) — not a tantra and far later than median 870. No library source dates it; consensus_date left null to avoid fabrication.

title: Rādhākṛṣṇāṣṭakālīyalīlāsmaraṇakramapaddhati
basis: No source in library
note: Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava smaraṇa-paddhati; Dhyānacandra was a disciple in the Gopāla Guru line, a generation+ after Caitanya (d. 1533). No library scholarship found; date is conventional only. Retained current median bounds.

title: Harināmacintāmaṇi
basis: authorial attribution (Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, 1838-1914)
note: Modern Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava devotional work (composed c.1900), NOT a tantric scripture. Misclassified/mis-dated; date from well-established author biography, not from library.

title: Haribhaktivilāsa 1–11
basis: Authorship (Gopāla Bhaṭṭa Gosvāmin, with Digdarśinī ṭīkā of Sanātana Gosvāmin) confirmed in Mallinson & Singleton 2017
source_file: cleaned_Roots of Yoga_James Mallinson, Mark Singleton, 2017.txt
note: Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava ritual/smṛti digest, NOT a tantra. Mid-16th c. Vṛndāvana circle. No precise specialist date in library; cur_med consistent with authorship.

title: Rādhākṛṣṇārcanadīpikā
basis: Freschi & Maas (eds.) 2017, Adaptive Reuse (Okita chapter)
source_file: cleaned_Freschi and Maas_Adaptive Reuse_Aspects of Creativity in South Asian Cultural History.txt
note: Gaudiya Vaisnava devotional manual (not tantric). Author Jiva Gosvami dated 1517-1608; matches machine median.

title: Jñānāmṛtasāra-Saṃhitā [Nārada-Pañcarātra]
basis: Mylius, Geschichte der altindischen Literatur (2003/1983)
note: Mylius: demonstrably composed at the beginning of the 16th c.; Kṛṣṇa-Rādhā focus. Slightly later than current median, within tolerance.

title: Bhāvanāsārasaṃgraha
basis: No specialist dating found in reference-literature/
note: Not a tantra: a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava rāgānugā-bhakti sādhana manual by a post-Caitanya Siddha at Govardhana. Library discusses the rāgānugā/Vaiṣṇava-Sahajiyā milieu (White, Yoga in Practice; Urban 2001) but gives no date for this work or author. Retaining current 19th-c. bounds; consistent with a late post-Caitanya composition.

title: Kramadīpikā
basis: no specialist dating of Keśava's Tantric Kramadīpikā in library
source_file: cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 4 - Potter 1987.txt
note: Same work as item SA_MB_kramadipikaHK. Library's only Kramadīpikā dating is a different (Sāṃkhya) text; no source for Keśava's. Retain current median (Keśava fl. c.1500).

title: Mukundamālā
basis: no specialist dating in library; only Buddhism-in-Kerala references to a king Kulaśekhara
note: Vaiṣṇava stotra (devotional, non-tantric), wrongly in this Śaiva batch. No specialist date found; kept broad Āḻvār-era range around current median.

title: Laghubhāgavatāmṛta
basis: Companion to World Philosophies (Deutsch & Bontekoe) 1999
source_file: cleaned_Companion to World Philosophies_Eliot Deutsch, Ron Bontekoe, 1999.txt
note: Gaudiya Vaisnava (not tantric). Rupa Gosvami explicitly a 16th-c. theologian and disciple of Caitanya; matches machine median.

title: Kṛṣṇāmṛtamahārṇava
basis: standard Madhva chronology (traditional dates 1238-1317)
note: NOT a tantra: a Vaisnava (Dvaita) devotional/dharmic compilation by Madhvacarya. No specialist dating of the work in this library, but it is securely an authentic work of Madhva, conventionally dated 1238-1317; current median 1257 falls within his lifetime and is retained.

title: Muktācarita
basis: Tantra in Practice (D.G. White, ed.) 2000; cf. Companion to World Philosophies 1999 (16th-c. Gaudiya theologians)
source_file: cleaned_Tantra in Practice_David Gordon White, ed, 2000_a.txt
note: Gaudiya Vaisnava (not tantric). Raghunathadasa is one of the six Gosvamins of Vrndavana, direct disciples of Caitanya (16th-c. Bengali Krsna mystic); 16th-c. dating confirmed.

title: Prakāśa-Saṃhitā [Pāñcarātra-Saṃhitā]
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: Pāñcarātra saṃhitā; library (Rastelli/Czerniak corpus absent) has no dated discussion of a Prakāśasaṃhitā. No date assigned to avoid inventing one.

title: Pauṣkara-Saṃhitā
consensus_date: 9th-10th c. CE (latest of the early Pāñcarātra saṃhitās)
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Pāñcarātra Pauṣkara-saṃhitā. Sanderson: among the seven relatively old saṃhitās, the Sātvata and Pauṣkara are the latest; the Sātvata group is 'very unlikely to have been produced before the ninth century', so Pauṣkara ~9th-10th c. Far later than machine median 573.

title: Haribhaktirasāmṛtasindhu
basis: Authorship by Rūpa Gosvāmī (Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava Vṛndāvana circle) confirmed; no precise dating quote in library
source_file: cleaned_Freschi and Maas_Adaptive Reuse_Aspects of Creativity in South Asian Cultural History.txt
note: Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava bhakti-rasa treatise, NOT a tantra. Rūpa Gosvāmī fl. c. 1530s-1550s (Caitanya circle). Library has no specialist date; cur_med agrees with established authorship period.

title: Sādhanādīpikā
basis: no dating found in library; only an edition bibliography entry (Krishnadasababa ed.)
note: Gaudiya Vaisnava ritual manual, not strictly Tantric; author tied to Gadadhara's lineage (17th c.). No specialist dating in library; kept ~17th c. range consistent with current median.

title: Sātvatatantra
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Saiva Age
note: Satvata(samhita), a foundational Pancaratra scripture, re-dated LATER by Sanderson: not before the 9th c. and among the latest of the early Pancaratra texts (so 9th-10th c.). Much later than the current median 671; major change.

title: Daśavidhahetunirūpaṇa
basis: Potter, Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies vol. 1 (places Śrīnivāsa Dīkṣita / Śrīviśiṣṭādvaitabhāṣya among late post-Deśika Viśiṣṭādvaitins)
source_file: cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 1 - Potter.txt
note: NOT a tantra: a Viśiṣṭādvaita śāstra defending Pāñcarātra authority (ten arguments), by the late Śrīviśiṣṭādvaitabhāṣya author Śrīnivāsa Dīkṣita, a post-Vedāntadeśika (16th–18th c.) Viśiṣṭādvaitin. No firm library date; midpoint ~1700 differs >100y from current 847.

title: Ṣaṭsandarbha: 1. Tattvasandarbha
basis: Standard Gauḍīya chronology (Jīva Gosvāmī c. 1517–1608); no text-specific dating in library
note: Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava theological digest, not a tantra. Jīva Gosvāmī flourished mid-to-late 16th c.; Tattvasandarbha is the first of the Ṣaṭsandarbha. Library has no specialist dating; date is the established consensus. Matches current median.

title: Ṣaṭsandarbha: 2. Bhāgavatasandarbha
basis: Standard Gauḍīya chronology (Jīva Gosvāmī c. 1517–1608); no text-specific dating in library
note: Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava theological work, not a tantra. Bhagavat(a)sandarbha is the 2nd Sandarbha. No specialist library source; established consensus date retained. Matches current median.

title: Ṣaṭsandarbha: 3. Paramātmasandarbha
basis: Standard Gauḍīya chronology (Jīva Gosvāmī c. 1517–1608); no text-specific dating in library
note: Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava work, not a tantra. 3rd Sandarbha (Paramātmasandarbha). No specialist library source; established consensus retained. Matches current median.

title: Ṣaṭsandarbha: 4. Kṛṣṇasandarbha
basis: Standard Gauḍīya chronology (Jīva Gosvāmī c. 1517–1608); no text-specific dating in library
note: Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava work, not a tantra. 4th Sandarbha (Kṛṣṇasandarbha). No specialist library source; established consensus retained. Matches current median.

title: Ṣaṭsandarbha: 5. Bhaktisandarbha
basis: Freschi & Maas (eds.) 2017, Adaptive Reuse (Okita chapter)
source_file: cleaned_Freschi and Maas_Adaptive Reuse_Aspects of Creativity in South Asian Cultural History.txt
note: Gaudiya Vaisnava (not tantric). Same author Jiva Gosvami, dated 1517-1608; matches machine median.

title: Ṣaṭsandarbha: 6. Prītisandarbha
basis: Freschi & Maas (eds.) 2017, Adaptive Reuse (Okita chapter)
source_file: cleaned_Freschi and Maas_Adaptive Reuse_Aspects of Creativity in South Asian Cultural History.txt
note: Gaudiya Vaisnava (not tantric). Jiva dated 1517-1608 by specialist scholarship; matches machine median.

title: Ajaḍapramātṛsiddhi
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (Utpaladeva fl. c. 925-975)
note: One of Utpaladeva's three Siddhi tracts; same floruit c. 925-975.

title: Vaikhānasamantrapraśna
basis: Bronkhorst 2011, Buddhism in the Shadow of Brahmanism (Vaikhānasa Gṛhya/sūtra corpus); Alper 1989, Understanding Mantras
source_file: cleaned_Buddhism in the Shadow of Brahmanism_Johannes Bronkhorst, 2011.txt
note: Mantrapraśna is the most archaic (mantra) layer of the Vaikhānasa corpus; library has no precise specialist date for it, only general placement of the para-vedic Vaikhānasa sūtra material. Kept near current median, low confidence.

title: Viṣṇudharma
basis: Eliot 1921, Hinduism and Buddhism Vol. 2 (general Puranic dating; no specialist statement on the Viṣṇudharma itself in library)
source_file: cleaned_Hinduism and Buddhism,  Historical Sketch, Vol  2_Charles Eliot, 1921.txt
note: Viṣṇudharma (a Vaiṣṇava upapurāṇa, distinct from the later Viṣṇudharmottara) cited only in passing; no recent specialist date in library. Range follows general early-Puranic (Gupta) horizon; broadly consistent with current median.

title: Viṣvaksenasaṃhitā
basis: no explicit dating found; Sanderson 2009 cites text without dating it
note: Pāñcarātra saṃhitā; Sanderson cites it (re Śaiva/Pāñcarātra reinstallation) but does not date it; library lacks a Rastelli/Czerniak-type specialist dating. cur_nb/na 600-1000 retained as the broad Pāñcarātra-saṃhitā range but unsourced here.

title: Stotraratna
basis: Standard Śrīvaiṣṇava chronology; Yāmuna predates Rāmānuja (Olson 2007)
note: Authentic devotional hymn of Yāmuna, Rāmānuja's intellectual predecessor; floruit mid-10th to early 11th c. Midpoint shifts only slightly (~1002); within 100y of current median.

title: Īśvarapratyabhijñāvimarśinī
basis: Padoux, Vāc (1990); standard scholarly consensus on Abhinavagupta's floruit
note: Abhinavagupta's commentary on Utpaladeva's Īśvarapratyabhijñākārikā; firmly c. 1000 CE.

title: Mālinīślokavārttika: Kāṇḍa 1
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Abhinava's verse commentary on the Mālinīvijayottara; dated by author's floruit. Among his earlier (pre-Tantrāloka) works.

title: Mālinīślokavārttika: Kāṇḍa 2
basis: Padoux, Vāc (1990); standard consensus on Abhinavagupta's floruit
note: Abhinavagupta's verse commentary on the Mālinīvijayottara; his floruit c. 990-1015 CE.

title: Tantrasāra
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age; Sanderson, History through textual criticism
note: Dated by author's well-attested floruit; Abhinava gives Saptarṣi dates 4066/4068/4090 (c.990–1015 CE) for several of his works. Tantrasāra is a prose digest of the Tantrāloka.

title: Anuttaraprakāśapañcāśikā
note: A KSTS Kashmir Śaiva (Trika) hymn attributed to Ādyanātha; appears only as a catalogue entry (Potter Encyclopedia DU10.1; skt-en-mono.txt) with no dating discussion in the library.

title: Anuttarāṣṭikā
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Short devotional/non-dualist hymn (aṣṭaka) of Abhinavagupta, within his floruit c. 975-1025.

title: Paramārthasāra
basis: skt-en-mono (Abhinavagupta alive AD 1000); Sanderson, The Saiva Age
note: Abhinavagupta's recasting of Adisesa's earlier Paramarthasara; Abhinava fl. c. 975-1025. Current median 926 ~75y too early. Pratyabhijna/Trika digest, not a tantra.

title: Bhairavastava
basis: Padoux, Vāc (1990); standard consensus on Abhinavagupta's floruit
note: Short hymn by Abhinavagupta; dates to his floruit c. 990-1015 CE.

title: Svabodhasiddhi
basis: Potter (ed.) 1990, Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies vol. 5 (Bhūtirāja a teacher of Abhinavagupta, fl. 1014)
source_file: cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 5 - Potter 1990.txt
note: Bhūtirāja was a teacher of Abhinavagupta, hence fl. c. 950-1000; agrees with current median

title: Bodhapañcadaśikā
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Short Trika doctrinal work of Abhinavagupta, whose floruit Sanderson fixes at c. 975-1025. cur_med 932 slightly early.

title: Kramastotra
basis: Sanderson, History through textual criticism (citing Abhinava's own colophon date)
note: Saptarṣi 4066 ≈ 990 CE — a firm absolute date Abhinava gives himself. (Distinct from the older Kramastotra of Eraka quoted in Tantrālokaviveka.)

title: Bodhavilāsa
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (Abhinavagupta fl. c. 975-1025); Tuske ed. 2017 / Ratié (Kṣemarāja = Abhinavagupta's disciple)
source_file: cleaned_Indian Epistemology and Metaphysics-Bloomsbury Academic_Joerg Tuske, ed , 2017.txt
note: Kṣemarāja, pupil of Abhinavagupta (fl. c.975-1025 per Sanderson), so active early-mid 11th c.; current median 946 too early by ~75y but within tolerance

title: Manthānabhairava Tantra
basis: White 2003, Kiss of the Yogini
source_file: cleaned_Kiss of the Yogini. Tantric Sex in its South Asian Contexts_David Gordon White, 2003.txt
note: Massive late Kubjikā tantra (Kumārikākhaṇḍa etc.); Dyczkowski's text. Consistent with current median midpoint.

title: Paramārthasāra
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (Abhinavagupta's dated colophons)
note: Paramārthasāra is a genuine work of Abhinavagupta (cf. Flood, Tantric Body, Yogarāja's vivṛti). Within his attested floruit c. 990–1015. Dating unchanged.

title: Parātriṃśikā
basis: Sanderson, Saivism and the Tantric Traditions (Trika scripture, established in Kashmir by early 9th c.); Padoux, Vac, 1990
note: Short Trika scripture (36 verses, ascribed to Rudrayamala), commented by Abhinavagupta; predates early-9th-c. establishment of Trika in Kashmir. Consistent with median; distinguish from Abhinava's PT-vivarana (c.1000).

title: Parātriṃśikāvivaraṇa
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Saiva Age (Abhinavagupta floruit)
note: This is the Parātriṃśikā-vivaraṇa, a COMMENTARY by the firmly datable Abhinavagupta (floruit c. 975-1025); composed c. 1000 CE, not the base scripture.

title: Paryantapañcāśikā
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (Abhinava floruit); attribution confirmed in Padoux, Vāc
source_file: cleaned_Vac. The Concept of the Word in Selected Hindu Tantras_Andre Padoux, 1990.txt
note: A short late work of Abhinavagupta; dated by his floruit c. 975–1025.

title: Pratyabhijñāhṛdaya
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (Abhinavagupta fl. c. 975-1025); Tuske ed. 2017 / Ratié (Kṣemarāja = Abhinavagupta's disciple)
source_file: cleaned_Indian Epistemology and Metaphysics-Bloomsbury Academic_Joerg Tuske, ed , 2017.txt
note: Kṣemarāja's well-known Pratyabhijñā compendium; pupil of Abhinavagupta so active early-mid 11th c.; current median 956 ~50-75y early

title: Bhairavānukaraṇastotra
basis: Sanderson, History through textual criticism (Kṣemarāja as Abhinavagupta's pupil); Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Kṣemarāja was Abhinavagupta's direct pupil, so active a generation later (c. 1000–1050); current median ~945 is slightly early.

title: Spandakārikā
basis: N.N. Bhattacharyya 2006, History of the Tantric Religion; Feuerstein 1997 (Vasugupta and pupil Kallaṭa both 9th c.)
source_file: cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: Attributed to Vasugupta or his pupil Kallaṭa (fl. under Avantivarman r. 855-883), so mid/late 9th c.; Spandanirṇaya commentary by Kṣemarāja is 11th c.

title: Tantrāloka
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Abhinavagupta's magnum opus on Trika/Kaula ritual and philosophy, a mature work composed within his floruit c. 975-1025. cur_med 933 slightly early but within range.

title: Timirodghāṭana
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism; cited in Kṣemarāja (Mantramarga Bhairava scripture list)
note: Only attested as an early Saiva (Bhairava/Vidyapitha) scripture in scripture-lists and Ksemaraja quotations; no precise specialist date, broad Mantramarga 7th-9th c.

title: Īśvarapratyabhijñāvṛtti
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (Utpaladeva fl. c. 925-975)
note: Utpaladeva's own vṛtti on his Īśvarapratyabhijñākārikā; fl. c. 925-975, consistent with current median.

title: Janmamaraṇavicāra
basis: Padoux (cited in skt-en-mono compendium), prefatory to ed. of Janmamaranavicara; cf. Nemec 'Language of Life and Death'
note: Kashmir Saiva (Pratyabhijna/non-dualist) prose work. Author is grand-disciple of Abhinavagupta (disciple of Yogaraja, pupil of Ksemaraja) and contemporary of Kashmiri king Anantadeva (r. c.1028-1063), placing him mid-11th c. Earlier than current median (~140y shift).

title: Svabodhodayamañjarī
basis: Sanderson (History through Textual Criticism) and Dyczkowski/skt-en-mono synthesis
note: Kaula yogic tract; author post-Utpaladeva (c.925) and not later than Abhinavagupta. Distinct from the Pāñcarātra Vāmanadatta. Slightly earlier than current median; <100y change.

title: Spandakārikā
consensus_date: second half of the 9th c. CE
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism; Sanderson, Śaivism and the Tantric Traditions
note: Foundational Trika/Spanda verse-text; composed by Vasugupta or Kallaṭa, mid-to-late 9th c. Kashmir. Matches cur_med.

title: Vātūlanāthasūtra
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (Krama/Kālīkula text, commentary by Anantaśakti)
note: Late Kashmirian Krama (Kālīkula) scripture; Sanderson links its commentator Anantaśakti to later Krama exegesis. Krama emerged late 9th–10th c.; sūtra likely 10th–12th c. NB: White assigns commentator Anantaśaktipāda to 16th c., but that is the commentary, not the sūtra.

title: Vijñānabhairava
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (listed among early Trika/Vidyāpīṭha sources; known to Pāñcarātra AhSaṃ)
note: Kashmir Trika scripture cited by Abhinavagupta (c.1000) and known to the Pāñcarātra Ahirbudhnyasaṃhitā; placed among the early Trika revelation, 8th-9th c. Midpoint 800, within ~70y of current median.

title: Ānandatantra
basis: Sanderson (per Abhinavagupta, Tantraloka), Saiva Age; via skt-en-mono compendium
note: Vama-srotas (Tumburu/four-sisters) scripture quoted by Abhinavagupta (so pre-c.1000); the Vama division is among the oldest Saiva tantric currents ('as old or older'), broadly 7th-9th c. No precise date.

title: Brahmayāmala (Picumata)
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (core text); cf. Hatley 2018 BraYā ed.
note: Vidyāpītha Yāmala scripture; listed in Skandapurāṇa-Ambikākhaṇḍa ms of 810; major source for Laghuśaṃvara. Far earlier than cur_med 830 -> changed.

title: Devīrahasya (Devīrahasyatantra)
basis: no specialist dating located in library
note: Late Śākta/Śrīvidyā ritual digest (Varāhī-related); only an incidental mention found, no scholarly date. Library offers nothing specific; current_med 785 is unsupported and likely far too early for this late compilation.

title: Gorakṣayogaśāstram
basis: Mallinson, cited in White (ed.), Yoga in Practice 2011
note: Hatha-yoga text in the Goraksasataka group attributed to Gorakhnath. Mallinson dates the 'original Goraksa Sataka' to the 10th-11th c.; the legendary Gorakhnath is conventionally placed 12th-13th c. (White). Attribution is pseudepigraphic, so date uncertain; specialist (Mallinson) date adopted, earlier than current median.

title: Utpaladeva: Īśvarasiddhi
basis: Sanderson, dating cited in Nemec/Somananda ed. (skt-en-mono); Sanderson, The Saiva Age
note: Utpaladeva fl. c. 925-975 (Sanderson's standard dating); current median 924 a touch early but within range. Pratyabhijna work, not a tantra proper.

title: Īśvarapratyabhijñāvivṛtivimarśinī, Vol. 1
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (colophon date)
note: IPVV firmly dated by colophon to 1014/15 CE. Dating unchanged.

title: Īśvarapratyabhijñāvivṛtivimarśinī, Vol. 2
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (colophon date)
note: IPVV firmly dated by colophon to 1014/15 CE. Dating unchanged.

title: Īśvarapratyabhijñāvivṛtivimarśinī, Vol. 3
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (colophon date)
note: IPVV firmly dated by colophon to Saptarṣisaṃvat 4090 = Kalisaṃvat 4115 = 1014/15 CE, Abhinavagupta's latest dated work. Dating unchanged.

title: Īśvarapratyabhijñāvimarśinī
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (Abhinavagupta's dated colophons)
note: IPV (shorter comm. on Utpaladeva's IPK) precedes the longer IPVV (completed 1014/15). Within Abhinavagupta's floruit. Dating unchanged.

title: Īśānaśivagurudeva: Īśānaśivagurudevapaddhati, Volume 1
basis: Flood, gavin-flood; ISP follows the 11th-c. Somaśambhupaddhati
note: Vol 1 of the same Kerala paddhati; post-Somaśambhu. Consistent with current median.

title: Jñānārṇavatantra
consensus_date: late medieval (Śrīvidyā tantra), c. 15th–17th c. CE
basis: Encyclopedia of Tantra (Santideva 1999) / skt-en-mono; no specialist dated study in library
note: Śākta Śrīvidyā/Tripurasundarī tantra (Kāmarāja/15-syllable mantra), generally regarded as a late text; cited in the smṛti-digest Nirṇayasindhu. NOT the Jain Jñānārṇava of Śubhacandra. Library lacks a precise specialist date; estimate departs strongly from machine median of 702.

title: Kālasaṃkarṣiṇīmatatantraṭippaṇī
basis: no specific dating found in library; Krama/Kālīkula context (Sanderson) only generally

title: Kaularahasya
basis: no specialist dating located in library
note: Only an undated quotation of Kaularahasya (re the five makāras, via Haṃsavilāsa) found in Santideva's Encyclopedia of Tantra; no scholarly composition date in the library. Left null per instructions.

title: Kulapradīpa
basis: no specialist dating found; only terminus ante quem from Hamsavilasa (Kiss, in White ed. 2011)
note: Kaula/Sakta work by the Gauda (Bengal) author Sivanandacarya. Library gives no date, only that it is a source quoted by Hamsamitthu's Hamsavilasa (terminus ante quem ~17th-18th c.). No firm consensus date; none asserted.

title: Kulasāra
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age / History through Textual Criticism
note: Kaula/Śākta scripture cited by Sanderson; listed among texts known to early Kashmirian (10th-11th c.) authors and in Nepalese MSS (terminus ~11th c.), so composed by 9th-10th c.

title: Kulavṛtti
basis: Associated with Pūrṇānanda of Bengal (fl. late 16th c., Ṣaṭcakranirūpaṇa 1577); no text-specific dating in library
note: Bengali Śākta/Kaula work attributed to Pūrṇānanda. Library has no specialist dating; dated by association with Pūrṇānanda's late-16th-c. floruit. Matches current median.

basis: no dating scholarship located

note: Abhinavagupta's Kaula commentary on the Parātriṃśikā; dated by his floruit. Author-work; current median confirmed.

Kashmiri Trika scholar. Current median (1593) likely too early but unconfirmed in library, so bounds widened, low confidence.

basis: Santideva, Encyclopedia of Tantra 1999; Benard, Chinnamasta 2010
note: Section of the c.1820 Prāṇatoṣiṇī nibandha by Rāmatoṣaṇa; modern Tantric digest. Current date confirmed.

basis: Santideva, Encyclopedia of Tantra 1999; Benard, Chinnamasta 2010

basis: Kṣemarāja = pupil of Abhinavagupta (fl. c.975-1025): Ratié in Tuske ed. 2017; Sanderson, Śaiva Age

note: Work of Kṣemarāja, direct pupil of Abhinavagupta; active c.1000-1050. cur_med confirmed.

basis: terminus ante quem from Kṣemarāja (pupil of Abhinavagupta, fl. c.1000-1025) who commented it; Sanderson, Śaivism and the Tantric Traditions

title: Spandakārikā
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Kallaṭa active under Avantivarman (r. 855-883); Sanderson places the work in the second half of the 9th c., confirming the machine median.

title: Śrīkarabhāṣya
basis: no specialist dating in library; only bibliographic listing
source_file: cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt

note: Bengali Śākta/Śrīvidyā tantric author, pupil of Brahmānandagiri; confirmed 16th c. Not Gaṅgeśa's Nyāya Tattvacintāmaṇi. Current median fits.

title: Tattvacintāmaṇi (Śrītattvacintāmaṇi), chapters 11-15
basis: Pūrṇānanda of Bengal, fl. late 16th c. (Ṣaṭcakranirūpaṇa = ch. 6 of the work, dated 1577); no specialist library source
note: Same Śākta compendium (chapters 11–15). No specialist library dating; dated by Pūrṇānanda's late-16th-c. floruit. Matches current median.

title: Tattvacintāmaṇi (Śrītattvacintāmaṇi), chapters 16 to end
basis: Pūrṇānanda of Bengal, fl. late 16th c. (Ṣaṭcakranirūpaṇa, a chapter of the Śrītattvacintāmaṇi, dated 1577); no specialist library source
note: Śākta compendium; its 6th chapter is the famous Ṣaṭcakranirūpaṇa (cited in Padoux/Avalon bibliography but undated there). Pūrṇānanda flourished late 16th c.; date by association. Matches current median.

title: Svacchandatantra
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (2009), relative chronology of the Mantramārga; cf. Mallinson-Singleton 2017
note: Principal Mantrapīṭha scripture; redacted after early Niśvāsa corpus, before Tantrasadbhāva and Netra. Far earlier than current median 975 — CHANGED.

title: Sarvajñānottara
basis: Sanderson 2009 (The Saiva Age) lists it among the early Siddhanta scriptures; Watson 2006, following Goodall's criteria, places it before Sadyojyotis (7th-8th c.)
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Early Saiva Siddhanta scripture (Mantramarga), demonstrably pre-Sadyojyotis; one of the genuinely early/datable items in this batch. cur_med 784 too late by ~150-200 yrs.

title: mukuṭāgama
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Saiva Age (groups the Makuta among the south-Indian Saiddhantika scriptures)
note: Mukuta/Makuta is one of the 28 Saiva Siddhanta Agamas; Sanderson treats it as a south-Indian recension exhibiting the later Saktization he otherwise links to the 13th c. onward, so likely a later/south-Indian text, not 7th c. No firm single date in library; broad range given.

title: Tantracintāmaṇi
basis: A. Bharati, Tantric Tradition (1965)
note: Author minister under Bhūpālendra Malla and Bhāskara Malla of Kathmandu; fl. late 17th-early 18th c. Consistent with current median.

title: Tantrāloka, chapters 1-14 (āhnikas)
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (2009)
note: Same work as preceding (first half of Tantrāloka); dated by Abhinavagupta's floruit.

title: Tantrāloka, chapters 15-38 (āhnikas)
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (2009)
note: Abhinavagupta's magnum opus on Trika ritual/doctrine; dated by his floruit (commonly placed c. 990-1015).

title: tārārahasyavṛtti 2
basis: Alexis Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (2009)
note: Item is the VṚTTI (commentary), dated firmly 1630 by Sanderson (author Gauḍīya Śaṅkara). The root Tārārahasya of Brahmānandagiri (teacher of Pūrṇānanda) is 16th c. Author field lists Brahmānanda but text is the commentary.

title: Tattvaratnāvalī
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (Vimalaśiva = Kalacuri rājaguru)
note: Author Vimalaśiva was rājaguru of the Kalacuris of Tripurī (Jayasiṃha/Vijayasiṃha r. c.1188-1210), so a Śaiva Tattvaratnāvalī by him is late 12th c. NB: a different, Buddhist Tattvaratnāvalī is by Advayavajra (~11th c.).

title: Tridaśaḍāmarāpratyaṅgirāviṣayakanānātantra
basis: no dating scholarship found
note: A 'nānātantra' (miscellany) on Tridaśaḍāmara / Pratyaṅgirā material; no specialist dating in the library (Sanderson, Hatley, Törzsök silent on this title). Likely a late Śākta compilation but undatable from sources read. Left null.

title: Tridaśaḍāmaratantre Siddhikālīstotra
basis: no specialist dating located in reference-literature/
note: Kālī stotra embedded in Ḍāmaratantra material; the Ḍāmara corpus is late/popular Śākta-tantric, but no sourced consensus date found in library.

title: Vāmakeśvarīmata (Nityāṣoḍaśikārṇava)
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (2009); fundamental scripture of the Tripurasundarī/Śrīvidyā cult, pre-Jayaratha
note: Developed Śākta scripture, no direct Vidyāpīṭha antecedents; predates Jayaratha's 12th-13th c. Vivaraṇa. Library has no firmer century; broad 9th-10th c. retained.

title: Yogavāsiṣṭha, Part 6, with Tātparyaprakāśa
basis: Mallinson & Singleton 2017, Roots of Yoga
source_file: cleaned_Roots of Yoga_James Mallinson, Mark Singleton, 2017.txt
note: Mokṣopāya base text, 10th-11th c.; commentary Tātparyaprakāśa is later (c. 17th-18th c.). Changed: text predates current median by ~165y.

title: Yogavāsiṣṭha, Part 1 (Vairāgyaprakaraṇa), with Tātparyaprakāśa
basis: Mallinson & Singleton 2017, Roots of Yoga
source_file: cleaned_Roots of Yoga_James Mallinson, Mark Singleton, 2017.txt
note: Underlying text = Kashmirian Mokṣopāya, 10th-11th c.; dated here rather than the much later Tātparyaprakāśa commentary by Ānandabodhendra Sarasvatī (c. 17th-18th c., no library date found). Changed: text predates current median by ~165y.

title: Yogavāsiṣṭha, Part 2 (Mumukṣuvyavahāraprakaraṇa), with Tātparyaprakāśa
basis: Mallinson & Singleton 2017, Roots of Yoga
source_file: cleaned_Roots of Yoga_James Mallinson, Mark Singleton, 2017.txt
note: Mokṣopāya base text, 10th-11th c.; commentary much later. Changed: text predates current median by ~169y.

title: Āgamaprāmāṇya
basis: Standard Yāmuna floruit (Potter, Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies; Pāñcarātra scholarship)
source_file: cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 1 - Potter.txt
note: Genuine work of the Śrīvaiṣṇava teacher Yāmunācārya (c. 10th-11th c.), defending Pāñcarātra Āgama. Library confirms author but gives no narrower date; retained 950-1040. No change.

title: Āśaucadīpikā
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Vedajñānaguru II of Cidambaram is firmly placed in the 16th c. by Sanderson; his Āśaucadīpikā is a 16th-c. Śaiva Siddhānta ritual/expiation work. Changed: ~384y later than current median (1166).

title: Acintyaviśvasādākhyam
consensus_date: 10th c. CE (pre-11th, post early Siddhānta āgamas)
basis: Goudriaan/Brunner, in Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism (Padoux festschrift, 1992); Flood, Tantric Body (2006)
source_file: cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism. Studies in Honor of André Padoux_Teun Goudriaan, ed, 1992.txt
note: Late Śaiva Siddhānta Upāgama 'born from paddhatis'; Somaśambhu (11th c.) follows it line-by-line, so terminus ante ~11th c. No precise date in library; estimate 10th c. Midpoint 950 vs cur_med 712 differs >100y -> changed.

title: Advayatārakopaniṣad
basis: White, Yoga in Practice (2012)
note: Advayatāraka is one of the brief mantra-/nāda-yoga Yoga Upaniṣads; listed in the South Indian collection. The Yoga-Upaniṣad corpus is N.Indian 800-1300 / S.Indian 1300-1500; Advayatāraka is generally treated as a later medieval Yoga Upaniṣad. Current median 630 far too early; only catalogue-level mentions in library, hence low confidence.

title: Āgamakalpalatā
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: Only a bare mention in the Encyclopedia of Tantra; no datable scholarship. Yadunātha Cakravartī is a Bengali Smārta-Tantric digest author conventionally placed ca. 16th-17th c., but no source read confirms this.

title: Agnikāryapaddhati
basis: Sanderson (Meaning in Tantric Ritual / Hindu Tantric Chaplains) cites it only as a Kashmirian Śāradā MS source
note: A Kashmirian Śaiva ritual paddhati known only from manuscripts; a paddhati (post-scriptural digest, here post-Somaśambhu/Svacchanda usage) so necessarily medieval (likely 11th-13th c.), but no firm composition date is given in the library. Left null (low).

title: Ahirbudhnyasaṃhitā, Volume 1
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Pāñcarātra saṃhitā. Sanderson dates it AFTER Utpaladeva (fl. c. 925), since it draws on Kashmirian Śākta non-dualism; thus 10th c. or later, considerably later than the machine date of 799. Changed.

title: Ahirbudhnyasaṃhitā, Volume 2
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (re-dating against Schrader's 8th c.)
note: Pāñcarātra saṃhitā. Sanderson overturns Schrader's 8th-c. dating: it postdates Kṣemarāja (early 11th c.) and draws on Abhinavagupta and Kashmirian Śaiva nondualism, so 11th-12th c. Major change from cur_med 855.

title: Matsyendranātha: Akulavīratantra
basis: White 2003, Kiss of the Yogini
source_file: cleaned_Kiss of the Yogini. Tantric Sex in its South Asian Contexts_David Gordon White, 2003.txt
note: White dates this specific Akulavīratantra (ascribed to Mīnasahajānanda) to 12th-13th c., distinct from the mid-11th c. Kaulajñānanirṇaya. Changed >100y from current median.

title: Amṛtabindūpaniṣad
basis: White (tr.), Yoga in Practice 2012 (Yoga Upaniṣads chapter)
note: Earliest of the northern Yoga Upaniṣads (800-1300 corpus); placed early in the range, ~9th c. Original title Brahmabindu.

title: Amṛtanādopaniṣad
basis: J. C. Ruff in D. G. White (ed.), Yoga in Practice, 2012
note: Yoga Upaniṣad of the early ('bindu/nāda') cluster (Eliade groups Amṛta-/Nāda-bindu together). Ruff's 9th-13th c. range applied; brackets median 874.

title: Aṃśumatkāśyapāgama
consensus_date: c. 9th-12th c. CE (later Śaiva Siddhānta Āgama)
basis: Einoo & Takashima eds. 2005 (From Material to Deity), citing the Aṃśumadāgama as a Saiddhāntika scriptural source
source_file: cleaned_From Material to Deity. Indian Rituals of Consecrations_Einoo & Takashima, ed., 2005.txt
note: Kāśyapa-affiliated South-Indian Śaiva Siddhānta pratiṣṭhā/śilpa Āgama (Aṃśumat/Aṃśumadbhedāgama). Library has no firm date; such ritual/iconographic Āgamas are generally placed among the later Siddhānta scriptures (Sanderson groups the southern Āgamas after the early Niśvāsa corpus). Broad range; not the 6th c. machine date.

title: Aniruddhasaṃhitā
basis: no match in reference-literature/
note: Pāñcarātra saṃhitā; no Aniruddhasaṃhitā dating (Rastelli/Smith/Czerniak) present in library. Date unverified; cur_med likely a machine artifact.

title: Anuttaraprakāśapañcāśikā
basis: no dating scholarship located (Potter lists author as date-unknown)
source_file: cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 1 - Potter.txt
note: Potter's bibliography files Ādyanātha under prefix 'DU' = Date Unknown; no century given. A short Kashmiri Śaiva nondualist hymn (KSTS, ed. with Bodhapañcadaśikā). No specialist date in library; left null.

title: Āryamañjuśrīmūlakalpa, Part 2
consensus_date: 8th c. CE (core; whole spans early 8th-11th c.)
basis: Wallis 2002, Mediating the Power of Buddhas (citing Przyluski & Matsunaga)
source_file: cleaned_Mediating the Power of Buddhas. Ritual in the Manjusrimulakalpa_Glenn Wallis, 2002.txt
note: Layered/composite text; chapters span early 8th to 11th c. (Mmk 1-3 added ~11th c. per Przyluski). 8th c. working estimate for the body; Part 2 not separately dated. Midpoint 750 vs 583 -> changed.

title: Āryamañjuśrīmūlakalpa, Part 3
basis: Wallis 2002, Mediating the Power of Buddhas; cf. Delhey, Textual Sources of the MMK
source_file: cleaned_Mediating the Power of Buddhas. Ritual in the Manjusrimulakalpa_Glenn Wallis, 2002.txt
note: MMK is a stratified compilation; Wallis gives working estimate 8th c. (Pāla era), with partial Chinese translations from early 8th c. (Delhey). Later strata reach 10th-11th c.; 8th c. is the consensus for the core.

title: Āścaryayogamālā
basis: no specialist dating of this title in library; only related Rasaratnākara discussed
note: Alchemical/rasaśāstra work, not a tantra. The library discusses the alchemist Nāgārjuna's Rasaratnākara but contains no dating statement for the Āścaryayogamālā itself; the legendary 'alchemist Nāgārjuna' attribution is not chronologically reliable. No source read gives a date; current median kept, confidence none.

title: Aśeṣakulavallarī
basis: No dating scholarship found in library; only off-list / muktalib7 catalogue references in src
note: Kaula/Srividya digest (paddhati) by the named author Gaṇananāthabhaṭṭa, hence a relatively late-medieval/early-modern compilation, not an early scripture. Library has no specialist date; bounds are a rough author-type estimate. cur_med 779 implausibly early for a named nibandha.

title: Bhagavadgītā
basis: Watson 2006, The Self's Awareness of Itself (Bhaṭṭa Rāmakaṇṭha a tenth-century Kashmiri Saiddhāntika)
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Dated item is Rāmakaṇṭha's Śaiva (Sarvatobhadra) commentary on the Gītā, not the Gītā itself; Bhaṭṭa Rāmakaṇṭha II fl. 10th c. Kashmir.

title: Bhagavadgītā (Vīraśaiva-bhāṣya)
basis: N. N. Bhattacharyya, History of the Tantric Religion (Vīraśaiva movement dating)
source_file: cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: This item is a Vīraśaiva-bhāṣya COMMENTARY on the Gītā, not the root text. The Vīraśaiva (Liṅgāyat) tradition begins 12th c. (Basava); such Gītā commentaries (e.g. Śrīpati tradition) are 13th–15th c. Root Gītā itself is c. 2nd c. BCE–2nd c. CE (no library dating). Current median 1002 is a misplaced placeholder.

title: Bhāvacūḍāmaṇi
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (in: Les sources et le temps / IFP)
note: Vidyākaṇṭha = pupil of Bhaṭṭa Rāmakaṇṭha (fl. c. 950–1000) and contemporary of Abhinavagupta; Bhāvacūḍāmaṇi is his comm. on the Mayasaṃgraha. Dating unchanged.

title: Sadyojyoti: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa — Bhogakārikā
basis: Watson 2006, The Self's Awareness of Itself, ch. 'The Date of Sadyojyotis'; following Sanderson
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Bhogakārikā explicitly listed among Sadyojyotis's works; Sanderson favours earlier (7th c.) on doctrinal grounds. Midpoint 700 ~ current 715, no major change.

title: Bhūtaśuddhitantra
basis: Padoux Festschrift (Goudriaan ed. 1992) cites it only via the late Tantrasamgraha digest; no independent dating in library
source_file: cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism. Studies in Honor of André Padoux_Teun Goudriaan, ed, 1992.txt
note: Late Sakta tantra transmitted within nibandha/digest literature; no explicit scholarly date in library. Corpus-based estimate; cur_med 781 too early.

title: Bhuvaneśvarīmahāstotra
basis: none found in library
note: Śākta hymn to Bhuvaneśvarī (one of the ten Mahāvidyās). Mahāvidyā cult is attested in late/medieval east-Indian Śākta corpus (Sanderson), suggesting a later date than 802, but no source dates THIS stotra or its author Pṛthvīdhara. The Pṛthvīdhara in Bendall is a Mṛcchakaṭika commentator, probably a different person. No supported bounds.

title: Abhinavagupta: Bodhapañcadaśikā
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (2009)
note: Short Trika doctrinal work by Abhinavagupta; dated by his floruit.

title: Brahmasandhāna
basis: none found
note: No scholarship dating this title found in the library under either diacritic or ASCII-folded forms. No date assigned.

title: Brahmavidyā Upaniṣad
basis: Mallinson & Singleton 2017, Roots of Yoga (citing Bouy 1994, Les Nātha-yogin et les Upaniṣads)
source_file: cleaned_Roots of Yoga_James Mallinson, Mark Singleton, 2017.txt
note: A Yoga Upaniṣad; per Bouy 1994 the corpus borrows wholesale from hathayoga texts and postdates hatha's formative period, so it is centuries later than the machine date of 863.

title: Bṛhannīlatantra
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (2009); Kinsley, Tantric Visions of the Divine Feminine (1998)
note: Late east-Indian/Assamese Śākta scripture of the ten-Mahāvidyā (Toḍala/Nīla) corpus, adding Kāmākhyā of Assam; this Mahāvidyā/Kāmākhyā stratum is late medieval, well after the 15th-c. Sarvānandanātha milieu Sanderson describes.

title: Kṛṣṇānanda Āgamavāgīśa: Bṛhattantrasāra
basis: Goudriaan (ed.), Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism (Padoux festschrift), 1992
source_file: cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism. Studies in Honor of André Padoux_Teun Goudriaan, ed, 1992.txt
note: Bengali Śākta ritual digest (Tantrasāra). Festschrift assigns it to the 17th c.; the common compromise (Kṛṣṇānanda fl. c. 1580–1600) gives a late-16th/early-17th c. range. Midpoint 1600 vs median 1568 = ~32y, not a >100y change.

title: Candrajñānāgama (Vīraśaiva recension)
consensus_date: before c. 1000 CE (underlying Śaiva tantra); Vīraśaiva recension 12th c. or later
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (Candrajñāna excerpted by Hṛdayaśiva of Mālava, c. 1000); Vīraśaiva context 12th c. (Basava)
note: Candrajñāna is a Śaiva tantra of unknown affiliation cited (thus pre-dating) Hṛdayaśiva's c.1000 compilation. The specifically Vīraśaiva recension is a later (12th c.+) redaction. Bounds bracket the underlying tantra.

title: Candrajñānāgama (Candrajñāna)
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (Śaiva Tantras known to early Kashmirian exegetes Abhinavagupta/Kṣemarāja, fl. c.975-1025)
note: Śaiva scripture (affiliation uncertain in Sanderson) attested as known to/quoted by the early Kashmirian exegetes, so terminus ante quem c.10th c.; no precise composition date in library. Range is corpus-level inference, hence low confidence.

title: Candrajñānāgama (Candrajñāna)
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (Śaiva Siddhānta scripture list)
note: Śaiva Siddhānta scripture (one of the Siddhāntāgamas), affiliation 'unknown' per Sanderson; known to the early Kashmirian exegetes (terminus ante quem c. 1000, Abhinavagupta/Kṣemarāja). No precise specialist date in library; broad 8th-10th c. Current median within range.

title: Carcāstava
basis: no specialist dating found; library has only Pañcastavī book-title references (Encyclopedia of Tantra; skt-en-mono)
note: Kashmiri Śākta/Śrīvidyā hymn-pentad of uncertain date; no scholarly consensus in this library, retaining traditional ~11th c. placement only as machine estimate, not grounded.

title: Śrīvatsa: Cidgaganacandrikā
basis: Encyclopedia of Tantra (Santideva 1999), Krama lineage of Hrasvanātha c.1025-1075
note: Krama commentary on Kramastotra of Siddhanātha; author indebted to Hrasvanātha (c.1025-75), predates Maheśvarānanda. Consistent with current median.

title: Cinmantramālinī
basis: none found
note: No specialist dating located in the library for this title or its attribution to Prakāśānanda. Retain machine estimate; unverified.

title: Cinmayadīkṣāvidhāna
basis: no specialist dating found in reference-literature/
note: Ritual initiation (dīkṣā) manual, Muktabodha digital-library source. No scholarly dating located in the library; cannot date responsibly.

title: Darśanopaniṣat (Darśana / Jābāladarśana Upaniṣad)
basis: Bouy 1994 (Les Nātha-yogin et les Upaniṣads); Mallinson & Singleton, Roots of Yoga 2017
source_file: cleaned_Roots of Yoga_James Mallinson, Mark Singleton, 2017.txt
note: CHANGED >100y: the Darśanopaniṣad is a late Yoga Upaniṣad that borrows wholesale from haṭha texts and postdates the Haṭhapradīpikā (15th c.); Bouy/Mallinson place the corpus in the 14th-18th c. cur_med 1031 far too early. Not a tantra proper.

title: Dattātreyabodha
basis: no specific dating; Dattātreya as Tantric/Yoga figure prominent from c. 12th-13th c. (Mallinson 2003; Freschi-Maas)
note: No scholarship in library dates this specific text. Dattātreya becomes a tantric/yoga deity-author only from c. 12th c. (Dattātreyayogaśāstra ~13th c., Mallinson). The Dattātreyabodha is a minor late attribution; no sourced date, confidence none.

title: Devīnāmavilāsa
basis: Hanneder, 'Sāhib Kaul's presentation of Pratyabhijñā philosophy in Devīnāmavilāsa' (title only in Potter Encyclopedia bibliography)
source_file: cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 1 - Potter.txt
note: Library has no explicit date statement, only a study title attributing the work to Sāhib Kaul; Kashmiri Śākta author of early-mid 17th c. Range kept consistent with current median.

title: Dhyānaratnāvali
basis: no occurrence in library
note: No hit anywhere in reference-literature/. No source read; left null.

title: Dīkṣādarśa
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (works of Vedajñānaguru II)
note: Śaiva Siddhānta initiation manual by Nigamajñānadeśika (Vedajñāna II), Cidambaram; one of the works in which late Āgamas are first cited. Sanderson dates it to the latter half of the 16th c.

title: Dīkṣāpaddhati
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (Śaiva dīkṣā-paddhati genre; Kalādīkṣāpaddhati of Manoda/Manodadatta, Somaśambhupaddhati ~1100)
note: Generic Śaiva initiation manual (paddhati); no dated identification of this specific Muktabodha/HK transcription. Paddhati genre flourishes 11th-13th c. (after Somaśambhu). Date uncertain.

title: Dīkṣāprakāśa
basis: authorial attribution (Raghunandanaprasāda Śukla); no ancient dating in library
note: Modern compilation/initiation manual by the named modern editor-author Raghunandanaprasāda Śukla (Woodroffe-era Hindi Tantra milieu), not an early tantra. The Encyclopedia chapter-list near 'Dīkṣāprakāśa' belongs to a different Kashmiri work. Not a medieval text; date is modern.

title: Dīkṣottara
consensus_date: relatively late Niśvāsa layer, c. 7th–9th c. CE
basis: Goodall (Kirana/Parakhya ed., in skt-en-mono); Sanderson 2009, The Saiva Age
note: Uttara(ottara)sutra layer of the Nisvasa corpus; Goodall ranks it 'a relatively late date' alongside Kirana/Matanga/Parakhya. Niśvāsa Mūlasūtra is 450-550 but Dīkṣottara is a later addition. No firm century in library; range estimated. Current median 926 likely too late.

title: Durgārcanasmṛti
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: Sakta Durga-worship smrti/digest from shivashakti.com (Mike Magee); attributed to a modern editor Laksminarayana Goswami. No scholarly dating in library; likely a late/modern Sakta liturgical compilation, not an early tantra.

title: Dviśatikālottara
basis: Goodall (ed. Śatika Kālajñāna) and Sanderson; Kālottara among earliest Śaiva Siddhānta scriptures, pre-10th-c. commentators
note: One of the short non-eclectic Kālottara recensions; Kālottara is an early Saiddhāntika scripture (early Nepalese ms; commented by Rāmakaṇṭha 10th c.). Far earlier than current median 981 — CHANGED.

title: Bhagavadgītā (Gītārthasaṃgraha)
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age 2009 (dates of Abhinavagupta)
note: Despite the 'Bhagavadgītā' label, this file is Abhinavagupta's Gītārthasaṃgraha commentary on the Gītā, not the epic Gītā itself. Dated by Abhinavagupta's floruit, c. 1000 CE; far later than cur_med 854.

title: Gorakṣaguṭikā
basis: Mallinson, Khecarīvidyā (2003) and White, Yoga in Practice / Sinister Yogis: Nāth haṭha corpus (Gorakṣaśataka group) tied to Gorakṣanātha and the 13th-c. emergence of the Nāth Siddhas
note: Haṭhayoga text of the Gorakṣa(śataka/Vivekamārtaṇḍa) corpus attributed to Gorakṣanātha; oldest mss are late and composition is post-1100. cur_med 760 too early; midpoint moves +440y (>100).

title: Guhyasamājatantra
basis: Davidson 2002, Indian Esoteric Buddhism: A Social History of the Tantric Movement
source_file: cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism_ A Social History of the Tantric Movement.txt
note: Foundational yoga/Mahayoga tantra; Davidson dates composition to mid-8th c. (materials 'unassailably from the eighth century'). Machine date 645 ~110y too early.

title: Gurustutiratnāvalī
basis: no dating found in library (only an unrelated modern pandit 'Govinda Kaula' in Sanderson's chaplains article)
note: The only 'Govinda Kaula' hit is a 19th-c. Kashmiri pandit working with Grierson, NOT this author. No specialist dating of the Gurustutiratnāvalī in library; 18th-c. range retained from current median (cf. cited Brill IIJ / McGill sources outside library).

title: Hāhārāvatantra
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: No genuine scholarship on this minor Śākta tantra in reference-literature/ (only a spurious 'ardhahārāvasāne' substring match). No grounded date; null.

title: Haṃsasāra
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: Obscure Śākta/Niruttara-tantra text (src: shivashakti.com/niruttar). No scholarly dating located; only false-positive hits on the compound haṃsa-sārasa in poetry. Consensus null.

title: Haṃsa Upaniṣad
basis: White 2011, Yoga in Practice (introduction to the Yoga Upaniṣads)
note: Late (Yoga) Upaniṣad. White places the North Indian Yoga Upaniṣads 800-1300 CE and dates the Haṃsa specifically to 'around 1200', notably later than current median ~902. Midpoint 1200 vs 902 = +298y, changed.

title: Haṭhayogapradīpikā
basis: multiple secondary sources (Yoga Powers, Jacobsen 2012; White, Yoga in Practice)
note: Consistently dated 15th c. (Mallinson school); matches current median. Compilation of older āsana/haṭha verses.

title: hayaśīrṣapañcarātra
basis: Einoo & Takashima eds. 2005, From Material to Deity (Pancaratra liturgy / Agni Purana connection)
source_file: cleaned_From Material to Deity. Indian Rituals of Consecrations_Einoo & Takashima, ed., 2005.txt
note: Pancaratra samhita liturgically tied to Agni Purana material; library gives no precise date, only an early-to-mid medieval Pancaratra horizon. Range kept broad.

title: Īśānaśivagurudeva: Īśānaśivagurudevapaddhati, Volume 3
basis: Flood, gavin-flood; ISP follows the 11th-c. Somaśambhupaddhati
note: Vol 3 of the same paddhati. Consistent with current median.

title: Īśānaśivagurudevapaddhati, Volume 4
basis: Flood, gavin-flood; ISP follows the 11th-c. Somaśambhupaddhati
note: Same work as vols 1-3; single Kerala paddhati of Īśānaśiva, post-Somaśambhu. Consistent with current median.

title: Bhaṭṭāraka Sundara: Īśvarapratyabhijñākaumudī
basis: Torella, ed. Isvarapratyabhijnakarika of Utpaladeva (skt-en-mono)
note: Commentary (kaumudi) on Utpaladeva's Pratyabhijnakarika, written to supply the missing end of Utpaladeva's vrtti. Torella characterizes it as a LATE South-Indian commentary; no precise date given, so broad post-13th c. range.

title: Īśānaśivagurudeva: Īśānaśivagurudevapaddhati, Volume 2
basis: Flood, gavin-flood (bhūtaśuddhi study); ISP follows the 11th-c. Somaśambhupaddhati
note: Kerala Saiddhāntika paddhati; quotes/follows Somaśambhu (1073 CE) so placed after, early-mid 12th c. Consistent with current median.

title: Īśvarapratyabhijñāvimarśinīvyākhyā
basis: Ratié, On the relationship between Buddhist and Śaiva idealisms (citing Sanderson 2007, 422)
source_file: cleaned_Ratié_On the relationship between Buddhist and Śaiva idealisms.txt
note: CHANGED >100y: this is the Bhāskarī, Bhāskarakaṇṭha's late-17th-c. commentary on Abhinavagupta's ĪPV, NOT an 11th-c. work. Potter lists him in the DU (c.18th c.) group. cur_med 1029 far too early.

title: Janmamaraṇavicāra
basis: Potter, Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies vol.1 (bibliographic index); cf. Nemec study (src)
source_file: cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Library gives no precise date; Vāmadeva placed after Abhinavagupta (fl. c.1000) by his Vivṛti on the Paramārthasāra, so a Kashmirian 11th-12th c. author. Consistent with current median; kept broad.

title: Jayākhyasaṃhitā
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Sanderson re-dates this Pāñcarātra saṃhitā LATER than the old 8th-c. (Wallis) or 5th-7th-c. views, as dependent on the mature Śaiva Mantramārga. Midpoint 850 vs 715 -> within 100, not counted as changed.

title: Matsyendranātha: Jñānakārikā
basis: White 2003, Kiss of the Yogini (Yoginikaula/Matsyendra texts); Harper & Brown 2002
source_file: cleaned_Kiss of the Yogini. Tantric Sex in its South Asian Contexts_David Gordon White, 2003.txt
note: Jñānakārikā is a Yoginikaula-school text of Matsyendranatha's circle; library dates the Kaulajñananirnaya/Matsyendra corpus to 9th-10th c. (White), Matsyendra placed early 10th c. (Dan Martin). No dating of Jñānakārikā by title found; inferred from the school. Current median 930 consistent.

title: jñānaratnāvalī
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Saiddhāntika ritual paddhati; Jñānaśiva is teacher of Trilocanaśiva and near-contemporary of Aghoraśiva (fl. 1157). Consistent with current median.

title: Jñānasaṅkalinītantra
basis: no specialist dating found (only mentioned, undated, in Dasgupta 1941)
note: Short Śaiva tantric tract on cakras; Dasgupta cites it but gives no date. Generally treated as a late minor compilation but no specialist date in library; left null.

title: Jñānadīkṣā
basis: no specific dating found in library
note: 'jñānadīkṣā' is a generic Śaiva/Nātha initiation category, not a datable independent tantra; the MB digital-library item is a ritual extract. No consensus date.

title: Jñānasāra
basis: No specialist dating identified; title is ambiguous (Jain Jñānasāra of Yaśovijaya, 17th c.; Buddhist Jñānasārasamuccaya post-6th c.; etc.) and the intended Śaiva/tantric work is unidentified
source_file: cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Title shared by several unrelated works; the specific text behind this item cannot be securely identified or dated from the library. Left null.

title: Jñānasiddhyāgama
consensus_date: Śaiva Siddhānta scripture, pre-12th c. CE (prob. 10th-11th c.)
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Sanderson cites the Jñānasiddhyāgama as a Śaiva Siddhānta scripture among sources of the post-12th-c. Tirumantiram synthesis, implying it predates the 12th c. No precise date; broad 10th-11th c. range, low confidence.

title: Kālānalatantra
basis: no specialist dating located in library
note: No titled Kālānalatantra dating found; only generic occurrences of the word kālānala ('fire of cosmic destruction'). Left null per instructions.

title: Kālahastīśavilāsa (Kālahastīśvaravilāsa)
basis: no dating scholarship found in library
note: Not found in reference-literature/. A South Indian (Vijayanagara/Nāyaka-era) Śaiva court kāvya; no specialist date locatable. Left null per instructions.

title: kālikārahasya
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: No direct scholarly dating of a 'kālikārahasya' in the library. By type a late Bengal Kālī-cult Śākta tantra/rahasya (Kālīkula / Kālīvilāsatantra milieu), generally medieval-to-early-modern, but library has nothing specific; not dated rather than guess. cur_med 936 unsupported.

title: Kāllottara (Kālottara)
basis: Watson 2006 (list of early Saiddhāntika scriptures); Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Early Saiddhāntika scripture, part of the Mantramārga corpus emerging 'from about the fifth century onwards' (Sanderson) and cited by Sadyojyotis (7th-8th c.), so among the oldest tantras; exists in several redactions. Midpoint 650 ~ current 720, not counted as changed.

title: Kāmākhyātantra
basis: no specialist dating of the Kāmākhyātantra located in the library
note: Library references to Kāmākhyā concern the goddess/pīṭha, not this late Śākta tantra. No sourced date found; consensus_date null per instructions. (Generally regarded as a late, post-12th-c. Assamese Śākta tantra, but unverified here.)

title: Kāmadhenutantra
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (2009), on the east-Indian Śākta corpus; Zeiler in Keul ed., Transformations and Transfer of Tantra (2012)
source_file: cleaned_Transformations and Transfer of Tantra in Asia and Beyond_Istvan Keul, ed., 2012_a.txt
note: Grouped with the late east-Indian Śākta/Mahāvidyā tantras (Phetkāriṇī, Jñānasaṅkālinī, Toḍala group). No precise date in library; late medieval Bengal/Assam Śāktism. Heuristic.

title: Puṇyānanda: Kāmakalāvilāsa
basis: Padoux 1990, Vāc (cites KKV as a Śrīvidyā work; Goudriaan-Gupta HTL dating tradition)
source_file: cleaned_Vac. The Concept of the Word in Selected Hindu Tantras_Andre Padoux, 1990.txt
note: Library has no explicit date; Padoux only cites it as a Śrīvidyā work with Naṭanānanda's comm. Standard Śrīvidyā placement ~14th c. retained near current median; not strongly grounded.

title: Kāmaratnatantra
basis: Urban, Power of Tantra (2010); Bharati, Tantric Tradition (1965) - no firm composition date given
source_file: cleaned_Power of Tantra. Religion, Sexuality, and the Politics of South Asian Studies_HUGH B. URBAN, 2010.txt
note: Popular Assamese/Kāmarūpa magical (ṣaṭkarman) compendium, multiply re-authored; no scholarly consensus date in the library. Likely late-medieval, but not datable here; current median (651) is unsupported.

title: Kāmikāgama, Uttarabhāga
consensus_date: pūrvabhāga pre-11th c.; uttarabhāga later (12th c. or after)
basis: Flood 2006, The Tantric Body (Somaśambhupaddhati basis); Brunner-Lachaux
source_file: cleaned_Tantric Body. The Secret Tradition of Hindu Religion_Gavin Flood, 2006.txt
note: South Indian Śaiva Siddhānta Āgama. Pūrvabhāga predates 11th-c. Somaśambhupaddhati; the UTTARABHĀGA is a recognised late supplement, no precise specialist date in library. Bounds reflect later appendix; moved later than current median.

title: Kākacaṇḍeśvarīmata
basis: David Gordon White 2003, Kiss of the Yogini (only a passing mention; no date given)
source_file: cleaned_Kiss of the Yogini. Tantric Sex in its South Asian Contexts_David Gordon White, 2003.txt
note: Library only classifies it among the Kaula/Śākta 'Mata' tantras; no specialist century given, so no consensus date asserted. Generally regarded as a relatively late Kaula text but not datable from sources read.

title: Kālāgniyajñavidhi
basis: none found in library
note: Obscure Śaiva ritual manual (yajñavidhi for Kālāgni[rudra]). No specific dating scholarship located in the library; machine date 800 is unsupported. Not changed (no basis to move it).

title: Kalaśārcanavidhi of Siddhalakṣmīdevī
consensus_date: Newar Malla period, c. 15th–18th c. CE
basis: Sanderson, Śaivism and the Tantric Traditions (Siddhalakṣmī as Malla patron goddess); Gray & Overbey 2016
note: A Newar Sarvāmnāya kalaśa-worship ritual manual for Siddhalakṣmī, the Malla dynasty's patron goddess; a Malla-period (or later) compilation, not an early tantra. The deity derives from the older Jayadrathayāmala but the manual itself is late; far later than the machine median of 709. (A 20th-c. Kathmandu manual of the same title also exists.)

title: Kālīkalpalatā
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: Śākta Kālī ritual digest by Vimarśānandanātha; neither title nor author appears in the library with any dating. No date assigned.

title: Kālikāpurāṇa
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Sanderson infers it postdates Ballālasena's Dānasāgara (1168 CE) since Ballālasena does not list it -> late 12th c. or later. This is later than the conventional 10th-11th c. dating; inference, hence low confidence. Midpoint 1175 vs 598 -> changed.

title: Kālottara
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age; Goodall, A first edition of the Kālajñāna (the shortest Kālottara recension)
note: One of the earliest Saiddhantika scriptures, belonging to the Mantramarga core that emerges from c.5th c.; exists in many redactions (Goodall). Sanderson groups it with the early Niśvāsa-era canon. Midpoint ~600, within ~100y of cur_med 629; not counted as changed.

title: Kāllottara (Kālottara)
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism
note: Early Saiddhāntika scripture (with Niśvāsa among the oldest); demonstrably current by 9th c., quite possibly 8th c. or earlier.

title: Kāmakalāvilāsa
basis: Padoux 1990, Vac (Srividya context); only Avalon-era refs in library
source_file: cleaned_Vac. The Concept of the Word in Selected Hindu Tantras_Andre Padoux, 1990.txt
note: Mature Srividya verse text by Punyananda; library lacks a precise specialist date (only Avalon ed. and Cidvalli commentary refs). Conventional 14th c. retained; consistent with current median.

title: Kāmyakarmakamalā (by Prāṇamañjarī)
note: No scholarship on this title/author found in the library; only generic occurrences of the term kāmyakarma in ritual texts. Cannot date.

title: Kāmikāgama: Pūrvabhāga
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (citing Goodall)
note: Extant Saiddhāntika Kāmika (Pūrvabhāga) first cited mid-12th c. (Jñānaśiva, ~1150s), so the surviving recension is no later than 12th c.; an earlier Kāmika of the same name predates it. Midpoint ~1140 vs 598 -> changed.

title: Kaṅkālamālinītantra
basis: Goudriaan & Gupta 1981 (HTSL) corpus; library has only catalogue listings (Encyclopedia of Tantra 1999; N.N. Bhattacharyya 2006)
note: Late Bengali Sakta/Kaula tantra of the same nibandha milieu as Mahanirvana/Niruttara; no explicit scholarly date in this library, only mentions. Date is corpus-based estimate; cur_med 776 far too early.

title: Karaṇāgama
consensus_date: late South-Indian Saiddhāntika Āgama, c. 12th–13th c. CE (extant recension)
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: South-Indian Saiddhāntika temple Āgama (Karaṇa). First datable citation in the 13th-c. Śivapūjāstavavyākhyā; part of the body of late South-Indian āgamic scripture. Much later than machine median of 705.

title: Karpūrādistotra (Karpūrādi-stotra)
basis: no dating scholarship found in library
note: Library has only references to its commentary (Karpūrādistava-ṭīkā/Prabodhinī of Rāmakiśora) and Woodroffe's edition, no date for the hymn itself. A late-medieval Śākta Kālī hymn; left null per instructions.

title: Kaulāvalīnirṇaya
basis: N.N. Bhattacharyya, History of the Tantric Religion (2006); D.G. White, Kiss of the Yogini (2003), bibl.
source_file: cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: Late Kaula compendium (21 ullāsas) by Jñānānanda Paramahaṃsa, ed. Avalon; cites Yāmalas and earlier tantras. No precise century in library; conventional 16th c. dating; far later than cur_med.

title: Kaulajñānanirṇaya
basis: White 2003, Kiss of the Yogini
source_file: cleaned_Kiss of the Yogini. Tantric Sex in its South Asian Contexts_David Gordon White, 2003.txt
note: Yoginī Kaula scripture attributed to Matsyendra. White explicitly dates it 9th-10th c., coeval with or earlier than core Kaula texts, rejecting Bagchi's mid-11th c. dating. Midpoint 900 vs current 910 = no significant change.

title: Kaulopaniṣad
basis: Bharati 1965, The Tantric Tradition (bibliographical note); commented by Bhāskararāya, 18th c.
note: One of the late Śākta Upaniṣads (printed with Bhāskararāya's 18th-c. commentary); no firm specialist date, treated as a late compilation; current machine median of 1076 is likely too early.

title: Kiñcitatantra
basis: no specialist dating found in reference-literature/
note: No scholarship located for this title (ag found no hits for kincittantra/kiñcittantra). A minor Muktabodha digital-library text; cannot date from this library.

title: Kramadīpikā
basis: no specialist dating of Keśava's Tantric Kramadīpikā in library
source_file: cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 4 - Potter 1987.txt
note: Only Kramadīpikā dated in library is a Sāṃkhya text (re Tattvasamāsa), NOT Keśava Kāśmīrī's Vaiṣṇava/Gauḍīya ritual Kramadīpikā. No source for this work; retain current median (Keśava fl. c.1500).

title: Kriyākramadyotikā
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (confirming Goodall 2000 / 1998 on the date)
note: Aghoraśiva's major Saiddhāntika ritual paddhati; the anchor work whose colophon (Śaka 1079) fixes Aghoraśiva to 1157 CE. Firm date.

title: Nīlakaṇṭha: Kriyāsāra, volume 2
basis: None
source_file: cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: No specialist date in library; only a passing citation. Vīraśaiva Kriyāsāra usually placed 15th-16th c. (Vijayanagara). Retained as low/no-confidence; current median sits at low end.

title: Kriyāsāratattvaprakāśinīvyākhyā
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: Vīraśaiva commentary on Nīlakaṇṭhaśivācārya's Kriyāsāra; appears only in bibliographic indices (no date). Underlying Kriyāsāra is a late-medieval Vīraśaiva text, so a 920 median is implausibly early, but no sourced date available.

title: Kriyākālaguṇottara
basis: no datable passage located in library; cf. Slouber, Early Tantric Medicine (cited in src, not present in library)
note: Only a bibliography entry found in-library (no dating discussion). Specialist (Michael Slouber, Early Tantric Medicine 2017 — the OUP source cited) treats it as a Gāruḍa/snakebite tantra composite, latest layers c. 11th-12th c. Bounds reflect that external consensus; confidence low because not verifiable from a read source here.

title: Kriyākramadyotikāvyākhyā
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Saiva Age (citing Goodall 2000)
note: Saiva Siddhanta. This is the VYAKHYA (commentary) on Aghorasiva's Kriyakramadyotika, a paddhati firmly dated to 1157 (Sanderson/Goodall). The commentary by Kacchapesvarasivacarya necessarily postdates 1157; later S. Indian commentary, so late-12th-13th c. Current median 1221 is consistent.

title: Kriyāsāra (volume 1)
basis: None
source_file: cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: Same Vīraśaiva work (vol.1) as kriyaasaara2; no specialist date in library, only a citation. 15th-16th c. (Vijayanagara) placement; current median at low end.
