title: Kṣurikā Upaniṣad
consensus_date: early Yoga Upaniṣad (around the era of the Maitrī Upaniṣad / didactic Mahābhārata)
basis: Eliade, Yoga: Immortality and Freedom, groups Kṣurikā among the older yogic Upaniṣads composed about the same time as the chief Sannyāsa Upaniṣads and the didactic Mahābhārata
note: A minor Yoga Upaniṣad, not a tantra. Only a broad, older (Eliade) dating available; specialist (Bouy/Mallinson) precision absent in library, hence low confidence. Midpoint ~100 CE vs cur_med 760: moves ~660y earlier (>100).

title: Kubjikātantra
basis: Flood 2006, The Tantric Body; cf. Sanderson 2009, The Saiva Age (relative chronology)
source_file: cleaned_Tantric Body. The Secret Tradition of Hindu Religion_Gavin Flood, 2006.txt
note: Title = the Kubjikāmata(tantra), root scripture of the Paścimāmnāya. Flood: 11th c.; Sanderson places it after Tantrasadbhāva and before Jayadrathayāmala hexad 1 (relative late dating). Likely later 10th-11th c. Much later than current median.

title: Kulacūḍāmaṇi Nigama
basis: Finn 1986 (ed./tr.), reported in Geoffrey Samuel, Origins of Yoga and Tantra (2008/2010)
source_file: cleaned_Origins of Yoga and Tantra. Indic Religions to the Thirteenth Century_Geoffrey Samuel, 2008 (2010).txt
note: Early Śākta/Kaula tantra; Finn's 9th-11th range, earlier end favored. Current median 938 lies inside range.

title: kuladīpikā
basis: no dating found; attribution is to the legendary Trika lineage-founder
note: Attributed to Tryambaka(-bhaṭṭāraka), the legendary founder of the Trika/Pratyabhijñā lineage, hence pseudepigraphic. Library 'Kuladīpikā' hits (Harischandra-kuladīpikā, Sampradāyakuladīpikā) are unrelated works. No consensus date.

title: Kulālaśāstra
basis: no mention found in library
note: No occurrence of Kulālaśāstra in reference-literature/. Cannot date; consensus_date null.

title: Kulamuktikallolinī
basis: A. Bharati, Tantric Tradition (1965) on Navasiṃha/Ādyānanda
note: Same author as Tantracintāmaṇi; minister under Kathmandu Malla kings Bhūpālendra (r. ~1687-1700) and Bhāskara Malla (r. ~1700-1714), so fl. late 17th-early 18th c.

title: Kulānandādi
basis: Urban 2010; Mallinson & Singleton 2017 (Kaula corpus of Matsyendranatha, c. 10th-11th c.)
source_file: cleaned_Power of Tantra. Religion, Sexuality, and the Politics of South Asian Studies_HUGH B. URBAN, 2010.txt
note: Kaula text attributed to Matsyendranatha (shivashakti.com). No dating of this specific title; aligned with the Matsyendra/Kaulajnananirnaya corpus dated 10th-11th c. The attributed text itself may be later.

title: Kulārṇavatantra
basis: Bühnemann, in Goudriaan (ed.) Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism (Padoux Festschrift), 1992
source_file: cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism. Studies in Honor of Andre Padoux_Teun Goudriaan, ed, 1992.txt
note: Far later than current median 803; specialist range is high medieval Kaula (11th-15th c.).

title: Kulasāra
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age / History through textual criticism (Vidyāpīṭha-Kaula corpus); discussed by Törzsök
note: Early Śaiva Kaula / Vidyāpīṭha scripture (Nepalese mss, transcribed by Vasudeva), cited by Sanderson and discussed by Törzsök; no explicit date statement, but belongs to the early Kaula corpus Sanderson places c. 9th-10th c. Midpoint ~900 vs 732.

title: Laghustava
basis: no dating statement found in library
note: Sakta Kundalini hymn of the Kashmiri Pancastavi pentad (NOT the Buddhist Laghustava of Sarvajnamitra). Library has only catalogue mentions (Potter, Bharati), no date; left null.

title: Laghustuti, with commentary by Rāghavānanda
basis: no specialist dating found; only Bendall catalogue listing (Devīpañcastavī)
source_file: cleaned_Bendall_Catalogue of Sanskrit, Pali, and Prakrit books in the British museum acquired during the years 1876-92.txt
note: Śākta hymn, one of the Devīpañcastavī, with Rāghavānanda's commentary. No specialist date in library; the commentary is later than the hymn. Confidence none.

title: Lakṣmītantra
basis: Sanderson (reported & endorsed in the Spanda/Bhagavadutpala study)
note: Pāñcarātra saṃhitā; Sanderson redates much later than older catalogues. Earlier scholarship (Gupta) put it ~9th-10th c.; specialist consensus now 12th-13th c. S. India.

title: Lakṣmītantra
basis: Sanderson (reported), via skt-en-mono; cf. older Olson 'ninth-twelfth c.'
note: Pāñcarātra Śākta saṃhita. Sanderson re-dates much LATER (12th-13th c. South India) on liturgical/Yajurveda-recension grounds, vs older 9th-12th c. (Olson). Adopt later specialist view; midpoint 1200 shifts >300y from cur_med 885 -> changed.

title: Mṛtyuñjayakalpa
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Saiva Age (Amrtesvarabhairava = Netratantra cult); Mallinson & Singleton 2017, Roots of Yoga (Netratantra 800-850 CE)
note: Mrtyunjaya = Amrtesvara/Netranatha; a kalpa (ritual manual) of the Mrtyunjaya/Amrtesa cult tied to the Netratantra (c.800-850). The kalpa is likely contemporary-with or later than the Netra, hence 9th-10th c. No standalone date for the kalpa itself; inferred from cult.

title: māheśvaratantra
basis: no dating scholarship located
note: No specialist dating found in library; only incidental mentions of the deity 'Maheśvara' (e.g. Mirror of Beryl) and catalogue listings, not the tantra. Likely a late/minor compilation. Date left null per instructions.

title: Madhyapīṭhapūjāvidhi
basis: no specialist dating located in reference-literature/
note: Nepalese Sarvāmnāya/Siddhilakṣmī ritual digest (pūjāvidhi); no dating in library. Such Nepalese Sarvāmnāya compilations are late-medieval, but no sourced consensus found.

title: mahāguhyakālīvidhānam
basis: Sanderson, Śaivism and the Tantric Traditions (cult of Guhyakālī)
note: Guhyakālī ritual manual (vidhāna), drawing on the Mata/Krama and Mahākālasaṃhitā traditions; Sanderson dates the iconic Guhyakālī cult from at least the 10th c. Specific vidhāna undated; date assigned to the cult. Current median 700 too early.

title: mahālakṣmīdīpadānavidhiḥ
basis: no dating scholarship located
note: Minor lamp-offering ritual (dīpadāna) vidhi, likely from a late Pāñcarātra/saṃhitā or paddhati context (MB collection). No scholarly dating found in library. Left null.

title: Mahālakṣmīpūjāvidhiḥ
basis: no specialist dating located in library
note: Anonymous pūjā-vidhi ritual manual (Mahālakṣmī/Siddhalakṣmī worship); no datable scholarship in library. Likely a late medieval/modern paddhati, not a primary tantra; date undeterminable here.

title: Mahānirvāṇa Tantra
basis: Flood 2006, The Tantric Body (citing J.D.M. Derrett)
source_file: cleaned_Tantric Body. The Secret Tradition of Hindu Religion_Gavin Flood, 2006.txt
note: Major correction: not an early medieval tantra but an 18th-c. Bengali composition (Derrett's 'juridical hoax'), popularized via Avalon's translation. Midpoint 1765 vs current 764 -> changed.

title: Mahāvākya Upaniṣad
basis: White, Yoga in Practice (2012) on the Yoga/minor Upaniṣads; cf. Bouy 1994 (cited in Mallinson & Singleton, Roots of Yoga 2017)
note: Non-tantric: a short late/minor (Atharvaveda-affiliated) Upaniṣad, part of the medieval minor/Yoga Upaniṣad corpus. Current median (649) is far too early; consensus is firmly medieval. Midpoint ~1050, >100y from 649 -> changed.

title: Mahānayaprakāśa (anonymous)
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (late-phase Krama/Mahānaya exegesis)
note: Late Krama exegetical work; the several Mahānayaprakāśas (Śitikaṇṭha, Arṇasiṃha-milieu) belong to the post-Abhinava/Kṣemarāja exegesis, 12th–13th c. Library gives no precise date for this specific text; current median ~945 is too early by ~2–3 c.

title: Śitikaṇṭha: Mahānayaprakāśa
basis: Sanderson, bibliographies in The Saiva Age & Essais sur le rituel (Old Kashmiri MNP; post-Jayaratha)
note: Distinct from the anonymous Sanskrit Mahanayaprakasa (which 'knew Jayaratha in the 13th c.'). Sitikantha's is Old-Kashmiri-language = later (14th-15th c.). No precise specialist date in library; low confidence. Midpoint ~1400.

title: Maheśvarānanda: Mahārthamañjarī
basis: Tuske ed. 2017, Indian Epistemology and Metaphysics; cf. Padoux 1990 (12th-13th c.)
source_file: cleaned_Indian Epistemology and Metaphysics-Bloomsbury Academic_Joerg Tuske, ed , 2017.txt
note: South-Indian Krama (Mahartha) work with autocommentary Parimala. Multiple library sources give 14th c.; Padoux gives 12th-13th c. Consistent with current median.

title: Mahotsavavidhikrama (Mahotsavavidhi)
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (Aghoraśiva completed Kriyākramadyotikā 1157); cf. Davis & Cox, A Priest's Guide for the Great Festival
note: Work of the South Indian Saiddhāntika Aghoraśiva, firmly dated by his Kriyākramadyotikā colophon of Śaka 1079 = 1157 CE. Festival-ritual manual.

title: Makuṭāgama
consensus_date: late South Indian Siddhānta āgama; first attested 16th c. CE
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Saiva Age (n. 662)
note: Sanderson: the extant Makuṭa is among Saiddhāntika āgamas first cited only by Vedajñānaguru II (16th c., Cidambaram), unlike the earlier core cited 12th-13th c. So the recension is late (post-12th, attested 16th c.). Far later than current median; range left wide pending firmer date.

title: Makuṭāgama (Vīraśaiva recension)
basis: no specialist dating found in reference-literature/
note: One of the 28 Śaiva Siddhānta Āgamas (mūla-/upabheda group); this Vīraśaiva recension is a late re-redaction. Library has no datable scholarship; Saiddhāntika Āgamas span c. 7th–11th c. but this recension likely later. Not changing on no evidence.

title: Mālinīvijayavārttika
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (2009); Padoux festschrift (Goudriaan ed., 1992) confirms attribution to Abhinavagupta
source_file: cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism. Studies in Honor of André Padoux_Teun Goudriaan, ed, 1992.txt
note: Abhinavagupta fl. c. 975-1025 (Sanderson). Commentarial vārttika on the Mālinīvijayottara; one of his Trika works.

title: Mālinīvijayottaratantra
basis: Trika scholarship per Sanderson; root-scripture of Abhinavagupta (fl. c. 975-1025) Trika exegesis
source_file: cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism. Studies in Honor of André Padoux_Teun Goudriaan, ed, 1992.txt
note: Base Trika tantra (Mālinīvijayavārttika of Abhinavagupta), so predates c. 1000; specialists place it 8th-9th c. Library lacks an explicit verbatim century-date, hence medium.

title: Maṇḍalabrāhmaṇa Upaniṣad
basis: no dating in library; only a catalog of commentaries (Karl Potter bibliography)
source_file: cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: One of the minor Yoga Upaniṣads (conventionally late, ~13th-15th c.) but no source read assigns a date. Left null. Not a tantra proper.

title: Mahādānavidhiḥ
basis: no specialist dating of this specific paddhati found in library
note: Ritual manual for the (ṣoḍaśa)mahādāna 'great gifts'. The mahādāna institution is Puranic (Matsya/Linga/Agni Purāṇa, ~6th-11th c.), but this particular anonymous vidhi paddhati is undated in the library; likely a late Nepalese/Newar ritual compilation.

title: Mantrakaumudī
basis: no occurrence of the Tantric Mantrakaumudī in library
note: No hits for 'Mantrakaumudī' in the library; author name 'Devanātha' appears only as a logician elsewhere. No source; retain current median (Mithilā, early 16th c.).

title: Mantrakoṣa
basis: none found in library
note: Mantra-compendium attributed to a named (likely late-medieval/modern) author Āśāditya Tripāṭhī. No specialist dating in the library; the named author suggests a far later (possibly 16th-20th c.) compilation, but no source read to confirm. No supported basis to set bounds.

title: Matatrayaikyaprakāśikā
basis: no specialist dating located in library
note: Late Śrīvidyā/Śaiva harmonising treatise; no dating discussion found in reference-literature/ (only catalogue/lexical hits). Date undetermined here.

title: Mātṛkācakraviveka
basis: Bhattacharyya 2006 (mentions work and its commentator Śivānanda but gives no date)
source_file: cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: Kashmiri Śrīvidyā philosophical work in 5 parts; library names it and its commentator Śivānanda but supplies NO explicit date. Its Śrīvidyā milieu (Puṇyānanda/Amṛtānanda lineage, late 13th-14th c.) suggests c.13th-14th c., but no source read states this; treated as undetermined/low.

title: Merutantra
basis: Bharati 1965, The Tantric Tradition; N.N. Bhattacharyya, History of the Tantric Religion
note: Reckoned among 'the most recent Tantras'; mentions the city of London, so post-contact (17th-18th c.). Drastically later than machine median 619.

title: Mohacūḍottara (Mohacūḍottaratantra)
consensus_date: before mid-12th c. CE (older Siddhānta layer)
basis: Goodall (Parākhyatantra intro) / Sanderson; commented by Aghoraśiva (fl. 1157)
note: Śaiva Siddhānta pratiṣṭhātantra (=Mohaśūrottara), preserved in early Newari palm-leaf MSS; commented by Aghoraśiva (1157), so an 'old' Siddhānta predating mid-12th c. Broad bounds reflect uncertainty within early-Siddhānta layer.

title: Sadyojyoti: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa — Mokṣakārikā
basis: Watson 2006 (Self's Awareness of Itself), 'The Date of Sadyojyotis', drawing on Sanderson
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Saiddhāntika philosophical work of the Aṣṭaprakaraṇa attributed to Sadyojyotis; date follows the author. Consistent with current median.

title: Mokṣasopāna
basis: none found in library
note: No dating scholarship located in reference-literature/ (only Indology-list / Muktabodha MS references in source URLs). Left undated.

title: Mṛgendrāgama
consensus_date: c. 8th c. CE (one of the latest of the early Saiddhantika scriptures)
basis: Watson 2006 (citing Goodall 1998, 2002), Self's Awareness of Itself; Sanderson on early Siddhanta
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Saiddhantika agama; among latest of the EARLY scriptures, shows knowledge of vivartavada; pre-dates/contemporary with Sadyojyotis (7th-8th c.). Dated ~8th c., far earlier than current median 963.

title: Aghoraśivācārya: Mṛgendravṛttidīpikā
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (Aghoraśiva completed Kriyākramadyotikā 1157); ed. Kṛṣṇaśāstrin & Subrahmanyaśāstrin 1928
note: Aghoraśiva's subcommentary (dīpikā) on Nārāyaṇakaṇṭha's Mṛgendravṛtti. Dated by Aghoraśiva's secure floruit of 1157 CE. A commentary, not a primary tantra.

title: Mṛgendrapaddhatiṭīkā
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (Aghoraśiva's dated floruit)
note: Sub-commentary on the Mṛgendrapaddhati attributed to the Saiddhāntika Aghoraśiva (fl. 1157); the ṭīkā necessarily post-dates it. NOT the Mṛgendrāgama itself. Current median ~946 far too early.

title: Nādabindu Upaniṣad
basis: J. C. Ruff in D. G. White (ed.), Yoga in Practice, 2012
note: Yoga Upaniṣad; Ruff dates earliest of this collection to 9th-13th c. Nādabindu is among the principal/early ones (also Eliade). Range brackets median 872.

title: Rāmakaṇṭha: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa — Nādakārikā
basis: Watson 2006 (Self's Awareness of Itself), following Goodall 1998 and Sanderson
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Nādakārikā is one of Rāmakaṇṭha II's eight authentic prakaraṇas; Kashmiri Saiddhāntika exegete fl. c. 950-1000. Author-work, not a tantra; date confirmed.

title: Sadyojyoti: Nareśvaraparīkṣā
basis: Watson, The Self's Awareness of Itself (2006), §6 'The Date of Sadyojyotis'
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Nareśvaraparīkṣā of Sadyojyoti (commented on by Rāmakaṇṭha, fl. c. 950-1000). Confirms current median (702) and bounds (675-725).

title: Nāṭyaśāstra, Vol. 1
basis: Larsen 2009 ('about 2000 years ago'); general consensus c.200 BCE-200 CE
source_file: cleaned_Buddhism in Popular Culture. The Case of Sri Lankan 'Tovil dance'_Hege Myrlund Larsen, 2009.txt
note: Non-tantric. Same as Vol.3; standard consensus core range c.200 BCE-200 CE. Differs from current median by >100y.

title: Nāṭyaśāstra, Vol. 3
basis: Deutsch & Bontekoe 1999, Companion to World Philosophies (Bharata 4th-5th c. view); general consensus c.200 BCE-200 CE
source_file: cleaned_Companion to World Philosophies_Eliot Deutsch, Ron Bontekoe, 1999.txt
note: Non-tantric dramaturgical treatise. Library datings vary (one says ~2000 yrs ago, another 4th-5th c. CE). Used standard consensus core range c.200 BCE-200 CE; changes vs current median ~916 by >100y.

title: Nāṭyaśāstra, Vol. 2
consensus_date: 4th-5th c. CE (received text; composite, core older)
basis: Companion to World Philosophies (Deutsch & Bontekoe eds., 1999)
source_file: cleaned_Companion to World Philosophies_Eliot Deutsch, Ron Bontekoe, 1999.txt
note: Not a tantra: dramaturgy/aesthetics treatise (rasa theory), composite. Library general-reference dates received text 4th-5th c. CE; core layers often put earlier (c.200 BCE-200 CE). Shift earlier from median 883. No tantra-specialist source applies.

title: Netratantra
basis: Mallinson & Singleton, Roots of Yoga (2017), following Sanderson's dedicated study
source_file: cleaned_Roots of Yoga_James Mallinson, Mark Singleton, 2017.txt
note: Amṛteśvara/Mṛtyujit Bhairava tantra, Kashmir; Sanderson dates to early 9th c. ~150y earlier than current median 979 — CHANGED.

title: Niruttaravāda
basis: Encyclopedia of Tantra (Niruttara-Tantra grouped with late Bengali Sakta tantras Yoni/Kumari-Tantra)
note: Excerpt/teaching from the Niruttara-Tantra, a late Bengali Kaula/Sakta tantra grouped with the Yoni- and Kumari-Tantras (generally 16th-18th c.). No precise specialist date; low confidence.

title: Nirvāṇatantra
basis: N.N. Bhattacharyya 2006 cites it only as a source in the late Sakta/Kaula nibandha corpus; no independent dating in library
source_file: cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: Late Bengali Sakta/Kaula tantra of the Mahanirvana/Niruttara group; no explicit scholarly date in library. Corpus-based estimate; cur_med 784 far too early.

title: Nirvāṇayogottara
note: No specific specialist dating of the Nirvāṇayogottara/Nirvāṇatantra found in the library; only encyclopedic mentions without a date. Left undated rather than invent one.

title: Niśvāsakārikā
consensus_date: 7th-8th c. CE (latest, fifth Sūtra of the Niśvāsa corpus)
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism and The Śaiva Age: the Niśvāsa Mūlasūtra is c.450-550 and the Niśvāsakārikā is the fifth/latest Sūtra of the corpus, appended to the earlier strata
note: Latest layer of the early Saiddhāntika Niśvāsa corpus (whole corpus c.450-9th c.); Mūlasūtra 450-550 but the Kārikā is a later addition, so 7th-8th c. cur_med 762 within range; midpoint ~700, change <100y.

title: Niśvāsakārikā
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age; cf. Goodall et al., Niśvāsatattvasaṃhitā ed. (EFEO/Pondicherry)
note: A LATER stratum of the Niśvāsa corpus, extant only in southern (Pondicherry transcript) mss and absent from the early Nepalese codex (per White, Sinister Yogis). The corpus core is mid-5th-6th c. (Mūlasūtra), but the Kārikā is a secondary expansion, hence later (7th-9th c.).

title: Nityāṣoḍaśikārṇavatantra with the commentary called Setubandha
consensus_date: early 18th c. CE (commentary); mūla tantra 10th-12th c. CE
basis: Padoux 1990, Vāc (on the Vāmakeśvarīmata/NŚA and Bhāskararāya's commentary); Bhattacharyya 2006
source_file: cleaned_Vac. The Concept of the Word in Selected Hindu Tantras_Andre Padoux, 1990.txt
note: Dated as the commentarial WORK (Setubandha by Bhāskararāya, fl. first half 18th c.). The mūla Nityāṣoḍaśikārṇava (=Vāmakeśvarīmata), root scripture of the Tripurasundarī/Śrīvidyā cult, is itself 10th-12th c. (Padoux: 'latest of the Kaula transmissions, tenth-twelfth century?'). Midpoint 1725 vs median 1123 -> changed.

title: Pāśupatabrahma Upaniṣad
basis: Eliade 1958, Yoga: Immortality and Freedom (classed among late yogic Upaniṣads)
note: Not a tantra; a late Yoga Upaniṣad of the syncretic corpus, dated by Eliade among the latest such texts. Current median 965 likely too early; pushed later.

title: Vedāntadeśika: Pāñcarātrarakṣā
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (cites the work by the well-dated Srivaisnava author Vedantadesika)
note: Authored by the historically well-dated Vedantadesika (1268-1369); a Srivaisnava defence of Pancaratra, not itself a samhita. Current median accurate.

title: Pārānandasūtra
basis: N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006, History of the Tantric Religion
source_file: cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: Only Bhattacharyya, who calls it 'an earlier Tantric work' (Kaula, pañca-makāra) without a century. No specialist date; current range 850-1000 retained, low confidence.

title: Paratantra (Parātantra)
basis: no specialist dating located in library
note: No specific dating found. Library hits for 'paratantra' are the Yogacara technical term (paratantra-svabhava) or Svatantrika/Madhyamaka usage, not a datable tantric work; could not confirm identity as a specific Pancaratra/Saiva text. Date left null to avoid invention.

title: Parātrīśikālaghuvṛtti
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Abhinavagupta's shorter commentary on the Parātrīśikā (Trika), within his floruit c. 975-1025; catalogue 'unknown author' is incorrect.

title: Somānanda II: Parātriśikātātparyadīpikā and Śāktavijñāna
consensus_date: c. 900-950 CE (Somānanda floruit; work itself undated)
basis: Sanderson, Śaivism and the Tantric Traditions (floruit of Somānanda)
note: Library dates the Pratyabhijñā founder Somānanda to fl. c.900-50; no specific dating found for this Parātriśikā commentary, and title labels author 'Somānanda II' (possibly later). Low confidence; current median retained.

title: parākramapūjā
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: No match for parākramapūjā in reference-literature/ (all 'parākrama' hits are the common word 'valor'). Obscure Kashmir Trika ritual text; no sourced date available, so consensus left null.

title: Abhinavagupta: Paramārthasāra
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (2009)
note: Abhinavagupta's reworking of the Ādhārakārikā; dated by his floruit.

title: Parameśvarīmatatantra
basis: Flood, The Tantric Body (2006), on the Kubjikā / Western Transmission corpus
source_file: cleaned_Tantric Body. The Secret Tradition of Hindu Religion_Gavin Flood, 2006.txt
note: Parameśvarī(mata)tantra is a Kubjikā / Western-Transmission (Paścimāmnāya) scripture; no dating of this exact title in library. Dated by association with the Kubjikā corpus (~10th-11th c. per Flood); later than machine median 790, but inference only.

title: Sadyojyoti: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa — Paramokṣanirāsakārikā
basis: Watson 2006 (Self's Awareness of Itself), 'The Date of Sadyojyotis'; Goodall & Watson ed. of the work
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Kārikā by Sadyojyoti (the vṛtti is by Bhaṭṭa Rāmakaṇṭha, later); date follows the author. Consistent with current median.

title: Paraśambhumahimnastava (Paraśambhumahimnaḥstava)
basis: Sanderson, Śaivism and the Tantric Traditions (Krama enters history early/mid 9th c.)
note: Krama stotra (Krodhabhaṭṭāraka/Durvasas epithet); no dating of this specific hymn in library, bracketed by the Kashmiri Krama's flourishing 9th-11th c.

title: Paraśurāmakalpasūtra
basis: no dating statement found in library
note: Late Srividya ritual sutra; library mentions it only in citation-lists (Freschi/Maas, Bhattacharyya, Encyclopedia of Tantra) with no date. Specialist consensus (Padoux ed., not present here) places it late (~15th-16th c.) but not citable from this library; left null.

title: Parātrīśikāvivṛti (Parātrīśikā-laghuvṛtti)
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (Abhinavagupta's own dated colophons)
note: Abhinavagupta's works dated by his own colophons span Saptarṣisaṃvat 4066 (990/91) to 4090 (1014/15). Dating unchanged.

title: Pauṣkarasaṃhitā
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Pāñcarātra Pauṣkara among the seven 'relatively old' saṃhitās but the latest; the Jayākhya/Sātvata group 'very unlikely to have been produced before the ninth century', Pauṣkara later still. Distinct from the Śaiva Pauṣkara-Pārameśvara. Later than median 755.

title: Jñānaprakāśācārya: Pauṣkaravṛtti
basis: Goodall, Bhaṭṭa Rāmakaṇṭha's Kiraṇavṛtti edition (treats Jñānaprakāśa as a late Saiddhāntika commentator); no explicit century stated
note: Late Śaiva Siddhānta commentator of Śālivāṭipura, Jaffna; library cites him but gives no firm date. Conventionally 16th c. (after Umāpati, 14th c.). Commentary on Pauṣkarāgama. Retained current median.

title: Pheṭakapratiṣṭhācaṇḍikāgnividhi
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: Anonymous Newar consecration/fire-rite (pratiṣṭhā + Caṇḍikā agnividhi) paddhati. Library matches for 'pheṭaka' are MS colophons describing 'khaḍga-pheṭaka' (sword-and-shield) iconography, not this text's date. Late medieval Newar ritual manual; undated in library.

title: Phetkāriṇītantra
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (citing Bühnemann 1996)
note: Terminus post quem set by borrowing from Śāśvatavajra (fl. c.1000); Sanderson thinks composed closer to his time than to its 15th-c. citation. Much later than current 715 -> CHANGED.

title: Piṅgalāmata
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Saiddhāntika Pratiṣṭhātantra; one of the early texts of its class, attested before end of 11th c. No tighter date in library. Midpoint 850 vs 596 -> changed.

title: Prāṇāgnihotra (Prāṇāgnihotra Upaniṣad)
basis: no dating statement found in library (Olivelle 1998 cites only Bock-Raming's study of agnihotra/prāṇāgnihotra, not a date)
source_file: cleaned_Early Upanisads. Annotated Text and Translation_Patrick Olivelle, 1998.txt
note: Minor late/sectarian Upaniṣad; library yields only a bibliographic reference, no date. Not in Olivelle's set of early Upaniṣads. No consensus date assigned.

title: Rāmatoṣaṇa (compiler): Prāṇatoṣiṇī, part 6
basis: Santideva, Encyclopedia of Tantra 1999; Benard, Chinnamasta 2010 (Prāṇatoṣiṇītantra, Calcutta 1928 ed.)
note: Modern Bengali nibandha (Tantric digest) compiled by Rāmatoṣaṇa Bhaṭṭācārya c.1820 CE; not an early tantra. Library cites it only as a late compilation. Current date confirmed.

title: Pratiṣṭhālakṣaṇasārasamuccaya
basis: Goodall (Niśvāsatattvasaṃhitā ed.) cites it as a compilatory source; no explicit date given
note: A compilatory pratiṣṭhā/iconography compendium that borrows from earlier Śaiva scriptures (Bṛhatkālottara etc.); thus a relatively late digest, but the library offers no firm specialist date. Left null; treat as post-9th c. at best (low confidence).

title: Pratyabhijñāhṛdaya (text version)
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (Abhinavagupta fl. c. 975–1025); Kṣemarāja his direct pupil
note: Kṣemarāja was the direct disciple of Abhinavagupta (fl. 975–1025), so his own Pratyabhijñāhṛdaya dates to the first half of the 11th c.; current median 1080 is slightly late.

title: Prāyaścittasamuccaya
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age 2009 (Trilocanaśiva 12th c.); Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (Hṛdayaśiva's PSS, MS dated NS 278 = A.D. 1158)
note: Saiddhāntika Śaiva expiation digest. Trilocanaśiva is 12th c. (teacher Jñānaśiva near-contemporary of Aghoraśiva who finished his work in 1157). The homonymous earlier Prāyaścittasamuccaya by Hṛdayaśiva (Mālava/Dhārā lineage) is late 10th-mid 11th c., extant MS dated 1158. Later than current median 660.

title: Prodgītāgama (Prodgīta)
note: Listed only as a name in Śaiva Siddhānta āgama/upāgama catalogues (skt-en-mono.txt index); no specialist dating discussion found in the library.

title: Puraścaryārṇava, volume 2
basis: authorial attribution (Pratāp Siṃha Śāh, King of Nepal r. 1775-1777)
note: Not a revealed tantra but a late-18th-c. ritual digest (nibandha) compiled by/for King Pratāp Siṃha Śāh of Nepal (r. 1775-1777). Firmly datable to c. 1775 by authorship; no separate library citation needed. Far later than machine median 735.

title: Puraścaryārṇava, volume 3
consensus_date: late 18th c. CE (compiled under King Pratāpasiṃha Śāha, r. 1775-77)
basis: Stated authorship/patronage; King Pratap Singh of Nepal (reigned 1775-1777), referenced in Santideva Encyclopedia of Tantra and Macdonald, Mandala and Landscape
note: Late Nepalese tantric ritual digest (nibandha) compiled by/under the Shah king, not an early tantra. cur_med 759 off by ~1000y; midpoint moves +1021y.

title: Puraścaryārṇava, volume 1
basis: Authorship by King Pratāpasiṃha Śāha of Nepal (r. 1775-77), per item metadata; corroborated as a late Tantric nibandha/digest
note: An 18th c. Nepalese royal Tantric digest (nibandha), NOT an early scripture; composed under King Pratāpasiṃha Śāha c. 1775. Library has no specialist dating but authorship is firm. Far later than cur_med 836 -> changed.

title: Śrīkaṇṭha: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa — Ratnatrayaparīkṣā
basis: Lit. of Philosophical Systems catalogue (Ratnatrayaparīkṣā a Śaiva Siddhānta work commented by Aghoraśiva, fl.1157, and Rāmakaṇṭha)
source_file: cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Śaiva-Siddhānta theologian Śrīkaṇṭha, post-Sadyojyoti and pre-Aghoraśiva(1157); library lacks a clean specialist (Goodall) century; retains ~10th c. The 12th-c. Śrīkaṇṭha in Potter vol.2 is a different (Vedānta/logic) author

title: Sādhanamālā, Vol. 2
basis: Bhattacharyya 1958, Indian Buddhist Iconography (Based on the Sādhanamālā)
source_file: cleaned_Indian Buddhist Iconography…Based on Sadhanamala_Benoytosh Bhattacharyya, 1958.txt
note: Anthology of sādhanas by many authors; individual sādhanas range earlier (cur_nb 600-na 1100), but the COMPILATION is fixed by the 1165 ms. Midpoint shifts later -> changed.

title: Śāktānandataraṅgiṇī
basis: Bühnemann 2003, Mandalas and Yantras in the Hindu Traditions
source_file: cleaned_Mandalas and Yantras in the Hindu Traditions_Gudrun Buhnemann 2003.txt
note: Śākta nibandha. Bühnemann explicitly calls it 16th-century; consistent with cur_med.

title: Devanandanasiṃha: Śāktapramoda
basis: External: Deva Nandan Singh's compilation, first published c.1890 (cf. Madhu Khanna ed.); library mentions author only in Benard 2010 index
source_file: cleaned_Chinnamasta The Aweful Buddhist & Hindu Tantric Goddess_Elisabeth Anne Benard  2010.txt
note: Late-19th-c. Śākta nibandha (collection of Tantras) by Deva Nandan Singh, first published c.1890; current median 1835 likely slightly early. Modern compilation, not an early tantra.

title: Śāṇḍilya Upaniṣad
basis: Ruff in White (ed.), Yoga in Practice 2012; Mallinson & Singleton, Roots of Yoga 2017 (citing Bouy 1994)
note: One of the 20-21 Yoga Upaniṣads; on their late composition see Bouy 1994. Śāṇḍilya is a developed aṣṭāṅga/nāḍī text, so toward the later end of the 9th-13th c. range; pushed later than cur_med.

title: Sādhanamālā, Vol. 1
consensus_date: compilation c. 7th-12th c. CE (collection closed by mid-12th c.)
basis: Williams/Tribe/Wynne 2000, Buddhist Thought; Nakamura 1980
source_file: cleaned_Buddhist Thought. A Complete Introduction to the Indian Tradition_Paul Williams, Anthony J. Tribe, Alexander Wynne, 2000.txt
note: Anthology of ~240 sādhanas by many authors spanning 7th-12th c.; earliest dated MS 1165 CE marks the closing of the collection. Range retained, not a single-date work.

title: Śaivāgamādimāhātmya (Śivāgamādimāhātmyasaṃgraha)
basis: skt-en-mono (Goodall, Kiranavrtti ed.) on Jnanaprakasa of Jaffna; OCHS lecture (src) groups him with later Saiva Siddhanta commentators
note: Jnanaprakasa of Salivati (Jaffna) is a late Saiva Siddhanta author, grouped with Umapati and later commentators; library gives no explicit year. Conventionally 16th c.; confidence low as no firm date is citable from this library.

title: Śaivakalāviveka
note: Only a catalogue mention of a Śaivakalāvivekavyākhyā (skt-en-mono.txt index); no dating statement found in the library.

title: Śaivasiddhāntaparibhāṣā
basis: R.H. Davis (ed.) 1998, Images, Miracles, and Authority; cf. catalogue 'Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy'
source_file: cleaned_Images, Miracles, and Authority in Asian Religious Traditions_Richard H. Davis, ed., 1998.txt
note: Saiva Siddhanta scholastic, not a tantra; library confirms author/work and lineage but gives no explicit century. Standard scholarship places Sivagrayogin in the 16th c. (post-Meykanda commentator); kept at machine median.

title: Śaivasiddhāntaparibhāṣāmañjarī
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (Vedajñānaguru II); cf. Dupuche/IFP critical edn of the Saivāgamaparibhāṣāmañjarī
note: Śaiva Siddhānta digest; also cited as Saivāgamaparibhāṣāmañjarī. By Nigamajñānadeśika (Vedajñāna II), Cidambaram. Sanderson dates his oeuvre to the latter half of the 16th c., slightly later than cur_med.

title: Sakalāgamasārasaṅgraha
basis: Only catalogue notices found (Bendall; Querying the Medieval handlist); no specialist composition date
source_file: cleaned_Bendall_Catalogue of Sanskrit, Pali, and Prakrit books in the British museum acquired during the years 1876-92.txt
note: A late South-Indian digest/epitome of the Śaiva Āgamas (a saṅgraha, not a primary scripture); appears in South-Indian Saiddhāntika handlists alongside Aghoraśiva-era works, suggesting it post-dates the 12th c., but no firm scholarly date in library. Left null.

title: Śaktisaṃgamatantra, Kālīkhaṇḍa
basis: N.N. Bhattacharyya 2006, History of the Tantric Religion
source_file: cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: Late Śākta tantra (4 khaṇḍas: Kālī, Tārā, Sundarī, Chinnamastā), ed. B. Bhattacharyya. Dated 16th-17th c. - drastically later than current median (~682). Major change.

title: Śaktisaṃgamatantra, Sundarīkhaṇḍa
basis: Kinsley 1998 (Ten Mahavidyas) treats it as a developed Mahavidya source; standard view (Goudriaan-Gupta 1981, Bhattacharyya) is that it is among the latest Tantras
source_file: cleaned_Tantric Visions of the Divine Feminine. The Ten Mahavidyas_DAVID KINSLEY, 1998.txt
note: Presupposes the fully systematized ten-Mahavidya scheme, a late development; conventionally one of the latest large Tantras (~16th-17th c.). No explicit dated statement in library; cur_med 777 far too early.

title: Śaktisaṃgamatantra: Tārākhaṇḍa
basis: N. N. Bhattacharyya, History of the Tantric Religion (2006)
source_file: cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: Tārākhaṇḍa is the Tārā part of the four-part Śaktisaṃgamatantra. Bhattacharyya dates the whole work 16th-17th c.; this late date (mid ~1600) is ~800y later than the machine median 788.

title: Śaktisūtra
basis: skt-en-mono (Kashmiri Śaiva monograph): Bhāskararāya equates 'Śaktisūtra' with Kṣemarāja's Pratyabhijñāhṛdaya; Gnoli's separate 'Śaktisūtra' attribution judged probably mistaken
note: Title is ambiguous in the scholarship: 'Śaktisūtra' is Bhāskararāya's name for Kṣemarāja's Pratyabhijñāhṛdaya (early 11th c.); a distinct work of that name is doubted as a scholarly error. Cannot pin the MB-collection text to one identity, so no firm bounds given.

title: Saṃnyāsa Upaniṣads
basis: Olivelle 1992, Saṃnyāsa Upaniṣads (cited as standard authority); Samuel 2008
source_file: cleaned_Origins of Yoga and Tantra. Indic Religions to the Thirteenth Century_Geoffrey Samuel, 2008 (2010).txt
note: This is a CORPUS of ~20 renunciant Upaniṣads spanning many centuries, not a single text; Olivelle 1992 is the standard edition/dating authority but his specific per-text spans are not in the library. The earliest are pre-CE, the latest medieval. A single median is not meaningful; broad span given, confidence low.

title: Śāradātilaka
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Sanderson dates it most probably to the 12th c.; tightens current 1000-1200 range to 12th c.

title: Sarvajñānottaravṛtti
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (citing Goodall 2000)
note: Vṛtti (commentary) on the scripture Sarvajñānottara by Aghoraśiva, dated firmly via his Kriyākramadyotikā (1157). The base scripture is far older (early Siddhānta); this item is his commentary, so mid-12th c. Confirms current median.

title: Sarvollāsatantra
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Sanderson dates the author (of the Sarvollāsatantra) born c.1400 in Bengal, so the work is early-to-mid 15th c. Slightly earlier than current median.

title: Ṣaṭcakranirṇaya (Ṣaṭcakranirūpaṇa)
basis: Feuerstein, Tantra: Path of Ecstasy 1998; Segall (ed.) 2003 encyclopedia entry
source_file: cleaned_Encountering Buddhism. Western Psychology and Buddhist Teachings_ Seth Robert  Segall ed, 2003.txt
note: Ch.6 of Pūrṇānanda's Śrītattvacintāmaṇi (1577). Far later than current median 657.

title: Ṣaṭsahasrakālottarāgama (Ṣaṭsāhasra Kālottara)
basis: D. Goodall (criteria) / A. Sanderson, The Śaiva Age, in skt-en-mono; A. Watson, Self's Awareness of Itself, 2006
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Śaiva Siddhānta scripture. Kālottara corpus is among Goodall's 'early scriptures' (predating Sadyojyotis 7th/8th c.; post-Niśvāsa tpq 550). The Ṣaṭsāhasra is a large/later recension of the multi-version Kālottara, hence 7th-9th c. rather than the oldest 6th-c. short Sārdhatriśatika.

title: Ṣaṭtriṃśattattvasandoha
basis: Potter bibliography (ed. with Rājānaka Ānandakavi's Vivaraṇa); Padoux on Amṛtānanda
source_file: cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Short Kashmirian Trika tattva-digest with Rājānaka Ānanda's Vivaraṇa; post-Abhinavagupta Trika scholasticism (11th c. or later). No precise specialist date in library; placed broadly post-1000 c. Current median 1019 within range.

title: Lalitā Sahasranāma
basis: White 2003, Kiss of the Yogini
source_file: cleaned_Kiss of the Yogini. Tantric Sex in its South Asian Contexts_David Gordon White, 2003.txt
note: Date is for the Lalitasahasranama itself (the text). The slug name Saubhagyabhaskari is Bhaskararaya's 18th-c. commentary (Buhnemann/Bhattacharyya), which is NOT what is dated here; the work dated is ca. 10th c.

title: Saubhāgyataraṅgiṇī
basis: no precise specialist dating in library; genre/lineage inference
note: Late Śrīvidyā commentary (on Tantrarāja/Saundaryalaharī) by Mukunda; no firm scholarly date in library, lineage notes only. Bracketed broadly as late medieval; far later than machine median 789 but confidence low.

title: Saundaryalaharī
consensus_date: uncertain; not an ancient work (c. 8th-12th c.)
basis: Padoux 1990, Vāc (classical Śrīvidyā hymn, with 16th-c. Lakṣmīdhara commentary; 'probably not an ancient work')
source_file: cleaned_Vac. The Concept of the Word in Selected Hindu Tantras_Andre Padoux, 1990.txt
note: Śākta Śrīvidyā hymn; the Śaṅkara attribution is rejected. Library gives no firm date, only Padoux's caution; current 700-1100 range retained.

title: Siddhalakṣmīpūjāpaddhati
basis: D.G. White (in Tantra in Practice, 2000) on Siddhilakṣmī/Taleju and Pratap Malla
source_file: cleaned_Tantric Visions of the Divine Feminine. The Ten Mahavidyas_DAVID KINSLEY, 1998.txt
note: Siddhilakṣmī is the uttarāmnāya (Northern Transmission) royal goddess of the Newar Malla kings (Taleju). The pūjāpaddhati is an anonymous Newar ritual manual tied to the 16th-17th c. Malla royal cult; far later than cur_med. Source-file attribution approximate (quote is from the Tantra in Practice/White corpus).

title: Siddhāntārthasamuccaya
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Saiva Age (on Trilocanasiva)
note: Saiva Siddhanta work by Trilocanasiva (cited in Torella's IPK edition). Sanderson dates him to the 12th c.; pupil of Jnanasiva and near-contemporary of Aghorasiva (fl. 1157).

title: Siddhilakṣmī Ārāmika
basis: Sanderson, Śaivism and the Tantric Traditions (cult of Guhyakālī/Siddhilakṣmī)
note: Siddhilakṣmī is a Pratyaṅgirā of the Jayadrathayāmala (1st Ṣaṭka late 9th c., later hexads 10th-11th c. per Sanderson Śaiva Age); cult Newar, flourishing from c. 10th c. The specific 'Ārāmika' recension is undated in the library — date assigned to the tradition, not the text. Current median 698 too early.

title: Siddhilakṣmīdevyārcana
basis: no specialist dating in library
note: Newar Siddhilakṣmī worship-manual (arcana). Only an incidental Sanderson mention of the goddess in the library; no date. Belongs to the late Newar Sarvāmnāya tradition (likely post-13th c.) but undated in available sources, so null/none. Current median 630 likely far too early.

title: Siddhilakṣmī-akṣaramālāmantra
basis: no specialist dating in library
note: Library has only a passing mention of Siddhilakṣmī as a Mantramārgic Śākta goddess (Sanderson); no date for this specific Newar mantra text. Siddhilakṣmī/Pratyaṅgirā worship is part of the late Newar Sarvāmnāya milieu (post-13th c.), but the text itself is undated here. Current median 630 almost certainly far too early; no source read that dates it, so null/none.

title: Siddhilakṣmīkoṭyāhutidinakṛtya
basis: Sanderson, Saivism and the Brahmanical Tradition; Lienhard-Manandhar Nepalese Mss catalogue
note: Not the early scripture: a Newar daily-ritual (dinakrtya/kotyahuti) manual for the Malla patron goddess Siddhilaksmi (a Jayadrathayamala Pratyangira); late Sarvamnaya paddhati, far later than median 806. Date uncertain.

title: Śivayogiśivācārya: Siddhāntaśikhāmaṇi
basis: Potter, Bibliography of Indian Philosophies (Renuka/Revanarya/Sivacarisvaracarya, 1190)
source_file: cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Virasaiva doctrinal text. Authorship/date much disputed (some place it 14th-15th c.); library's bibliographic source attributes it to Renuka c.1190. Low confidence; midpoint 1190 vs current 1297 < 100y so not counted as changed.

title: Jñānaprakāśa (Śālivāṭipuranivāsi): Śivāgamādimāhātmya
basis: Same author as Pauṣkaravṛtti; Goodall Kiraṇavṛtti ed. references Jñānaprakāśa; no explicit century
note: By the same Jaffna Saiddhāntika Jñānaprakāśa; no precise sourced date in library, conventional 16th c. Retained current median.

title: Śivārcanacandrikā
basis: Freschi & Maas (eds.) 2017, Adaptive Reuse (E. Freschi chapter)
source_file: cleaned_Freschi and Maas_Adaptive Reuse_Aspects of Creativity in South Asian Cultural History.txt
note: Saiva ritual/devotional, not an early tantra. Author firmly dated 1520-1593 by specialist scholarship; consistent with machine median.

title: Śivadharma (Śivadharmaśāstra)
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (Śivadharma corpus = obligations of uninitiated lay Śiva devotees, early)
note: Library lacks an explicit century; Śivadharmaśāstra is earliest layer of the Śivadharma corpus, placed by specialists (De Simini, Bisschop, Sanderson) c. 6th-7th c., well before cur_med 835 -> changed.

title: Śivadharma Nandiprokta (Śivadharmaśāstra)
basis: Peter Bisschop (cited in Kotyk 2017, Buddhist Astrology and Astral Magic in the Tang Dynasty)
source_file: cleaned_Buddhist Astrology and Astral Magic in the Tang Dynasty_Jeffrey Theodore Kotyk, 2017.txt
note: Bisschop's specialist dating to 6th-7th c. CE; substantially earlier than current median 919 (>100y change).

title: Śivadharmottara
basis: Sanderson, Hindu Tantric Chaplains (Śivadharma corpus of lay Śaiva observance); cited as a mantravāda source by Mallinson, Khecarīvidyā 2003
note: Early lay-Śaiva merit/devotion scripture (Śivadharma corpus), generally placed 7th-9th c.; library gives no precise specialist century, hence low confidence/broad range.

title: Śivadṛṣṭi
consensus_date: early 10th c. CE (Somānanda, teacher of Utpaladeva fl. c.925-975)
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (Utpaladeva fl. c. 925-975); Nemec, The Ubiquitous Śiva
note: Foundational Pratyabhijñā text by Somānanda, teacher of Utpaladeva (fl. c.925-975); thus Somānanda c.875-925/early 10th c.; commentator dated 9th C. Consistent with cur_med 888.

title: Śivajñānasiddhi
basis: Karl Potter bibliography (Lit. about the Philosophical Systems) + Klostermaier 1994, Survey of Hinduism (Meykaṇḍa school)
source_file: cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Library identifies this only as Aruḷnandi's Sivajñānasiddhiyār, the Tamil Śaiva-Siddhānta work by the disciple of Meykaṇḍar (fl. c.1221), i.e. mid-13th c., NOT 11th c. Metadata/collection (MB, Trika neighbours) leaves which Śivajñānasiddhi uncertain; low confidence. If genuinely this Tamil work, median should move ~+240y.

title: Śivasaṃhitā
basis: Mallinson (Śivasaṃhitā ed./tr., cited in White 2004, Yoga in Practice); a late haṭhayoga text for householders, post-dating the Gorakṣaśataka/Haṭhapradīpikā tradition
note: A late-medieval (c. 15th c.) haṭhayoga compendium, not an early tantra; machine date (815) far too early. Eliade also notes its first verse has 'a strong Buddhist coloring,' consistent with a late, syncretic work.

title: Śivastotrāvalī
basis: Sanderson, dating cited in skt-en-mono (Pratyabhijna corpus)
note: Duplicate of Utpaladeva's Sivastotravali (other recension/encoding); fl. c. 925-975. Current median 926 correct. Stotra, not a tantra.

title: Śivasūtravimarśinī (text version) [commentary on the Śivasūtra]
basis: Vimarśinī by Kṣemarāja, disciple of Abhinavagupta (fl. c.975-1025): Ratié 2017; Sanderson, Śaiva Age
source_file: cleaned_Indian Epistemology and Metaphysics-Bloomsbury Academic_Joerg Tuske, ed , 2017.txt
note: This text version is Kṣemarāja's Śivasūtravimarśinī commentary (c.1000-1050), not the root Śivasūtra (9th c., Vasugupta). Dated as the commentary. cur_med confirmed.

title: Somaśambhupaddhativyākhyā
basis: Flood, gavin-flood; root Somaśambhupaddhati is 11th c. (Somaśambhu 1073 CE), commentary later
note: COMMENTARY on the 11th-c. Somaśambhupaddhati (root text 1073 CE). The vyākhyā (Trilocanaśiva) post-dates the root, so 12th c. Library has no explicit date for the commentary itself.

title: Spandanirṇaya
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (Kṣemarāja as pupil of Abhinavagupta, whose dated works fall c.990-1015)
note: Kṣemarāja, direct pupil of Abhinavagupta (whose absolute dates run to Saptarṣisaṃvat 4090 = c.1014/15 CE); Spandanirṇaya thus first half 11th c. Median fits.

title: Śrītattvanidhi
basis: External: composed under patronage of Kṛṣṇarāja Wodeyar III, Mysore (r. 1799-1868); no library dating found
note: Modern iconographic/Tantric compendium produced at the Mysore court of Kṛṣṇarāja Wodeyar III; not an early tantra. Library has no specialist dating. Current date retained.

title: Śrīvidyārṇavatantra
basis: Goudriaan 1992, in Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism (ed. Padoux)
source_file: cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism. Studies in Honor of André Padoux_Teun Goudriaan, ed, 1992.txt
note: Goudriaan: a compilatory Śrīvidyā tantra, traditionally ascribed to Vidyāraṇya (14th c.) but actually compiled before 1726, i.e. a late (likely 16th-17th c.) compilation. Midpoint (1663) shifts >100y later than current median 1379.

title: Jagaddhara Bhaṭṭa: Stutikusumāñjali
basis: Bendall, Catalogue (attests the work); Stainton 2019 Poetry as Prayer dating tradition (not in library)
source_file: cleaned_Bendall_Catalogue of Sanskrit, Pali, and Prakrit books in the British museum acquired during the years 1876-92.txt

title: Subodhamañjarī
basis: Sanderson 2009/2014, History through textual criticism (note on the two Vāmanadattas)
note: Subodhamañjarī = Svabodhodayamañjarī, by the Śaiva Vāmanadatta son of Harṣadatta (Bodhavilāsa author), Kashmirian nondualist quoted by Śivopādhyāya/Abhinava and contemporary with Utpaladeva's milieu (Torella); hence c. 10th c., not 8th.

title: Śukavākya
basis: no occurrence in library
note: Title (Śukavākya, sayings of the parrot/Śuka) not attested anywhere in the 8201-file library; unidentifiable minor text. No basis to date; machine median 560 unsupported.

title: Sūkṣmāgama
consensus_date: by 13th c. CE (South Indian Śaiva Siddhānta, redacted form later than early canon)
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (citing Goodall, Early Tantra Workshop, on Saiddhantika scripture citations)
note: South Indian Saiddhantika temple agama; the surviving redaction with king-protection ritual is a later south-Indian text, cited only from c.13th c. (Sivapujastavavyakhya). Far later than cur_med 628; counted as changed.

title: Suprabhedāgama
consensus_date: by 13th c. CE (South Indian Śaiva Siddhānta)
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (citations in the 13th-c. Śivapūjāstavavyākhyā)
note: South Indian Saiddhantika temple agama; attested (cited) only by the 13th-c. Śivapūjāstavavyākhyā, so the surviving form is much later than the early Saiddhantika canon. Far later than cur_med 628; counted as changed.

title: Cidānandanātha: Svacchandapaddhati
basis: No specialist dating of this paddhati found in library; Cidānanda(nātha) is a Śrīvidyā dīkṣā-name borne by several late authors (cf. Bharati, Tantric Tradition)
note: A late ritual digest (paddhati) of the Svacchandatantra by an author bearing a Śrīvidyā initiation-name; the root Svacchandatantra is early-medieval (pre-9th c.) but this paddhati is a much later derivative manual. No firm date in library; left null.

title: Narasiṃhaṭhakkura: Tārābhaktisudhārṇava
basis: N. N. Bhattacharyya, History of the Tantric Religion (2006)
source_file: cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: Bhattacharyya places it at end of 17th c.; current median 1637 slightly early but within century.

title: Tārārahasyavṛttikā
basis: no specialist dating in library; only Haraprasāda Śāstrī bibliographic note in Winternitz, History of Indian Literature II (1933)
source_file: cleaned_History of Indian Literature II_MAURICE WINTERNITZ, 1933.txt
note: Library has no datable scholarship; Śaṅkara Āgamācārya is a late-medieval Bengali Tārā-cult author (commonly ~16th c.) but no source read confirms a date. Left null.

title: Tāratantra
basis: Bhattacharyya 2006, History of the Tantric Religion
source_file: cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt

title: Tantrābhidhāna
basis: no specialist dating found in library
source_file: cleaned_Almogi_Rong zom pa's Discourses on Buddhology.txt
note: A tantric bīja-mantra lexicon (ed. Avalon, Tantric Texts Series); appears only as bibliographic/reference mentions, never dated. Likely a late nibandha-style lexicon but no scholarly date in library.

title: Tantrarājatantra
basis: Hoens in Gupta-Hoens-Goudriaan 1979 (Hindu Tantric Tradition), cited by Ludvik 2001; comm. Sudarśanā 18th c. (Bharati 1965)
note: Late Śrīvidyā tantra; specialist date 16th-17th c., far LATER than current median 958 (>500y change); commentary Sudarśanā 18th c.

title: Tantrasāra
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (2009)
note: Abhinavagupta's prose digest of the Tantrāloka; dated by his floruit.

title: Tārārahasya
basis: Encyclopedia of Tantra (Sadhu Santideva) 1999
note: Sakta tantric digest. Brahmananda Giri dated to 16th c.; same author/era as the Tantrasara of Krsnananda (16th c.). Consistent with machine median.

title: Tattvaprakāśa (Aṣṭaprakaraṇa)
basis: King Bhoja of Dhārā reign dates (Inden/Ali/Walters 2000; Szántó, Catuṣpīṭha)
source_file: cleaned_Querying the Medieval. Texts and the History of Practices in South Asia_Ronald B. Inden, Daud Ali, Jonathan S. Walters,  2000.txt
note: Śaiva-Siddhānta work attributed to King Bhoja of Dhārā, r. c.1011-1055. cur_med 1033 confirmed.

title: Sadyojyoti: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa — Tattvasaṃgraha
basis: Watson, The Self's Awareness of Itself (2006), §6 'The Date of Sadyojyotis'
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa of Sadyojyoti. Confirms current median (702); refined bounds to 650-725.

title: Sadyojyoti: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa — Tattvatrayanirṇaya
basis: Watson, The Self's Awareness of Itself (2006), §6 'The Date of Sadyojyotis'
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa of the Śaiva Siddhānta theologian Sadyojyoti; outer limits 600-830, most likely 7th/early 8th c. Confirms current median (700) and bounds (675-725).

title: Tejobindu Upaniṣad
basis: White 2011, Yoga in Practice (Yoga Upaniṣads dated 800-1300 CE; Tejobindu among the northern texts)
note: Late (Yoga) Upaniṣad. White brackets the North Indian Yoga Upaniṣads to 800-1300 CE without a single date for Tejobindu; midpoint ~1050 vs current 908 = +142y, changed. The expanded southern Tejobindu is later still (1300-1750).

title: Timirodghāṭa(tantra)
basis: Alexis Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism; cited as scripture by Kṣemarāja (Svacchandatantroddyota)
note: Lost Vidyāpīṭha/Kaula Śaiva tantra, cited by Kṣemarāja (early 11th c.), hence pre-10th c. No precise date in library.

title: Tripurārahasya, Māhātmyakhaṇḍa
basis: N.N. Bhattacharyya 2006 (attribution to Haritayana, comm. Tatparyadipika of Srinivasa); recent specialist Schwarz Linder not in library
source_file: cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: Srividya work; Mahatmyakhanda is a late South-Indian Srividya/Puranic-tantric composition (commonly placed ~16th-17th c.). No firm century in library; cur_med 779 far too early.

title: Triśikhibrāhmaṇopaniṣat (Triśikhibrāhmaṇa Upaniṣad)
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: Only cited as a source in Eliade/White (a late Yoga Upaniṣad), never dated. Scholarly consensus elsewhere places Yoga Upaniṣads late medieval (post-11th c.), but no library statement; consensus_date null.

title: Troṭalātantra (Troṭala/Troṭalātantra)
basis: traditional canon-lists (listed among Bhairava/Yāmala tantras with Kubjikāmata, Mayottara); no work-specific scholarly date in library
note: Troṭala/Troḍala listed among the canonical Bhairava/Vidyāpīṭha tantras (same milieu as Kubjikāmata). No specialist date located; broad Bhairava-tantra range 8th-10th c. assigned at low confidence. Midpoint ~850 within ~50y of cur_med 893.

title: Ucchiṣṭacāṇḍālinīkalpa
basis: no dating scholarship located
note: Only ritual-procedure mentions of the goddess Ucchiṣṭacāṇḍālī (=Mātaṅgī) found (Tantra in Practice); no date given for this kalpa text. Mātaṅgī/Ucchiṣṭacāṇḍālinī worship is associated with later (post-10th c.) Śākta/Daśamahāvidyā material, but no firm in-library dating. Date left null.

title: Uḍḍāmareśvaratantra
basis: no specialist dating; only catalogue/index attestation (Encyclopedia of Tantra)
note: Late minor magical/Kaula tantra; generally regarded as a late (post-12th c.) compilation but no datable scholarly statement in library. Date unverified; cur_med 714 almost certainly far too early but left unchanged for lack of a sourced date.

title: Uddhārakośa
basis: no specialist dating in library
note: Saiva tantric mantra-syllabary lexicon (mantroddhara code) ascribed to Daksinamurti; only 1938 Raghu Vira edition cited in src, no specialist re-dating found in reference-literature. Generally treated as a late-medieval mantra dictionary but no grounded date available.

title: ugracaṇḍāpūjāpaddhati
basis: no dating scholarship found in library
note: A pūjāpaddhati (ritual worship manual) for the goddess Ugracaṇḍā (a Navadurgā/Mahiṣamardinī form). Sanderson mentions the deity but the library has no date for this specific paddhati; paddhatis are typically later compilations. No date assigned.

title: Umāsaṃhitā
basis: Sanderson (trans.), Śivapurāṇa (skt-en-mono) — locates but does not independently date
note: Not a tantra: the 5th saṃhitā of the Śivapurāṇa (Purāṇic, Śaiva). Library only describes its place in the Purāṇa; no specialist date found. Śivapurāṇa broadly c.10th-14th c.; current median ~868 unverified.

title: Unmattabhairava
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (Mantrapitha Bhairavatantra) and The Saiva Age
note: One of the four Mantrapitha Bhairavatantras (with Svacchanda/Canda/Krodha), cited in early scripture-lists (Picumata, Svacchanda); early Mantramarga, redacted after the Nisvasa corpus, broadly 7th-9th c.

title: ūrdhvāmnāyapūjāvidhi
basis: White 2003, Kiss of the Yogini (on āmnāya systematization)
source_file: cleaned_Kiss of the Yogini. Tantric Sex in its South Asian Contexts_David Gordon White, 2003.txt
note: Pūjā manual of the ūrdhvāmnāya, latest (fifth/'upper') Kaula transmission. White: āmnāyas systematized 11th c., ūrdhvāmnāya is latest and linked to the 13th-14th c. Kulārṇava; a pūjāvidhi for it is necessarily late/post-classical, far later than 8th c.

title: ūrdhvāmnāya damanārohaṇapaddhati
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: Newar/Nepalese Kaula ritual paddhati for the damanaka (damanārohaṇa) festival within the ūrdhvāmnāya transmission. Anonymous ritual manual, almost certainly late medieval/early modern Newar; no source read gives a date.

title: Vāruṇapaddhati
basis: none found
note: No specialist dating for this Śaiva paddhati located in the library (incl. Sanderson). The cited 'Śaiva Literature' survey is not present in the corpus. Retain machine estimate; unverified.

title: Vārāhītantra
basis: no dating found; only doctrinal citations (Bharati, Tantric Tradition 1965)
note: Library only quotes its contents, never dates it. A late Śākta digest tantra (likely post-1300) but no source read gives a date. Left null.

title: Vārāhopaniṣat (Varāha Upaniṣad)
basis: Feuerstein 1997, Teachings of Yoga (Yoga-Upanisad group)
note: Varaha Upanisad is one of the late Yoga-Upanisads (Advaita-Vedantic Yoga); Feuerstein dates this group 15th-16th c. Late medieval, NOT a tantra and not 10th c. Current median 930 ~570y too early.

title: Varivasyārahasya
basis: Hüsken/Neubert, Negotiating Rites (2011); D. R. Bhandarkar Volume (1940); Padoux, Vāc (1990)
source_file: cleaned_Negotiating Rites_Ute Husken, Frank Neubert, ed., 2011.txt
note: Bhāskararāya dated by his Lalitāsahasranāma bhāṣya of A.D. 1729 (Bhandarkar Vol.) and 'late 17th to mid-18th c.' (Hüsken). Varivasyārahasya is his Śrīvidyā work. Matches current median.

title: Varuṇapaddhativyākhyā
basis: no specialist dating located in library
note: South-Indian Śaivasiddhānta ritual commentary by Nigamajñāna II (associated in specialist literature with the 16th c. Cidambaram lineage, but NOT stated in this library). Only a lexical-file hit found; date undetermined here.

title: vāruṇapaddhativyākhyāna
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: Anonymous commentary (vyākhyāna) on a Vāruṇa paddhati; no scholarly date located in reference-literature. Kept current median; confidence none.

title: Vātūlanāthasūtra
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (Krama Sahasa-school scripture; commentator Anantaśakti)
note: Late Krama (Sahasa) scripture; its tradition postdates Kṣemarāja (early 11th c.), commentary MS dated 1158/9. No firm date for sutras themselves; late, 12th-13th c.

title: Vātulottarāgama (Vātulottara)
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (2009); Goudriaan/Gupta, Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism (1992)
note: Late South-Indian Śaiva Siddhānta upāgama of the Vātula group; cited by Saiddhāntika authors c.12th c. so it predates that. Library lacks a precise specialist date; placed in the later Siddhānta-āgama range. Heuristic only.

title: Vīrāgama
basis: no specific dating scholarship found
note: One of the 28 Śaiva Siddhānta Āgamas; the surviving printed Āgamas are generally late/South Indian, but no specialist (Goodall/Sanderson) dating of THIS text found in library. Left null rather than invent.

title: Vijayottaratantra
consensus_date: late South-Indian Saiddhāntika recension, c. 13th–16th c. CE
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age
note: Distinct from the Trika Mālinīvijayottara. The extant Vijaya/Vijayottara is a South-Indian Saiddhāntika Āgama whose citations do not appear before Vedajñānaguru II (2nd half 16th c.); a much later text than the machine median of 705.

title: Vijñānabhairava (Vijñānabhairavatantra)
basis: Sanderson, History through Textual Criticism (Spandapradīpikā, 1st half 10th c., draws on the VBh)
note: Trika scripture; terminus ante quem early 10th c. (drawn on by the Spandapradīpikā and the Pratyabhijñā tradition; commented by Kṣemarāja). Conventional placement 8th–9th c.; consistent with current median.

title: Vijñānabhairava, with commentary Kaumadī (Kaumudī)
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age; Mallinson & Singleton 2017, Roots of Yoga (chronological placement among early Śaiva tantras)
source_file: cleaned_Roots of Yoga_James Mallinson, Mark Singleton, 2017.txt
note: Root Vijñānabhairava is a Bhairavatantra of the Trika orbit, grouped with the 8th-9th c. tantras and quoted by Abhinavagupta/Kṣemarāja (early 11th c.), so it predates them. The commentary Kaumudī by Bhaṭṭa Ānanda is far later (medieval/early-modern). Midpoint 800 vs median 1139 -> changed (dating the root tantra).

title: Vimalāvatītantra
basis: no reference located in library
note: No occurrence of Vimalāvatī/Vimalāgama in any form in the reference library; no scholarly dating available. Date left null.

title: Viśvasāratantra
basis: no specific dating found in library
note: Late Śākta tantra cited only as a doctrinal source via Avalon/Prāṇatoṣiṇī-type compilations (Bhattacharyya, Kinsley, Encyclopedia). No specialist date given; the kind of tantra excerpted in late (16th c.) nibandhas, so likely late-medieval, but no sourced consensus to assign.

title: Yogavāsiṣṭha, Part 3, with the commentary Tātparyaprakāśa
basis: Mallinson & Singleton 2017, Roots of Yoga (citing Hanneder/Slaje Mokṣopāya project)
source_file: cleaned_Roots of Yoga_James Mallinson, Mark Singleton, 2017.txt
note: Base text = the Kashmirian Mokṣopāya, 11th c. (Hanneder/Slaje). The attached commentary Tātparyaprakāśa by Ānandabodhendra Sarasvatī is much later (c. 16th-17th c.); date given is for the primary work.

title: Yogavāsiṣṭha, Part 4 (Sthitiprakaraṇa), with Tātparyaprakāśa
basis: Mallinson & Singleton 2017, Roots of Yoga
source_file: cleaned_Roots of Yoga_James Mallinson, Mark Singleton, 2017.txt
note: Mokṣopāya base text, 10th-11th c.; commentary much later. Changed: text predates current median by ~168y.

title: Bhaṭṭa Rāmakaṇṭha: Vyomavyāpistava
basis: Watson 2006 / Goodall 1998 / Sanderson on Rāmakaṇṭha II's floruit
source_file: cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Rāmakaṇṭha II's commentary/hymn on the Vyomavyāpin mantra; dated by his floruit c. 950-1000. Date confirmed.

title: Yogacūḍāmaṇyupaniṣat (Yogacūḍāmaṇi Upaniṣad)
basis: Mallinson 2003, Khecarīvidyā (Yoga Upaniṣads are post-Haṭhapradīpikā derivative texts)
note: One of the late Yoga Upaniṣads; Mallinson treats them as derivative texts post-dating the Haṭhapradīpikā (~1450), later compiled into the Muktikā canon. Far later than cur_med 835 -> changed.

title: Yogajāgama
basis: no specific dating found in library
note: South Indian Śaiva Āgama (Yogaja-āgama) known mainly from IFP/Pondicherry manuscript collections; no specialist dating present in this library.

title: Yogakuṇḍalyupaniṣat (Yogakuṇḍalinī / Yogakuṇḍalī Upaniṣad)
basis: Mallinson & Singleton, Roots of Yoga (2017), citing Bouy 1994
source_file: cleaned_Roots of Yoga_James Mallinson, Mark Singleton, 2017.txt
note: Late 'Yoga Upaniṣad' derivative of hathayoga texts; far later than machine date 940. Major change.

title: Yogapīṭhakramodaya
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: No scholarship dating this text located in reference-literature/. Title appears only as a generic 'yogapīṭha' substring; no firm date can be grounded without inventing one.

title: Yogasārasaṃgraha
basis: Harris 1981, Neoplatonism and Indian Thought (fl. ca. 1575); also Chakrabarti 1999, Sinha 1938 (16th c.)
source_file: cleaned_Neoplatonism and Indian Thought_R  Baine Harris, 1981.txt
note: Work of the late-medieval polymath Vijñānabhikṣu, fl. c. 1575 (16th c.). Current median 920 is grossly off; not an early tantra.

title: Yogaśikhopaniṣat (Yogaśikhā Upaniṣad)
consensus_date: late (Yoga Upaniṣad), c. 13th-14th c. CE for extant text
basis: Mallinson 2003, Khecarividya; cf. Bouy, Les Natha-Yogin et les Upaniṣads
note: Library treats the Yoga Upaniṣads as late, derivative haṭha-yogic compilations (post-Hahapradīpikā milieu); no exact date for Yogaśikhā specifically. Eliade loosely groups a short core early, but extant expanded text is late medieval. Low confidence; cur_med 932 likely too early for extant recension.

title: Yogatattvopaniṣat (Yogatattva Upaniṣad)
basis: White, Yoga in Practice (2012), 'Yoga in the Yoga Upaniṣads'
note: One of the (early-layer, but haṭha-influenced) Yoga Upaniṣads; no work-specific date in library. Broad 9th-13th c. range; midpoint ~1050 within 100y window of cur_med 887? midpoint 1050 vs 887 = 163y, technically >100 -> counts as changed (range widened later).

title: Yogaviṣaya
basis: no specialist dating located in library
note: Short Nāth haṭha-yoga text attributed to Matsyendra; no dating statement found in reference-literature/ (Matsyendra/Mīnanātha hits are all Buddhist-siddha or general). Date undetermined here.

title: Yogavāsiṣṭha, Part 5 (Upaśamaprakaraṇa), with Tātparyaprakāśa
basis: Mallinson & Singleton 2017, Roots of Yoga
source_file: cleaned_Roots of Yoga_James Mallinson, Mark Singleton, 2017.txt
note: Mokṣopāya base text, 10th-11th c.; commentary much later. Changed: text predates current median by ~168y.

title: Yoginīhṛdaya
basis: Padoux 1990, Vac (Traipuradarsana / Yoginihrdaya); ed. Le coeur de la yogini
source_file: cleaned_Vac. The Concept of the Word in Selected Hindu Tantras_Andre Padoux, 1990.txt
note: Srividya text, the later (uttarardha) layer of the Vamakesvarimata/Nityasodasikarnava. Padoux dates the Traipuradarsana to 10th-12th c.; the Yoginihrdaya is its later component, so near the end of that range.

title: Yoginītantra
basis: Hugh B. Urban, The Power of Tantra, 2010
source_file: cleaned_Power of Tantra. Religion, Sexuality, and the Politics of South Asian Studies_HUGH B. URBAN, 2010.txt
note: Śākta Assamese Yoginī Tantra (Kāmākhyā), distinct from Buddhist yoginītantra genre; references 16th-c. Koch king Viśva Siṃha. Massively later than current median 652.

title: Anaparāddhastotra
basis: H. Nakamura, Indian Buddhism: A Survey with Bibliographical Notes, 1980
source_file: cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Buddhist hymn (stotra), not a tantra. Ascribed to the poet Mātṛceṭa, contemporary of Kaniṣka, 2nd c. CE. Attribution confirmed in Nakamura and Tillemans (Études bouddhiques 1992). Massive shift earlier from median 881.

title: Dvādaśakāranayastotra
basis: no specialist dating found in reference-literature/
note: Devotional hymn on the Twelve Deeds of the Buddha, traditionally (pseudepigraphically) ascribed to Nāgārjuna. Roloff notes it as 'a Controversial Hymn' but gives no composition date. Genre is later than the historical Nāgārjuna (2nd c.); no firm date in library.

title: Dharmadhātustava
basis: Brunnhölzl 2008, In Praise of Dharmadhātu (Nāgārjuna and the Third Karmapa)
source_file: cleaned_In Praise of Dharmadhatu.  Nagarjuna and Third Karmapa, Rangjung Dorje_Karl Brunnhölzl,  2008.txt
note: Brunnhölzl marshals strong Indian testimony (Bhāvaviveka, Atiśa, Nāropa) attributing it to Nāgārjuna and treats it as his; some modern scholars dispute the attribution (tathāgatagarbha content). Dated to Nāgārjuna's floruit (~2nd c.); re-dated ~340y earlier than current median (490).

title: Bhadracaryādeśanā (Bhadracarīpraṇidhāna)
basis: Osto 2004, Gaṇḍavyūha-sūtra study
source_file: cleaned_Gandavyuha-sutra. Study of Wealth, Gender and Power in an Indian Buddhist Narrative_Douglas Edward Osto, 2004.txt
note: The Bhadracari(pranidhana)/Bhadracaryadesana is the verse aspiration appended to / circulating with the Gandavyuha-sutra, among its oldest layers. Osto places the Gandavyuha's formative period c. 3rd c. CE (Dhanyakataka); allowing for accretion, c. 3rd-4th c. Aligns with current median 300.

title: Vikramāraccivaibhava
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: Poetic eulogy (vaibhava) of Vikramaracci, a Sri Lankan Buddhist physician (per Dan Martin glossary). No date given for the work; not datable from library.

title: Ākāśagarbhanāmāṣṭottaraśatastotra
basis: none found
note: 108-name hymn to Ākāśagarbha; library has an Ākāśagarbha-sūtra translation but no dating of this specific stotra. Likely a late anonymous Vajrayāna liturgical hymn, but no dated source - left null.

title: Tārānamaskāraikaviṃśatistotra [Āryatārānamaskāraikaviṃśatistotra]
basis: general scholarly placement of the early Tārā cult (Davidson, Indian Esoteric Buddhism); no item-specific dated quote in library
source_file: cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism_ A Social History of the Tantric Movement.txt
note: The 'Praise to Tārā in Twenty-One Homages' (Toh 438), embedded in the Sarvatathāgatamātṛtārā root-tantra; among the earliest Tārā texts, conventionally placed with the emergence of the Tārā cult in the 7th–8th c. Library has no specialist dated statement for this exact stotra; confidence low.

title: Tārāstuti [Āryatārāstuti]
basis: Willson, In Praise of Tara (1992); Shaw, Buddhist Goddesses (2006) on the emergence of the Tārā cult and the Sragdharāstotra
source_file: cleaned_In Praise of Tara. Songs to the Saviouress_Martin Willson  1992.txt
note: Anonymous Buddhist Tārā hymn. Tārā cult attested from 6th-7th c. (Shaw: introduced 7th c.); the comparator Sragdharāstotra is late-7th/early-8th c. No firm date for this specific stuti; current median ~650 plausible.

title: Maṅgalāṣṭaka
basis: no specialist dating in library
note: Maṅgalāṣṭaka is a genre of auspicious eight-verse benedictions (Newar ritual / wedding mangalashtaka), not a single datable composition; library has only incidental mentions. No grounded date.

title: Avadhānastotra [Vandanāstava]
basis: no specific dating found in library
note: Anonymous Buddhist hymn from a Buddhastotrasaṃgraha-type collection; no dating scholarship located in library.

title: Avalokiteśvarahayagrīvadhāraṇī
basis: Dan Martin 2006 bibliography; Hopkins/Tsong-kha-pa, Deity Yoga (canonical classification only)
source_file: cleaned_Deity Yoga In Action and Performance Tantra_Dalai Lama, Tsong-ka-pa, Jeffrey Hopkins, 1981.txt
note: Only catalogue attestations found (Dan Martin, Kanjur P379/531), classed as a Kriyā/Action-tantra dhāraṇī of the Lotus lineage. No specialist date statement; range estimated from dhāraṇī corpus, low confidence.

title: Avalokiteśvarāṣṭakastotra
basis: no specialist dating located in library
source_file: cleaned_Bendall_Catalogue of the Buddhist Sanskrit manuscripts in the University Library, Cambridge.txt
note: Short anonymous Buddhist hymn (aṣṭaka) to Avalokiteśvara; such stotras are attested in Nepalese MSS but undatable individually. No specific consensus date available.

title: Avalokiteśvarastotra
basis: Princeton Dict. of Buddhism 2014 (84-siddhas corpus); Verardi (Abhayadatta collection late 11th/early 12th c.)
note: Carpati is one of the 84 Mahasiddhas (active c. 8th-12th c.); no dating of this specific stotra in library. Range = floruit of the Mahasiddha siddha-author tradition.

title: Avalokiteśvarāṣṭottaraśatanāmastotra
basis: van Schaik, The Tibetan Avalokitesvara Cult in the Tenth Century
source_file: cleaned_Van Schaik_The Tibetan Avalokitesvara Cult in the Tenth Century.txt
note: 108-epithet praise of Avalokiteśvara listed in the Ldan dkar ma catalogue (AD 814), so it existed by early 9th c.; composition plausibly 8th c. No precise dating of this Skt stotra itself; terminus ante quem only.

title: Lokeśvaraśataka
basis: Winternitz 1933, History of Indian Literature II
source_file: cleaned_History of Indian Literature II_MAURICE WINTERNITZ, 1933.txt
note: Buddhist hymn to Avalokiteśvara; firmly placed in 9th c. by the Pāla king Devapāla synchronism (also noted by Nariman 1919).

title: Lokeśvarastotra
basis: no specialist dating of this anonymous hymn in library
note: Anonymous Avalokiteśvara/Lokeśvara hymn from the GRETIL/bodhisvara '108 Buddhist stotras' corpus. NB: the named Lokeśvarastotra in Hahn is Carpaṭi's, a DIFFERENT text. No basis to date this anonymous one.

title: Narakoddhārastotra
basis: Wedemeyer diss. (Āryadeva's Caryāmelāpakapradīpa); on stotras attributed to the Ārya-tradition Nāgārjuna
note: Hymn (Narakoddhāra-nāma) listed among the stotras attributed to the tantric Nāgārjuna of the Ārya Guhyasamāja exegetical tradition, conventionally placed late 9th-10th c. No firmer specialist date in library.

title: Rakṣākāla(kara)stava
basis: no specialist dating located in reference-literature/
note: Anonymous Buddhist protective stotra (GRETIL Buddhist-stotra corpus); library has only manuscript-catalogue attestations (Lienhard Nepalese MSS), no scholarly date. Not changed from cur_med; date unverified.

title: Ratnamālāstotra
basis: no specific dating of this Buddhist stotra found in library
note: Anonymous Buddhist hymn from the GRETIL stotra corpus. Library mentions of 'Ratnamālā'/'Yogaratnamālā' refer to Hevajra-related works/Kāṇha's commentary, not this stotra. No reliable consensus date.

title: Rūpastava
basis: Ramble (Both Fish and Fowl?) and Petech (Selected Papers) on the Licchavi king Narendradeva, to whom a recension of the hymn is ascribed
source_file: cleaned_Ramble_Both Fish and Fowl? Preliminary Reflections on Some Representations of a Tibetan Mirror-World.txt
note: Colophon in the Nepalese MS (Lienhard) names the Avalokiteśvara hymn as 'narendradeva-bhūpatikṛtam'; royal attribution dates it to Narendradeva's reign (acc. 643), agreeing with current median. Attribution traditional, hence low confidence.

title: Saptavidhānuttarastotra
basis: no specialist dating located; appears only as a Newar liturgical item (Locke)
note: Sevenfold supreme-worship (anuttarapūjā) hymn used in Newar Buddhist liturgy; no scholarly date for this specific stotra in the library. Genre old but undatable here.

title: Daśabhūmīśvaro nāma mahāyānasūtraratnarājastotra
basis: no dating source located in library for this hymn
note: Stotra praising the Daśabhūmika/Daśabhūmīśvara sūtra (a 'king of jewels' Mahayana sutra). No specialist dating of the stotra itself found in the library; the underlying Daśabhūmika-sūtra is early Mahayana (pre-297 CE Chinese tr.) but the hymn is not separately datable here. Left null.

title: Cakrasaṃvarastuti
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (2009), on the Cakrasaṃvara/Laghuśaṃvara corpus; Gerloff (Saroruhavajra's Hevajra-Lineage) for the stuti
note: Hymn (stuti) of the Cakrasaṃvara/Yoginītantra corpus, whose root tantra (Laghuśaṃvara) Sanderson dates late 9th c. with images/commentary only from 10th c.; the stuti is contemporary or somewhat later.

title: Catuḥṣaṣṭisaṃvarastotra
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (on Laghuśaṃvara/Cakrasaṃvara); Gray, The Cakrasaṃvara Tantra (Gray dates CS to 8th c.)
note: Hymn to the 64-fold Saṃvara (Cakrasaṃvara) mandala; depends on the Cakrasaṃvara/Laghuśaṃvara corpus. Gray favours 8th c., Sanderson late 9th-10th c.; the stotra is not earlier. Much later than cur_med 628; counted as changed.

title: Pīṭhastava
basis: no specialist dating located in library
note: Workitem src is a GRETIL Buddhist-stotras file; an anonymous Buddhist hymn (Pithastava) for which no dated specialist statement was found. A Pithastava (Kaula hymn to the 64 seats, Srinatha) also exists in skt-en-mono but that is a Sakta/Kaula text, not the Buddhist one indexed here. No reliable date.

title: Ṣaṭtriṃśatsaṃvarastuti
basis: no dating scholarship located
note: Anonymous Buddhist hymn (GRETIL 108 Buddhist stotras). No specialist dating in the library; sattrimsat/saṃvara word-matches are unrelated ritual passages. Left null.

title: Buddhagaṇḍīstava
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: Buddhist hymn (gandi-bell stava). Traditional attribution to Asvaghosa/Aryadeva is not credited by scholars; no datable specialist statement in the library. Hymn corpus generally medieval.

title: Gaṇḍīstava
basis: no dating discussion located in library
note: Only a title listing found (in a stotra index); no scholarly dating in the library. Traditional attribution to Aśvaghoṣa is unreliable. Date left null.

title: Gaṇeśastotra
basis: no specific dating found for this Buddhist Gaṇeśa hymn
note: Generic/anonymous stotra; no datable scholarship located. Left null.

title: Hāratīstotra
basis: no dating scholarship located
note: Anonymous Buddhist hymn to the yakṣiṇī Hārītī (GRETIL 108 Buddhist stotras / bodhisvara corpus). Library discusses Hārītī iconography/cult but does not date this stotra. Left null.

title: Mahācakravartināmāṣṭottaraśatastotra
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: Anonymous Buddhist hymn (Mahācakravartin = vidyārāja, related to the Mañjuśrī/Cakravartin vidyārāja-kalpa cult). No individual dating in the library; likely a later (post-7th c.) Mahāyāna/proto-tantric stotra but library gives no basis.

title: Vajramahākālastotra
basis: no specific dating found in library
note: Anonymous Buddhist Mahākāla hymn (from a stotra collection). Library has Mahākāla iconography/cult studies (Matsushita, Sperling) but no date for this particular stotra; Mahākāla as protector becomes prominent with later yoginitantra/Tibetan-transmitted material (post-8th c.) but no sourced consensus for the text itself.

title: Mañjuśrīnāmāṣṭottaraśatakastotra [Āryamañjuśrīnāmāṣṭottaraśatakastotra]
consensus_date: Buddhist Mañjuśrī stotra, c. 8th-11th c. CE
basis: only catalogue attestation (Skorupski sTog Kanjur catalogue); distinct from the Nāmasaṃgīti (pre-Vilāsavajra, mid-late 8th c.)
source_file: cleaned_Cakrasamvara Tantra. Discourse of Sri Heruka ( Sriherukabhidhana)_David B. Gray, 2007.txt
note: This is the 108-Names stotra, NOT the famous Mañjuśrīnāmasaṃgīti (which is pre-Vilāsavajra, c. 8th c.). The aṣṭottaraśataka stotra appears only in canon catalogues with no specialist date; estimated range, low confidence.

title: Pañcarakṣādevīstotrāṇi
consensus_date: 5th-8th c. CE (compiled as collection by c. 800 CE)
basis: Holz, The Bhadrakarātrī-sūtra (citing Skilling & Hidas); Nakamura 1980
source_file: cleaned_Holz_The Bhadrakarātrī-sūtra_Apotropaic Scriptures in Early Indian Buddhism.txt
note: Buddhist apotropaic dhāraṇī collection (Mahāpratisarā etc.); component texts emerged independently 5th-7th c. and were grouped by c.800 (Tib. transl.). Composite, evolved over centuries. Current median 601 reasonable.

title: Pratisarāstotra
basis: Nakamura, Indian Buddhism: A Survey (1980), on the Pañcarakṣā / Mahāpratisarā
source_file: cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Stotra attached to the Mahāpratisarā(-vidyārājñī), the leading Pañcarakṣā dhāraṇī; rendered into Chinese by Bodhiruci/Baosiwei c. 693 CE (terminus ante quem), so 7th c. or earlier. Within 100y of current median 699.

title: Bhaktiśataka
basis: Winternitz 1933, History of Indian Literature II; Nakamura 1980, Indian Buddhism
