source_file: cleaned_History of Indian Literature II_MAURICE WINTERNITZ, 1933.txt
note: Buddhist devotional Sataka by a Bengali Brahman convert; Nakamura groups the Bhaktiśataka with later Ceylon-transmitted Buddhist Sanskrit hymns. No precise date in library; 14th-15th c. consistent with current median.

title: Buddhabhaṭṭārakastotra
basis: no specialist dating located in reference-literature/
note: Minor anonymous Buddhist hymn; no specialist dating in the library.

title: Buddhagadyaya [Buddhagajjaya]
basis: only genre placement in Nakamura, Indian Buddhism (1980)
source_file: cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Sinhalese Buddhist Sanskrit kāvya-stotra grouped with Anuruddhaśataka etc.; no scholarly date in library. Date unverified.

title: Buddhastotra
basis: no specialist dating located in library
note: Generic anonymous Buddhist hymn from a GRETIL stotra collection; no individual dated specialist statement found. Buddhastotra as a genre spans Matrceta (1st-2nd c.) to medieval, so an individual anonymous piece cannot be reliably dated.

title: Nāmāṣṭaśataka
basis: no specialist dating; '108-names' (aṣṭaśataka) is a hymn genre with many instances
note: Title is generic '108 names' praise-hymn (Avalokiteśvara/Mañjuśrī/Tārā/Prajñāpāramitā variants exist). Cannot identify the specific text or its date from the library.

title: Śākyasiṃhastotra: navagrahakṛtam
basis: no specialist dating; genre placement only (Nakamura 1980)
source_file: cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Companion Buddha-hymn to the surapati version; anonymous Sinhalese/Theravāda Sanskrit stotra, undated in library. Date unverified.

title: Śākyasiṃhastotra: surapatikṛtam
basis: no specialist dating located; only genre placement in Nakamura, Indian Buddhism (1980)
source_file: cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Anonymous Sinhalese/Theravāda Sanskrit Buddha-hymn; Nakamura links it to the Narasiṃhagāthā tradition but gives no date. Date unverified.

title: Śākyasiṃhastotra: viṣṇukṛtam
basis: no specialist dating located in reference-literature/
note: Nepalese Buddhist hymn to Śākyasiṃha framed as spoken by Viṣṇu (Cambridge MS). The library hit (Nakamura's Śrī-Śākyasiṃhastotra in a Theravāda SE-Asian context) is unrelated; no dating.

title: Śākyasiṃhastotra: yaśodharākṛtam
basis: no specialist dating found; Kragh 2011 surveys female-authored texts but not this hymn
note: Anonymous Buddha-hymn pseudepigraphically ascribed to Yaśodharā, in the GRETIL 108-stotra corpus and Cambridge Nepalese mss. Not in Kragh's corpus of genuine female-authored texts (9th-11th c.). No basis to date; likely a later Nepalese-transmitted devotional stotra but unquantifiable here.

title: Sattvārādhanagāthā
basis: no specialist dating located
note: Only the common noun sattvārādhana ('service to beings') appears in the library (Bodhicaryāvatāra-pañjikā); the pseudo-Nāgārjuna Buddhist gāthā itself has no dating discussion found. Distinct from Hartmann's 'Sattvārādhanastava' manuscript noted in a catalogue. No date asserted.

title: Suprabhātastotra
basis: Schneider 2014, Buddhist Hymns: A New Collection (Suprabhātastava by Harṣadeva); reign dates of Harṣa of Kanauj 606-647 (Snellgrove, Indo-Tibetan Buddhism)
source_file: cleaned_Schneider_Buddhist Hymns_A New Collection_Sanskrit Texts from the Tibetan Autonomous Region.txt
note: Buddhist Suprabhata hymn ascribed to King Harṣa (r.606-647). Schneider notes the attribution is traditional and that Steiner argues the companion Ast belongs to an earlier Harṣa; dating rests on the attribution. cur_med 628 falls within Harṣa's reign; not changed.

title: Saptabuddhastotra
basis: no dating scholarship found in library (only catalogued as a MS hymn)
source_file: cleaned_Literary History of Sanskrit Buddhism_Nariman G K_1919.txt
note: Buddhist hymn to the Seven Buddhas (Saptatathāgata) known only from Nepalese manuscripts; Nariman (1919) lists it among unpublished MS stotras with no date. No specialist dating found.

title: Kamalākarasarvatathāgatastotra
basis: Potter (ed.), Survey of Mahayana texts 350-600 / Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies vol.9 (chapter of the Suvarnaprabhasa[uttama]sutra; first Chinese tr. Dharmaksema 385-433)
note: Identifiable with the Kamalakara-sarvatathagata(tattva)parivarta, a chapter/hymn of the Suvarnaprabhasottamasutra (Ruciraketu); core text translated by Dharmaksema (d.433), hence 5th-6th c. Identification is plausible but not certain, so low confidence.

title: Maṅgalaṣoḍaśastuti
basis: no specialist dating found in reference-literature/
note: No hits for Mangalasodasastuti / Maṅgalaṣoḍaśastuti; the Pali Solasamangala-gatha (Walker) is a different text. Minor Buddhist stuti; undatable from this library.

title: Mañjuvajrastotra
basis: Davidson, Indian Esoteric Buddhism (2002); Dalton on Buddhajñānapāda
source_file: cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism_ A Social History of the Tantric Movement.txt
note: Hymn to Mañjuvajra, the central deity of the Mañjuvajra maṇḍala of Buddhajñānapāda's Guhyasamāja system (late 8th/early 9th c. per Dalton). No dating of the stotra itself; dated by terminus a quo of the deity-cult, hence low confidence. Current median 629 too early.

title: Svayaṃbhūstava
basis: no dating scholarship located
note: Nepalese Buddhist hymn to the Svayambhū caitya (GRETIL 108 Buddhist stotras / bodhisvara corpus); library has many Svayambhū-related works but none dates this hymn. Left null.

title: Caṇḍikādaṇḍakastotra
note: No specialist dating of this daṇḍaka hymn to Caṇḍikā found in the library. Left undated; not invented.

title: Mahogratārāṣṭakastotra
basis: Willson 1992, In Praise of Tārā (dating the Kashmiri Ugratārā/Tārā-stotra milieu of Sarvajñamitra)
source_file: cleaned_In Praise of Tara. Songs to the Saviouress_Martin Willson  1992.txt
note: Anonymous eight-verse hymn to Ugratārā (Ekajaṭā/Mahogratārā). No direct dating found; dated indirectly via the Kashmiri Ugratārā stotra milieu (Sarvajñamitra, late 7th/early 8th c.). Author of THIS aṣṭaka not securely Sarvajñamitra; low confidence.

title: Mahogratārāstuti
basis: no specific dating scholarship located; Tārā-stuti corpus generally
note: No scholar in library dates this specific stuti (a hymn to Mahogra/Ugra Tārā). The Buddhist Tantric Tārā cult emerges from c. 7th-8th c.; Ugratārā/Ekajaṭā forms are later. No sourced date - left null-range, confidence none.

title: Dharmadhātuvāgīśvaramaṇḍalastotra
basis: Locke, Unique features of Newar Buddhism; Richardson, Shalu Murals diss. (descriptive only, no date)
note: Hymn to the Newar Buddhist Dharmadhātuvāgīśvara/Mañjughoṣa maṇḍala (Kathmandu Valley); library attests the maṇḍala iconographically but gives no composition date. Likely a late Nepalese Vajrayāna stotra, not early. No dated source - left null.

title: Vajrapāṇināmāṣṭottaraśatastotra
basis: none found
note: 108-name (aṣṭottaraśata-nāma) hymn to Vajrapāṇi; no scholarship dating this specific stotra found. Such nāmāṣṭottaraśata-stotras are typically late anonymous Vajrayāna/Nepalese liturgical compositions, but no dated source - left null.

title: Māravijayastotra
basis: no dating scholarship found
source_file: cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Short Buddhist hymn on Māra's defeat; only mentioned thematically by Nakamura, no date given. Library has no specialist dating. Left null.

title: Kalyāṇatriṃśatikāstotra
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: No dating scholarship located under any spelling. Buddhist praise-stotra (30-verse). Date undetermined.

title: Vajradevīstotra
basis: No dating of this specific hymn located; Vajradevī = Vajravārāhī/Vajrayoginī (cf. Sanderson, The Śaiva Age)
note: Anonymous Buddhist hymn to Vajradevī (Vajravārāhī/Vajrayoginī). The deity cult is post-9th-c. yoginītantra; but no specialist date for this stotra exists in the library, so left undated.

title: Vajravilāsinīstotra
basis: English, Vajrayogini: Her Visualization, Rituals, and Forms, 2002 (GSS43)
note: Stotra by Vibhuticandra, active to early 13th c. (Vikramasila sacked 1203); much later than median 804.

title: Vajrayoginīpraṇāmaikaviṃśikā
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (2009) on the Yoginītantra/Vajravārāhī-Vajrayoginī cult
note: 'Twenty-one Salutations to Vajrayoginī', a short hymn dependent on the developed Vajravārāhī/Vajrayoginī cult of the Cakrasaṃvara yoginītantra tradition (emerges late 8th-9th c., exegesis flourishes 9th-11th c.). Praise text postdates the cult; dated by inference, hence low confidence. Current median 633 too early.

title: Vajrayoginyāḥ piṇḍārthastutiḥ
basis: English, Vajrayogini: Her Visualization, Rituals, and Forms, 2002 (GSS26)
note: = GSS26 piṇḍārtha stuti by the mahasiddha Virupa, slightly younger than Indrabhuti/Sabara; yoginitantra siddha milieu 9th-10th c.

title: Vajrayoginyāḥ stutipraṇidhānam
basis: English, Vajrayoginī: Her Visualization, Rituals, and Forms (Wisdom 2002)
note: Anonymous Vajrayoginī praise/aspiration hymn. Cult emerges from the Cakrasaṃvara/yoginītantras (9th c.) and flourishes 10th-12th c.; the hymn belongs to that florescence. Current median 698 too early.

title: Vasudhārānāmadhāraṇīstotra (Āryavasudhārādhāraṇī)
basis: no firm composition date in library (treated as early Mahāyāna apotropaic dhāraṇī)
source_file: cleaned_Holz_The Bhadrakarātrī-sūtra_Apotropaic Scriptures in Early Indian Buddhism.txt
note: Early Mahāyāna apotropaic/prosperity dhāraṇī (Holz); discussed but not given a composition century in the library. Current span 400-799 plausible for the dhāraṇī genre but no sourced consensus -> left null.

title: Acintyastava
basis: Brunnhölzl 2009, The Luminous Heart; Brunnhölzl 2008, In Praise of Dharmadhātu
source_file: cleaned_Luminous Heart. Third Karmapa on Consciousness, Wisdom, and Buddha Nature_Karl Brunnholzl, 2009.txt
note: Hymn of the Catuḥstava; authorship by Nāgārjuna (the Madhyamaka master, c. 150 CE) accepted as undisputed by Brunnhölzl. Re-dated ~250y earlier than current median (414) to Nāgārjuna's floruit.

title: Lokātītastava
basis: Walser 2005, Nāgārjuna in Context
note: Walser treats the Lokatitastava as a genuine work of Nagarjuna (his 'group B'), whom he dates to the end of the 2nd c. CE ('Nāgārjuna writes at the end of the second century'). Hence c. 175 CE midpoint. Within ~37y of current median 212.

title: Prajñāpāramitāstotra
basis: Lamotte, Mahāprajñāpāramitāśāstra (Le Traité, vol. 2)
note: Incorporated into the Mahaprajnaparamitasastra (tr. Kumarajiva c.405 CE), so a firm terminus ante quem; Rahulabhadra placed as teacher/disciple of Nagarjuna (late 2nd-early 3rd c.), hence c. 3rd c. CE. Close to current median 210.

title: Tārāsragdharāstotra
basis: Willson 1992, In Praise of Tara; Nakamura 1980, Indian Buddhism
source_file: cleaned_In Praise of Tara. Songs to the Saviouress_Martin Willson  1992.txt
note: Sragdharā-stotra to Tārā by Sarvajñamitra of Kashmir; Willson and Nakamura (kāvya-style Tārā hymn) place him late 7th-early 8th c.; Rājataraṅgiṇī puts him c. 700 CE. Confirms existing date.

title: Buddhakapālatantra, with Abhayapaddhati of Abhayākaragupta
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (Buddhakapāla among yoginītantras); English 2002, Vajrayoginī (Abhayākaragupta abbot of Vikramaśīla)
note: Item is the Abhayapaddhati COMMENTARY (Chog Dorje 2009), datable to Abhayākaragupta's floruit c. 1084-1125. Base Buddhakapālatantra (a Heruka yoginītantra) is earlier, ~9th-10th c. Dated to commentary -> changed.

title: Kālacakrāvatāra (of Abhayākaragupta)
basis: Sanderson, The Śaiva Age (2009)
note: Authored work of Abhayākaragupta (1064-1125), who composed his major works in the early 12th c. under Rāmapāla; not an anonymous revealed tantra.

title: Kālacakrāvatāra
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (dates of Abhayākaragupta)
note: Author Abhayākaragupta is firmly dated 1064–1125 (regnal years of Rāmapāla); his Kālacakra-related works fall early 12th c., long after the Kālacakratantra's introduction (~1025). Author work, not a root tantra. Machine date (691) far too early.

title: Advayavajrasaṃgraha
basis: Shakabpa, One Hundred Thousand Moons (Maitripa 1007-1077); Lopez 2016; Joshi
note: Collection of short works by Advayavajra = Maitripa/Maitripada, firmly 11th c. (life 1007-1077). A samgraha of an 11th-c. author's tracts, not an early tantra.

title: Kālacakrapūjāvidhi
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (introduction/emergence of the Kālacakra)
note: A pūjāvidhi (ritual manual) necessarily postdates the Kālacakratantra, whose system emerged ~1025 under Mahīpāla I; such auxiliary vidhis are 11th-12th c. Not the root tantra. Machine date (695) much too early.

title: Ṣaḍaṅgayoga (Anupamarakṣita) with Raviśrījñāna's Guṇabharaṇī ṭippaṇī
basis: Lopez, Buddhism in Practice (Anupamarakṣita fl. c.1165 CE); ed. Sferra 2000
note: Kālacakra-tradition Six-Branch Yoga; edited by Sferra 2000. mūla-text dated to Anupamarakṣita's floruit c.1165. The Guṇabharaṇī commentary is by Raviśrījñāna (Kālacakra author, early-mid 13th c.). Current median ~520 is far too early; this is a 12th-c. work.

title: Pradīpoddyotana (Guhyasamājatantra-ṭīkā)
basis: Wedemeyer (Āryadeva's Lamp; pers. comm. in Vose, Resurrecting Candrakīrti)
note: Commentary on the Guhyasamāja by the tantric Candrakīrti (Ārya school), not the Madhyamaka author. Wedemeyer/Vose date c. 975-1025; Wedemeyer Lamp diss concurs. Current median ~2.5 c. too early.

title: Pradīpoddyotana on the Guhyasamājatantra
basis: Wedemeyer, Āryadeva's Lamp (diss.); cf. Davidson, Indian Esoteric Buddhism
note: COMMENTARY, not a root tantra. The tantric Candrakīrti of the Ārya/Jñānapāda exegetical school, distinct from the 6th-c. Madhyamaka philosopher; root Guhyasamāja is 8th c. (Davidson).

title: Vajrasattvasādhana (Vajrasattvaniṣpādanasūtra)
basis: Wayman 1980 / Warder 2000; cf. pseudo-Candrakīrti '?10thc.' in Pramāṇakīrtiḥ (Steinkellner Fs)
note: Work of the tantric Candrakīrti, Guhyasamāja Ārya-school exegete (author of Pradīpoddyotana). Dated 9th c. (Wayman, Warder); some place him as late as 10th c. Ed. Hong/Luo & Tomabechi 2009.

title: Guhyāvalīvivṛti (Ḍoṃbīpāda/Ghanadeva)
basis: no dating of this commentary found in library
note: This is a commentary (vivṛti) on the Guhyāvalī, ed. in Dhīḥ journal 2008, attributed to Ḍoṃbīpāda/Ghanadeva, within the Buddhist Guhyasamāja/Guhyāvalī orbit. No mention of this title or author with a date was located in the library; consensus null, confidence none.

title: Kālacakrabhagavatsādhanavidhi
basis: Arnold (ed.) 2016, As Long as Space Endures (Kālacakra essays); Wallace, Inner Kālacakra (Anupamarakṣita lineage)
source_file: cleaned_Arnold_As Long as Space Endures_Essays on the Kālacakra Tantra in Honor of H.H. the Dalai Lama.txt
note: Kālacakra entered India c.1025; Dharmākaraśānti sits late in the Anupamarakṣita ṣaḍaṅgayoga lineage (Anupamarakṣita–Śrīdhara–Bhāskaradeva–Dharmākaraśānti–Raviśrījñāna–Ratnarakṣita–Vibhūticandra, the last 12th–13th c.), so a 12th–13th c. text. Differs >100y from current 849.

title: Tattvajñānasaṃsiddhi (with ṭīkā Mahāsukhaprakāśikā)
basis: Sferra (ed.) 2008, Sanskrit Texts from Giuseppe Tucci's Collection I, identifying the MS and the Vajravarahi/yoginitantra opening
source_file: cleaned_Sanskrit Texts from Giuseppe Tucci’s Collection. Part I_Francesco Sferra, ed, 2008.txt
note: Buddhist tantric text opening with homage to Vajravilasini/Varahika (yoginitantra milieu, post-9th c.); the named commentaries (Sunyasamadhipada, Daśabalasrimitra, Viryasrimitra) are late Nepalese/Indian. Mahasukhaprakasika is a commentary, not the root work. No firm date in library; estimate. cur_med 781 too early.

title: Kamalanātha, Ratnāvalī (commentary on the Hevajratantra)
consensus_date: late 10th–11th c. CE (commentary); root Hevajratantra late 8th–early 9th c.
basis: Snellgrove, Indo-Tibetan Buddhism (Hevajratantra composed in the mahāsiddha milieu, c. late 8th c.) / Concealed Essence ed. (Farrow & Menon 1992): root tantra 'approximately the eighth century'; commentaries by later siddhas
source_file: cleaned_Concealed Essence of the Hevajra Tantra With Comentary Yogaratnamala_ G.W. Farrow & I. Menon, 1992.txt
note: This item is a COMMENTARY (Ratnāvalī of Kamalanātha, ed. Conlon 2023) on the Hevajratantra, not the root tantra. The Hevajratantra itself is dated late 8th–early 9th c.; a Sanskrit commentary by Kamalanātha is necessarily later (placed 10th–11th c., the commentarial florescence). No verbatim date of Kamalanātha found in library; bounds are inferential, hence low confidence.

title: Kriyāsaṃgrahapañjikā
basis: Onians 2002 (Kuladatta fl.1045-); Tanemura 2004 ed.; Bajracharya 2018
source_file: cleaned_Monasticism in Buddhism of Nepal-Mandala. Continuity and changes_SM Bajracharya, 2018.txt
note: Not an anonymous tantra but a Buddhist tantric ritual MANUAL (kriyāsaṃgraha) by the named Nepalese author Kuladatta, fl. mid-11th c. (Onians: 'fl.1045-'), placed 11th-12th c.; edited by Tanemura 2004/2020. Current median 635 grossly too early.

title: Yogaratnamālā (comm. on Hevajratantra) by Kṛṣṇācārya/Kāṇha
basis: Geoffrey Samuel, Origins of Yoga and Tantra (2008/2010); Snellgrove, Indo-Tibetan Buddhism
source_file: cleaned_Origins of Yoga and Tantra. Indic Religions to the Thirteenth Century_Geoffrey Samuel, 2008 (2010).txt
note: Kāṇha's commentary on the Hevajratantra (root tantra late 8th-early 9th c.); commentary itself ~9th c. Edition Tripathi-Negi 2006.

title: Laghukālacakratantra with Vimalaprabhā (Puṇḍarīka)
consensus_date: tantra c. 1000 CE; Vimalaprabhā early 11th c. (transl. Tibet 1027)
basis: Orofino/PIATS Graz 1995; Arnold (ed.) As Long as Space Endures
note: Kālacakratantra = last major Buddhist tantra composed in India. Laghutantra by Mañjuśrīkīrti/Mañjuśrīyaśas c.967-1010; Vimalaprabhā by Kalkī Puṇḍarīka, in Tibet by 1027. Machine median 619 wildly early.

title: Laghukālacakratantra with Vimalaprabhā
basis: Geoffrey Samuel, The Origins of Yoga and Tantra 2008/2010 (citing Wallace 2001, Newman 1998)
source_file: cleaned_Origins of Yoga and Tantra. Indic Religions to the Thirteenth Century_Geoffrey Samuel, 2008 (2010).txt
note: Abridged (laghu) Kālacakra root tantra with Vimalaprabhā; first Tibetan translation 1027 (Hatchell, Simioli). Last major Indian Buddhist tantra; the named author Mañjuśrīyaśas matches the item. Much later than current median 661.

title: Laghukālacakratantra with Vimalaprabhā commentary
consensus_date: early 11th c. CE (tantra ~1025; Vimalaprabhā late 11th c.)
basis: Buswell, Encyclopedia of Buddhism; Simioli (intro to Tibet 1027)
note: Latest of the major Buddhist tantras; introduced to Tibet 1027 CE, Vimalaprabhā systematised by end of 11th c. Midpoint 1025 vs 590 -> changed (huge).

title: Caryāgītikośa (with Munidatta's commentary)
basis: Williams (ed.), Buddhism: Critical Concepts vol.6 (Kānha 11th c.); Urban, Power of Tantra 2010 (Caryāpadas 10th-12th c.)
source_file: cleaned_Power of Tantra. Religion, Sexuality, and the Politics of South Asian Studies_HUGH B. URBAN, 2010.txt
note: Apabhraṃśa/Old-Bengali Buddhist siddha songs (Caryāgīti) with Munidatta's Sanskrit vṛtti; songs by Kānha and others 10th-12th c., the kośa-collection and commentary 12th c. Later than cur_med.

title: Yogimanoharā (Pañcakramaṭippaṇī of Muniśrībhadra)
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (Muniśrībhadra at Jagaddala under Rāmapāla c. 1072–1126)
note: Commentary (tippaṇī, the 'Yogimanoharā') on Nāgārjuna's Pañcakrama, ed. Jiang & Tomabechi 1996. Author Muniśrībhadra was a monk of Jagaddala Mahāvihāra, flourishing in the reign of Rāmapāla (c. 1072–1126), i.e. early 12th c. A commentary, not the root Pañcakrama. Midpoint 1110 vs current 766 -> changed.

title: Sekoddeśaṭīkā (Paramārthasaṃgraha)
basis: Sferra & Merzagora 2006, Sekoddeśaṭīkā by Naropa
source_file: cleaned_Sekoddesatika by Naropa (Paramarthasamgraha)_Francesco Sferra & Stefania Merzagora, 2006.txt
note: Buddhist Kālacakra COMMENTARY (on the Sekoddeśa portion of the Ādibuddha/Paramādibuddha tantra) by Nāropā; firmly dated first half 11th c. by Sferra. Far later than machine median 736.

title: Adhyātmasāraśatakaṭīkā
basis: item metadata (Dhīḥ journal, 1998); cf. modern Adhyātmasāraśataka author entries
source_file: cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: NOT a historical tantra: a modern Sanskrit commentary published in the Dhīḥ journal (Sarnath) in 1998. Empty title/author in workitem. Date is editorial/modern, not a textual-history consensus; current_med 787 is meaningless for this item.

title: Vimalaprabhā on the Laghukālacakratantra
basis: Multiple (Karmay 1998; Cornu; Ardussi) on Kālacakra appearance 1024-1027; Pundarika = author of Vimalaprabha commentary
source_file: cleaned_Arrow and the Spindle. Studies in history, myths, rituals and beliefs in Tibet. vol I (Dzogchen)_S. G. Karmay, 1998.txt
note: Commentary (Vimalaprabha/Stainless Light) by Pundarika on the Laghukalacakra; Kalacakra corpus is latest major Indian tantra, root tantra c.1024-27, this commentary early 11th c. Was machine-dated 642 (~400y too early).

title: Ratnākaraśānti, Muktāvalī (commentary on Hevajratantra)
basis: Makransky 1997, Buddhahood Embodied (Ratnākaraśānti ca. 1000 C.E.)
source_file: cleaned_Buddhahood Embodied. Sources of Controversy in India and Tibet (Abhisamayalankara)_John J Makransky, 1997.txt
note: Item is Ratnākaraśānti's Muktāvalī COMMENTARY (ca. 1000 CE), not the root Hevajratantra (which Snellgrove dates late 8th c.). Dated to author floruit ca. 1000.

title: Guṇabharaṇī(-nāma-Ṣaḍaṅgayoga-ṭippaṇī) of Raviśrījñāna
basis: Sferra 2000 (ed.); Davidson, Pure Realms and Hidden Abodes; Szántó, Siddhas
note: Raviśrījñāna's Guṇabharaṇī is a Kālacakra-tradition (post-c.1025 CE) commentary; he was used by Bu-ston/'Gos-lo and his Amṛtakaṇikā was further glossed by Vibhūticandra (early 13th c.), placing him in the late 12th-early 13th c. A LATE Buddhist tantric exegete, not 6th c.

title: Śāradātilaka with Rāghavabhaṭṭa's commentary (Padārthādarśa)
basis: André Padoux, Vāc (1990)
source_file: cleaned_Vac. The Concept of the Word in Selected Hindu Tantras_Andre Padoux, 1990.txt
note: Item is the Woodroffe 1933 edition keyed to Rāghavabhaṭṭa's commentary, firmly dated 1484; the root Śāradātilaka is 11th c. Bounds set on the commentary. Major change.

title: Sekanirdeśapañjikā (comm. on Maitrīpa's Sekanirdeśa)
basis: Mathes 2015, A Fine Blend of Mahāmudrā and Madhyamaka (Maitrīpa 986-1063); ed. Isaacson & Sferra 2014
source_file: cleaned_Fine Blend of Mahamudra and Madhyamaka.  Maitripa’s Collection of Texts on Non-conceptual Realization (Amanasikara)_Klaus-Dieter Mathes,  2015.txt
note: Item is Rāmapāla's COMMENTARY (Isaacson-Sferra 2014 ed.) on Maitrīpa's Sekanirdeśa; Rāmapāla was Maitrīpa's pupil, so the pañjikā is late 11th-early 12th c., later than median 867.

title: Hevajra sādhana of Saroruha(vajra)
basis: Davidson 2002, Indian Esoteric Buddhism; Dowman 1986, Masters of Mahamudra
source_file: cleaned_Masters of Mahamudra. Songs and Histories of Eighty-Four Buddhist Siddhas_Abhayadatta, Keith Dowman, 1986.txt
note: Hevajra-system sadhana by the siddha Saroruhavajra/Padmavajra; follows the Hevajratantra (dated by Davidson to late 9th/early 10th c., by Snellgrove late 8th). Saroruha placed late 9th-early 10th c.

title: Ṣaṭsāhasrikā (Hevajrapiṇḍārthaṭīkā) of Vajragarbha
basis: Isaacson & Sferra, 'Tantric Literature' (Brill Enc. Buddhism), via Tomlinson 2019; Snellgrove
source_file: cleaned_Buddhahood and Philosophy of Mind. Ratnakarasanti, Jnanasrimitra, and Debate over Mental Content (Akara)_David Kerman Tomlinson, 2019.txt
note: COMMENTARY (piṇḍārthaṭīkā) on the Hevajratantra, attributed to Vajragarbha (Hevajra's interlocutor), in the Kālacakra-influenced 'bodhisattva commentary' tradition; therefore 11th c., postdating the 9th-10th c. root tantra. Far later than machine median 615.

title: Ṣaṭsāhasrikā (Hevajrapiṇḍārthaṭīkā), ch. 1
basis: Arnold (ed.), As Long as Space Endures (Kalacakra Festschrift)
source_file: cleaned_Arnold_As Long as Space Endures_Essays on the Kālacakra Tantra in Honor of H.H. the Dalai Lama.txt
note: Commentary on Hevajra root tantra by Vajragarbha, linked to Kalacakra bodhisattva-commentary corpus; dated 10th-11th c. (NOT the Hevajratantra itself, which is late 8th c.). Machine date 645 ~350y too early.

title: Ṣaṭsāhasrikā Hevajraṭīkā (Hevajrapiṇḍārthaṭīkā)
basis: Hatchell, Naked Seeing (diss.); Kālacakra circle, Tib. transl. of Kālacakra ca. 1027
note: Commentary on Hevajratantra, an important Kālacakra-circle exegetical work; Kālacakra appeared in India early 11th c. (first Tib. transl. ca. 1027), so Vajragarbha is early 11th c. Distinct from the Hevajratantra itself (late 8th-9th c.).

title: Laghutantraṭīkā (Cakrasaṃvara commentary)
basis: Cicuzza 2001 (ed.); English, Vajrayogini (Kālacakra-circle exegete)
note: Commentary (on Cakrasaṃvara/Laghuśaṃvara) by Vajrapāṇi, an early-11th-c. Kālacakra-influenced exegete; ed. Cicuzza 2001. A commentary, not the root tantra.

title: Rahasyadīpikā (comm. on Kṛṣṇācārya's Vasantatilakā) of Vanaratna
basis: Vanaratna dates 1384-1468 (Doney; Dudjom; McKeown; van der Kuijp)
note: COMMENTARY by Vanaratna (1384-1468, 'last Indian paṇḍita') on Kṛṣṇācārya/Kāṇha's Vasantatilakā; composed mid-15th c. Machine median 620 vastly too early.

title: Amṛtakaṇikoddyotanibandha (sub-comm. on Raviśrījñāna's Amṛtakaṇikā on the Mañjuśrīnāmasaṃgīti)
basis: Arnold (ed.), As Long as Space Endures (Kālacakra essays); Sobisch 2002; Mathes 2015
source_file: cleaned_Arnold_As Long as Space Endures_Essays on the Kālacakra Tantra in Honor of H.H. the Dalai Lama.txt
note: Buddhist Kālacakra-tradition commentary. Vibhūticandra fl. late 12th/early 13th c. (12th/13th-c. per Sobisch); his nibandha glosses Raviśrījñāna's Amṛtakaṇikā (Raviśrījñāna ~mid/late 12th c., a century after Anupamarakṣita). Composition c.1200. Huge shift later from median 884.

title: Niṣpannayogāvali
basis: Kano 2018/2020 (Abhayākaragupta's dated works fall 1108-1120, Vikramaśīla); Davidson, attribution
source_file: cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism_ A Social History of the Tantric Movement.txt
note: Securely attributed late work of Abhayākaragupta, composed at Vikramaśīla in the same period as his three colophon-dated works (1108-1120); no own colophon date, so c.1100-1125.

title: Caryāsaṅgrahapradīpa
basis: Davidson 2005, Tibetan Renaissance (Atiśa's dates) + Potter bibliography (work listed among Atiśa's)
source_file: cleaned_Tibetan Renaissance. Tantric Buddhism in the Rebirth of Tibetan Culture_Ronald M. Davidson, 2005.txt
note: Listed (#10) among Atiśa Dīpaṃkaraśrījñāna's works in Potter's bibliography; Atiśa firmly 982-1054, arrived Tibet 1042. Composed first half 11th c. Median fits.

title: Cittaviśuddhiprakaraṇa
basis: Wedemeyer 2007 (Āryadeva's Lamp...) and Wedemeyer diss.
source_file: cleaned_Wedemeyer_Aryadeva's Lamp That Integrates the Practices.txt
note: Wedemeyer establishes the four Guhyasamaja-tradition works of the tantric Aryadeva (incl. the Cittavisuddhiprakarana) as by the same author as the Caryamelapakapradipa, datable mid-to-late 9th c. (terminus ante quem early 11th c.). NOT the 2nd-3rd c. Madhyamaka Aryadeva. Changed by >100y vs median 224.

title: Gurupañcāśikā
basis: Nakamura 1980, Indian Buddhism (no firm specialist date; attribution flagged spurious)
source_file: cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Vajrayana guru-devotion text; attribution to 1st-2nd c. Asvaghosa is pseudepigraphic per Nakamura (grouped with works 'maybe spuriously' ascribed). Tantric guru-cult content implies a much later (c. 8th-11th c.) date, but the library gives no precise specialist dating; left null. Current median 117 (=historical Asvaghosa) is certainly far too early for the text itself.

title: Caryāmelāpakapradīpa (Caryāmelāvaṇapradīpa)
basis: Wedemeyer 2007, Āryadeva's Lamp That Integrates the Practices
source_file: cleaned_Wedemeyer_Aryadeva's Lamp That Integrates the Practices.txt
note: Wedemeyer's specialist study: terminus post quem from cited works (Padmavajra placed 2nd quarter 9th c. per Davidson; later than Hevajra-tantra sources), terminus ante quem early 11th c. So mid-to-late 9th c. Tantric Aryadeva, not the early Madhyamaka one. Changed by >100y vs median 225.

title: Guhyasamājamaṇḍalavidhi
basis: Davidson 2002, Indian Esoteric Buddhism; Wedemeyer diss.
note: The 450-verse Guhyasamājamaṇḍalavidhi; Dīpaṃkarabhadra is the direct disciple of Buddhajñānapāda, whose congregations Davidson dates to the early 9th c. (studied under Haribhadra, late 8th c.). Confirms current median.

title: Garbhasaṃgraha
basis: Apple 2019, Atisa Dipamkara (Atiśa b. 982, d. 1054); Davidson 2005, Tibetan Renaissance (attribution issue)
source_file: cleaned_Atisa Dipamkara  Illuminator of the Awakened Mind_James B  Apple, 2019.txt
note: Work attributed to Atiśa (982-1054), so dated to his floruit; Davidson lists a *Garbhasaṃgraha among six works of Kāṇha/Kṛṣṇa, so attribution is contested. Dated to Atiśa per workitem source.

title: Khasarpaṇasādhana
basis: Lopez (ed.) 1997, Religions of Tibet/Buddhism in Practice (Sādhanamālā Khasarpaṇa sādhana attributed to Anupamarakṣita)
note: Khasarpaṇa (form of Avalokiteśvara) sādhana of the Sādhanamālā (late 11th-12th c. compilation), this one by Anupamarakṣita c.1165. Far later than current 716 -> CHANGED.

title: Mahāpratisarāvidyāvidhi
basis: Nakamura, Indian Buddhism: A Survey (1980); cf. Hidas on Mahāpratisarā
source_file: cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Mahāpratisarā dhāraṇī (one of the Pañcarakṣā) has a firm TAQ: Chinese translation 693 CE. The vidhi/ritual frame may be somewhat later, but the core text is 6th-7th c. Current median 629 lies within range.

title: Mṛtyuvañcanasitatārāsādhana
basis: Tiso/PIATS 1992 (on Vāgīśvarakīrti's Saptāṅga and Mṛtyuvañcana cycle)
note: White-Tārā 'cheating-death' sādhana in Vāgīśvarakīrti's circle; he was a disciple of Nāropa and guru of Marpa, i.e. early-mid 11th c. Median fits.

title: Mṛtyuvañcanopadeśatārāsādhana
basis: Bajracharya 2018 (Monasticism in Buddhism of Nepal-Mandala); White ed., Tantra in Practice 2000
source_file: cleaned_Monasticism in Buddhism of Nepal-Mandala. Continuity and changes_SM Bajracharya, 2018.txt
note: Author Vāgīśvarakīrti, predecessor of Atiśa at Nālandā/Vikramaśīla, fl. c.1004; work composed early 11th c. Current median (824) ~180y too early.

title: Mahāśītavatī vidyārājñī
basis: Holz, The Bhadrakarātrī-sūtra (citing Hidas 2017 critical ed.; Skilling 1992)
source_file: cleaned_Holz_The Bhadrakarātrī-sūtra_Apotropaic Scriptures in Early Indian Buddhism.txt
note: One of the five Pañcarakṣā Śrāvakayāna rakṣā/dhāraṇī texts; 'composite compilations that must have evolved over several centuries.' Companion Mahāpratisarā t.a.q. late 6th c.; Tibetan transl. c.800 CE, oldest Indian mss c.1100. Range brackets the core compositional stratum. Consistent with current 300-700 bounds.

title: Guhyavajravilāsinīsādhana
basis: Elizabeth English 2002, Vajrayogini: Her Visualization, Rituals, and Forms
note: GSS10 in the Guhyasamayasādhanamālā, a Vajrayoginī sādhana; attributed to the siddha Śabara, tied to 11th c. (teacher of Advayavajra, reign of Neyapāla 1007-85). GSS texts range 9th-12th c. Much later than current median 754.

title: Guhyāvalī
basis: no dating found in library
note: No scholarship on a text titled Guhyāvalī located in the library. No consensus date.

title: Kāṇhapādasya dohākoṣa
basis: Davidson 2005, Tibetan Renaissance (Kāṇha among the mahāsiddhas, contributor to the Caryāgīti corpus); siddha period generally placed late 10th–early 11th c.
source_file: cleaned_Tibetan Renaissance. Tantric Buddhism in the Rebirth of Tibetan Culture_Ronald M. Davidson, 2005.txt
note: No verbatim dating of the Dohākoṣa found; placed in the mature mahāsiddha/Caryāgīti period (10th–11th c.), later than the machine date. Library notes several siddhas named Kāṇha/Kṛṣṇa, so identity is unsettled (Davidson).

title: Mahāmāyāsādhanopāyikā
basis: terminus ante quem from Ratnākaraśānti's Guṇavatī commentary (early 11th c.); Buddhist yoginītantra genre
source_file: cleaned_Crazy for Wisdom. The Making of a Mad Yogin in Fifteenth-Century Tibet_Stefan Larsson, 2012.txt
note: Sādhana(s) on the Buddhist Mahāmāyātantra (a yoginītantra). Library gives no explicit composition century; only firm terminus is Ratnākaraśānti (Vikramaśīla, c.1000) who wrote the Guṇavatī commentary, implying tantra predates ~1000. Genre conventionally late 9th-10th c. Low confidence.

title: Mahāmāyātantra
basis: Davidson 2002, Indian Esoteric Buddhism (class as yoginī/mother tantra of the Kukuripa cycle)
source_file: cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism_ A Social History of the Tantric Movement.txt
note: Buddhist yoginītantra (mother tantra), associated with the mahāsiddha Kukuripa. Library has no explicit century date; classed among the developed yoginītantras (coeval with Hevajra), giving a 9th-10th c. window. Current median ~900 retained.

title: Marmakālikā
basis: Dasgupta 1950, Introduction to Tantric Buddhism (relative dating: cites Pancakrama)
source_file: cleaned_Introduction to Tantric Buddhism_Shastri Bhusan Dasgupta, 1950.txt
note: Buddhist tantra; library gives no explicit date. It quotes the Pancakrama/Dohakosa commentaries, so it postdates the late Yoginitantra/siddha literature (>=11th c.); range estimate only, no firm scholarly date found.

title: Sarahapādasya dohākoṣa
basis: Davidson 2002, Indian Esoteric Buddhism
source_file: cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism_ A Social History of the Tantric Movement.txt
note: Saraha is the founding Buddhist mahāsiddha; dohās date to the 8th-9th c. florescence of siddha composition. Nakamura's '10th-12th c.' dating is now superseded. Median unchanged.

title: Śrīvajravārāhīsādhana
basis: English 2002, Vajrayoginī: Her Visualization, Rituals, and Forms
note: Elizabeth English edits this very Vajravārāhī sādhana (GSS11) and dates Umāpatideva to the early-to-mid 12th c. (contemporary of Śākyarakṣita; received Kālacakra from Abhayākara lineage). Midpoint 1125 vs current 765 -> changed.

title: Caryāgītikoṣa
basis: Schaeffer (Saraha diss.); Harper & Brown 2002, Roots of Tantra
note: Multi-author Buddhist tantric song anthology; Harper-Brown put compilation 'about the eleventh century', Lui-pa late 9th/early 10th, Saraha 9th c. Wide range reflects diverse authorship.

title: Śrīguhyasamājamaṇḍalavidhi
basis: Sanderson 2009, The Śaiva Age (places Dīpaṃkarabhadra in reign of Dharmapāla); Wedemeyer diss.
note: This is the Guhyasamāja Maṇḍalavidhi (Maṇḍalopāyikā '450') of Dīpaṃkarabhadra, disciple of Buddhajñānapāda, placed in Dharmapāla's reign c. 775–812. An authored maṇḍala-rite manual of the Jñānapāda school, not the root Guhyasamājatantra. Slight forward shift from 697 to ~800.

title: Tattvasiddhi
basis: Almogi (Rong zom pa's Discourses on Buddhology) reporting Steinkellner 1999/2001's edition of the Tantric Tattvasiddhi attributed to Śāntarakṣita
source_file: cleaned_Almogi_Rong zom pa's Discourses on Buddhology.txt
note: Buddhist tantric apologetic work; if by Śāntarakṣita then 8th c. (consistent with cur_na 800). Attribution debated (also linked to Śāntideva). NOT the Abhidharma *Tattvasiddhiśāstra of Harivarman. Midpoint ~756 vs 762: change <100y.

title: Vāṅmaṇḍalanamaskāraślokāḥ
basis: no specialist dating found in library
note: Only a bare occurrence of the compound (sarvatathāgata-vāṅmaṇḍalam) found in a sutra text, not a dated discussion of this short hymn. No date assigned.

title: Bauddha pāribhāṣikāḥ śabdāḥ
basis: modern lexical compilation, not a historical text
note: Modern glossary/compilation of Buddhist technical terms (paribhasika sabda), not a datable premodern composition. No historical date applies.

title: Samantabhadra nāma sādhana ṭīkā
basis: Wedemeyer diss. (Samantabhadrasādhana = one of Buddhajñānapāda's 14 Guhyasamāja works)
note: Commentary (ṭīkā) on Buddhajñānapāda's Samantabhadrasādhana (Jñānapāda Guhyasamāja school). The root text is late 8th–early 9th c., so this ṭīkā is post-quem early 9th c.; commentator unidentified, hence broad 9th–11th c. range. Current median 851 falls within range.

title: Samājasādhanavyavastholi
basis: Wedemeyer diss.; Pramāṇakīrtiḥ (van der Kuijp) Steinkellner Festschrift
note: Guhyasamāja-sādhana of the Ārya (Noble) exegetical school, by the tantric Nāgabodhi/Nāgabuddhi; the Ārya Nāgārjuna/Nāgabodhi 'variously dated to the ninth and tenth centuries'. Ārya school postdates the Guhyasamāja Tantra (mid-8th c.). Roughly consistent with median 756.

title: Mantroddhāra
basis: no specific dating found; mantroddhāra is a generic ritual-chapter type, not a single datable work
note: Mantroddhāra ('extraction of mantras') is a chapter-genre found across many tantras, not an individually datable scripture; library has no consensus date. Cannot ground.

title: Balimālikā
basis: Bendall, Catalogue of Buddhist Skt MSS (Cambridge) — descriptive only, no composition date
source_file: cleaned_Bendall_Catalogue of the Buddhist Sanskrit manuscripts in the University Library, Cambridge.txt
note: Short Buddhist (Vajrasattva/Hevajra-milieu) bali offering manual in a Nepalese MS; appears alongside Kuladatta's Kriyāsaṃgraha (Kuladatta fl. c.1045-12th/13th c.) but Balimālikā itself is undated. No firm year bounds; null.

title: Ādikarmapradīpa [Ādikarmapradīpa Bodhicaryāvatāraṭīkā]
basis: Tuladhar-Douglas, Remaking Buddhism in Medieval Nepal; Szántó (citing Takahashi 1993 ed.)
note: Newar Vajrayāna ritual digest by Anupamavajra (Takahashi 1993 ed.); grouped with late Nepalese ritual compendia. No firm single date in library; broad 11th-12th c. window. Title here conflates it with a Bodhicaryāvatāra commentary, but the Ādikarmapradīpa itself is a kriyā manual.

title: Prajñopāyaviniścayasiddhi
basis: Snellgrove, Indo-Tibetan Buddhism; Davidson, Indian Esoteric Buddhism (2002)
note: Guhyasamāja-based 'Siddhi' corpus; companion Guhyasiddhi (Padmavajra) dated 9th c. by Davidson. cur_med 711 slightly early but within plausible late-8th window; midpoint shifts <100y so not counted as changed.

title: Śrīsahajasiddhi
basis: Davidson 2002, Indian Esoteric Buddhism (siddha chronology)
source_file: cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism_ A Social History of the Tantric Movement.txt
note: Buddhist mahāsiddha author; Ḍombī Heruka belongs to the 9th-c. siddha generation (post-Saraha, Hevajra/Saṃvara milieu). No direct dating of the Sahajasiddhi text in library. Median essentially unchanged.

title: Tattvaratnāvalī
basis: Sferra 2008, Sanskrit Texts from Tucci's Collection I (Advayavajra/Amanasikāra lineages ending 11th c.)
source_file: cleaned_Sanskrit Texts from Giuseppe Tucci’s Collection. Part I_Francesco Sferra, ed, 2008.txt
note: Doxographical work of Advayavajra/Maitrīpa, the 11th-c. mahāsiddha (c.1007-1085); library confirms his 11th-c. lineage but gives no verbatim text date. Median fits.

title: Adhyātmasāraśataka [Adhyātmasāraśatakasaṭīka]
basis: only a bibliographic catalogue mention (Potter, EIP vol.1); no tantric dating
source_file: cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 1 - Potter.txt
note: Likely a non-tantric spiritual/Vedānta-Jain 'spiritual-essence century' verse text with commentary (saṭīka), not a tantra. No date available in library.

title: Abhayapaddhati
consensus_date: ca. 1108 CE (25th regnal year of Rāmapāla)
basis: Kano 2018/2020, A Survey of Passages ... in the Munimatālaṃkāra (colophon dates)
source_file: cleaned_Kazuo Kano_A Survey of Passages from Rare Buddhist Works Found in the Munimatālaṃkāra.txt
note: Colophon-dated; 30th yr of Rāmapāla = ca. 1113, so 25th yr ≈ 1108. Commentary (Abhayapaddhati) on the Buddhakapālamahātantra.

title: Paribhāṣāvṛtti
basis: src = Wujastyk 1993, Metarules of Pāṇinian Grammar (Vyāḍi's Paribhāṣāvṛtti); no dating discussion located in library
note: NON-TANTRIC: a Pāṇinian grammatical metarule treatise (paribhāṣā collection), not a tantra. Library confirms the Wujastyk/Vyāḍi attribution but gives no firm composition date; cannot date here.

title: Bauddhadharme tathā purāṇeṣu karuṇā
note: Not an ancient text. The title ("Compassion in the Buddhist Dharma and in the Purāṇas") is a modern scholarly study title; not a datable primary Tantric work. No source found.

title: Kriyāsaṁgrahapañjikā
basis: Iain Sinclair (in Yoshizaki, Newar Buddhism); Tanemura 2004, Kuladatta's Kriyāsamgrahapañjikā ed.
note: Pañjikā by the Nepalese Vajrācārya Kuladatta on the Kriyāsaṃgraha; Sinclair dates it 11th c., Tuladhar-Douglas groups it among late Nepalese ritual compendia (12th c.). Standard ed. Tanemura 2004.

title: Tattvajñānasaṁsiddhi-mahāsukhaprakāśikā-ṭīkā
basis: Snellgrove, Indo-Tibetan Buddhism (siddhi-corpus / Anaṅgavajra c. 8th c.); Davidson 2002
note: Commentary (ṭīkā) on a Tattvajñānasiddhi of the Buddhist 'siddhi-text' corpus (Guhya-/Jñāna-/Prajñopāyaviniścaya-/Tattvajñāna-siddhi) tied to the Guhyasamāja-Hevajra milieu of Ḍombiheruka; base texts c. 8th-9th c., so the ṭīkā is 9th-10th c. Indirect evidence; no direct dating of this ṭīkā in library.

title: Āryamāyājālamahātantroddhṛtamaṇḍalagāthāṭippaṇī
basis: Tibetan tradition / encyclopedic; Māyājāla classed as a Buddhist Father (Yoga) Tantra of the Guhyasamāja group
note: This item is a ṭippaṇī (gloss) on maṇḍala-gāthās extracted from the root Māyājāla-mahātantra (~8th c. Buddhist Yoga/Father tantra); the gloss is later, so placed 9th-10th c. Low confidence; no dating of the ṭippaṇī itself found.

title: Kālacakrāvatāra
basis: Newman, Epoch of the Kālacakra; Sanderson 2009 (Abhayākaragupta active under Rāmapāla, dates of composition Śaka ~1047)
note: Work of Abhayākaragupta, abbot of Vikramaśīla under Rāmapāla; his dated colophons fall c. 1100–1125, so the Kālacakrāvatāra belongs to the early 12th c.

title: Munimatālaṁkāra
consensus_date: ca. 1113 CE (30th regnal year of Rāmapāla)
basis: Kano 2018/2020, A Survey of Passages ... in the Munimatālaṃkāra (colophon)
source_file: cleaned_Kazuo Kano_A Survey of Passages from Rare Buddhist Works Found in the Munimatālaṃkāra.txt
note: Colophon-dated firmly to ca. A.D. 1113.

title: Mañjuvajramukhyākhyāna
basis: Davidson 2002, Indian Esoteric Buddhism (on Buddhajñānapāda's Guhyasamāja/Mañjuvajra system); cf. Dalton, Interiorization of Ritual
note: Mañjuvajra is the central deity of Buddhajñānapāda's Guhyasamāja maṇḍala/exegetical system (Mukhāgama corpus); the akhyāna text belongs to that early-9th-c. body. Distinct from the older Guhyasamājatantra root text.

title: Caryāsaṃgraha
basis: Davidson 2005, Tibetan Renaissance, and PIATS-06 (Kvaerne) on the Caryāgīti corpus; the Caryā/performance-song anthology assembled from siddha works of the 10th–12th c.
source_file: cleaned_Tibetan Renaissance. Tantric Buddhism in the Rebirth of Tibetan Culture_Ronald M. Davidson, 2005.txt
note: Taken as a collection of Buddhist caryā/performance songs (Caryāgīti-type) by the mahāsiddhas; the corpus is dated 10th–12th c. by Kvaerne/Davidson, considerably later than the machine date. Title match approximate; low confidence.

title: Jñānasiddhi
basis: Davidson 2002, Indian Esoteric Buddhism
source_file: cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism_ A Social History of the Tantric Movement.txt
note: Buddhist tantric siddha-author treatise. Indrabhūti associated with late 8th-early 9th c. siddha milieu; library has no direct dating of the Jñānasiddhi itself. Median essentially unchanged.

title: Laghutantraṭīkā
basis: Cicuzza 2001, The Laghutantraṭīkā by Vajrapāṇi: Critical Edition (intro on the Kālacakra cycle)
source_file: cleaned_Laghutantratikaby Vajrapani. Critical Edition of the Sanskrit Text_Claudio Cicuzza, ed., 2001.txt
note: Bodhisattva Vajrapāṇi's commentary on the opening verses of the Cakrasaṃvara-/Laghuśaṃvara cycle; Kālacakra milieu dated X-XI c., compilation end of X c. Median fits.

title: Boudhatantrokta Siddhauṣadhi Saṅgraha
basis: no specialist dating found; 'siddhauṣadhi' appears only as a generic term in Sanderson 2009 and others, never as this title
note: Obscure minor compilation of tantric medicinal herbs (siddha-auṣadhi); no edition or specialist date in library. The matched phrase is merely the generic term for magical herbs, not this work. No consensus date assigned.

title: Laghukaumudīvyākaraṇa
basis: Bendall, Catalogue of the Sanskrit Manuscripts in the British Museum
source_file: cleaned_Bendall_Catalogue of the Sanskrit manuscripts in the British museum.txt
note: Grammatical primer (abridgement of Bhaṭṭoji Dīkṣita's Siddhāntakaumudī), NOT a tantric text. Varadarāja was Bhaṭṭoji's direct pupil, so early-mid 17th c. Current median (1620) well grounded.

title: Cakrasaṃvaratantra Vol. 2
basis: Gray, Cakrasamvara article (forthcoming translation/study); cf. Davidson, Indian Esoteric Buddhism
note: Gray dates root CST (Laghusaṃvara/Herukābhidhāna) to late 8th c.; Davidson likewise treats it as an 8th-c. text. Consistent with current median (830).

title: Ḍākinījālasaṃvararahasya
basis: Gray (Cakrasaṃvara history and praxis); Sarvabuddhasamāyogaḍākinījālasaṃvara is the earliest yoginītantra
note: A 'rahasya' (secret/explanatory) text of the Ḍākinījālasaṃvara cycle (Sarvabuddhasamāyogaḍākinījālasaṃvara, earliest yoginītantra, late 7th-early 8th c. per Gray/Amoghavajra; also the Hevajra is called Ḍākinījālasaṃvara). Specific rahasya undated, so low; placed in 8th c. period.

title: Sekoddeśapañjikā
basis: PIATS 1992 / Sferra (anonymous commentary on the Sekoddeśa of the Kālacakra cycle)
note: Anonymous commentary on the Sekoddeśa, part of the Kālacakra corpus (entered India c. 1025); commentaries cluster in the 11th–12th c. Distinct from Nāropā's/Naḍapāda's Sekoddeśaṭīkā.

title: Pañcakrama
basis: Wedemeyer, Āryadeva's Lamp That Integrates the Practices (2007)
source_file: cleaned_Wedemeyer_Aryadeva's Lamp That Integrates the Practices.txt
note: Core work of Ārya/Noble Guhyasamāja tradition Nāgārjuna; cited by Āryadeva's CMP (mid-to-late 9th c.), so Pañcakrama is earlier, early-mid 9th c. Within ~50y of cur_med 889.

title: Piṇḍīkrama
basis: Wedemeyer, Āryadeva's Lamp That Integrates the Practices (2007); de Jong, A New History of Tantric Literature
source_file: cleaned_Wedemeyer_Aryadeva's Lamp That Integrates the Practices.txt
note: Piṇḍīkṛtasādhana (=Piṇḍīkrama) by the same Ārya Nāgārjuna; cited alongside Pañcakrama, in the foundational layer of the Noble Guhyasamāja tradition (early-mid 9th c.). Within ~50y of cur_med.

title: Sekoddeśa [Sekoddeśaṭīkā]
basis: Nāropa dated 956-1040 (Apple 2019; Dimitrov diss.); Sekoddeśaṭīkā is his Kālacakra-tradition commentary
note: Commentary on the Sekoddeśa (Kālacakra cycle, c.1025). Nāropa fl. early 11th c.; cur_med 1024 confirmed. Tibetan sources give 1016-1100 variant.

title: Nityakarmapūjāvidhi
basis: Cited as a ritual source by Szántó (Catuṣpīṭha) and Holz; no composition date given
note: Newar Buddhist daily-worship ritual manual / compilation; used by editors as a witness to ritual practice but not itself dated as an early work. Likely a late Nepalese compilation; current median (652) unsupported. Left undated.

title: Pratisarākalpadhāraṇī
consensus_date: late 6th c. CE (terminus ante quem; earlier forms 5th c.)
basis: Hidas (cited in Holz), on Mahāpratisarā
source_file: cleaned_Holz_The Bhadrakarātrī-sūtra_Apotropaic Scriptures in Early Indian Buddhism.txt
note: = Mahāpratisarā(-mahāvidyārājñī), first of the Pañcarakṣā. Hidas: TAQ late 6th c., earlier (5th c.) forms possible; in Gilgit MSS by early 7th c. Current median 605 slightly late.

title: Bhramaharanāma Hevajrasādhana
basis: Nakamura 1980 (Ratnākaraśānti c. 1040 CE); cf. Isaacson on Ratnākaraśānti's tantric works
source_file: cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Dated by its author Ratnākaraśānti (Śāntipā, fl. c. 1000-1040, Vikramaśīla). A Hevajra sādhana, not the root tantra. Current median ~3 centuries too early.

title: Tattvajñānasaṃsiddhi
basis: Warder 2000 (Indian Buddhism), dating of the siddha Ḍombīheruka
note: Dated only indirectly via its purported author Ḍombīheruka, whom Warder places in the generation knowing the early Hevajra material (late 8th–early 9th c.). Attribution debated and no direct dating of the work itself; hence low confidence.

title: Vasudhārādhāraṇī 1 [Vasudhārādhāraṇīsūtra]
basis: Hamar, Faith in Buddhism (Āryavasudhārādhāraṇīsūtra ed. 2007); Lessing & Wayman, Introduction to the Buddhist Tantric Systems 1968
note: Early Mahayana dharani-sutra; library quotes the text but gives no explicit date. A Chinese version (Vasudharadharanisutra, T) was made early, implying a pre-7th c. terminus; range is broad and tentative. Midpoint ~550 within ~100y of cur_med, not counted as changed.

title: Vasudhārādhāraṇī 2 [Sucandragṛhapatiparipṛcchā/Sucandrāvadāna]
basis: Shaw, Buddhist Goddesses of India (2006) on Vasudhārā and her dhāraṇī (= Inquiry of Layman Sucandra); iconographic attestation
note: Buddhist wealth-goddess dhāraṇī. No firm composition date in library; Shaw dates a Vasudhārā image to ~8th c. and the goddess's emergence with Tārā. Dhāraṇī likely existed by 7th-8th c. (earlier Chinese versions exist); broad bracket, current median ~652 plausible.

title: Vasudhārādhāraṇī(sūtra)
basis: library notes Chinese translation as Vasudharadharanisutra (T.) giving terminus ante quem; dharani genre
source_file: cleaned_Abhandlung in 30 Versen über das reine Bewusstsein (Hsuan-tsang, Vasubandhu)(Trimsika)_Swati Ganguly.txt
note: Buddhist dharani (Vasudhara/wealth-goddess); attested via Chinese translation, so pre-existing by mid-1st millennium; among earlier dharani literature. No precise specialist date in library; broad low-confidence range.

title: Mṛtyuvañcanopadeśa
basis: Bajracharya 2018 (Monasticism in Nepal-Mandala) c.1004 AD; Makransky 1997 (contemporary of Jnanasrimitra, Ratnakirti); White (Tantra in Practice) predecessor of Atisa at Nalanda/Vikramasila
source_file: cleaned_Monasticism in Buddhism of Nepal-Mandala. Continuity and changes_SM Bajracharya, 2018.txt
note: Cheating-death (mrtyuvancana) text by Vagisvarakirti, abbot at Vikramasila, predecessor of Atisa, fl. early 11th c. Machine date 648 ~360y too early.

title: Tarkabhāṣāṭīkā
basis: Kajiyama 1966, An Introduction to Buddhist Philosophy (Tarkabhasa of Moksakaragupta)
source_file: cleaned_Introduction to Buddhist Philosophy. An Annotated Translation of the Tarkabhasa of Moksakaragupta_Kajiyama, Yuichi, 1966.txt
note: Buddhist Pramana handbook, not a tantra. Kajiyama brackets Moksakaragupta between Ratnakirti (~c.1000) and the 1202 sack of Jagaddala; standard dating c.1050-1202. Dated here is the Tarkabhasa itself; a later anonymous tika is even later but unidentified.

title: Śvānastavakāvya
basis: authorship: Davuldena Jñāneśvara Mahāsthavira, a 20th-c. Sri Lankan Sanskrit/Pali scholar-monk
note: Not a classical tantra: a modern (20th-c.) Sanskrit kāvya by the named Sri Lankan author. Machine median (863) is an artefact; date set to modern period. No library source dates the poem specifically.

title: Svastyayanagāthā
basis: no dating source located in library
note: 'Svastyayana' occurs in the library only as a common noun (benediction/auspicious blessing, e.g. in Manjusrimulakalpa and Lokottaravada Vinaya material), not as a separately datable titled work. No specialist dating found; left null.

title: Vajrayāna darśana mīmāṁsā
basis: none found
note: Title ('inquiry into the Vajrayāna view') reads as a modern/scholastic doctrinal treatise rather than a classical tantra; no dating scholarship found in the library. No date assigned.

title: Tarkarahasya
basis: Lo Bue 2011 and Tatz 1986 (Jetāri circa 10th c.); Jitāri was teacher of Atiśa (b. 982); ed. Bühnemann, Tarkarahasya and Vādarahasya
source_file: cleaned_Art in Tibet. Issues in Traditional Tibetan Art from the Seventh to the Twentieth Century_Erberto F. Lo Bue, ed, 2011.txt
note: NOT a tantra: a Buddhist Pramāṇa/logic doxographic text by Jitāri, Atiśa's teacher, active late 10th c.; later than median 867 though within ~100y.
